# Added two historical ICE detention facility lists (2015 XLSX, 2017 PDF) to the ice-detention targets pipeline. The 2015 source was renamed from `fy15_dmcp` to `facilities_data_dec2015` (a Dec 8, 2015 snapshot covering FY2010–FY2016). Diagnosed and fixed two data quality issues in `clean_dmcp_data()`: `facility_zip` zero-padded to 5 characters, `facility_county` title-cased. Fixed a critical PDF column boundary bug in `.dmcp_pdf_col_breaks`: the original midpoint-based boundaries caused 172 ADP coercion failures because right-aligned small values (especially "0") appear 3–4px further right than large values in the same column; the fix was to use actual header x-positions as left-edge boundaries, which eliminated all ADP failures. Implemented the Frictionless Data metadata convention for the first time: `data/dmcp-listings.yml` and `data/datapackage.json`. Updated AGENTS.md with a two-section metadata convention for main-pipeline vs. supplemental sources.

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01FFX8iQCmazKhdxxjsDfQT4"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"text","value":"[File: 5.0KB, 118 lines, showing lines 1-118]\n     1→# Plan: Ingest Wikipedia References →...[truncated, total 6021 chars]"},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"[File: 5.0KB, 118 lines, showing lines 1-118]\n# Plan: Ingest Wikipedia References → RIS → Zotero\n\n## Goal\n\nBuild an R script that takes a Wikipedia article title, extracts all cited references, deduplicates them into a tidy data frame, exports to RIS format, and imports into a new collection in a local Zotero library via `c2z`.\n\n## Key challenges\n\nWikipedia articles use two distinct citation patterns (and often mix them):\n\n1. **Inline `<ref>` tags** containing `{{Cite book|...}}`, `{{Cite journal|...}}`, `{{Cite web|...}}`, etc. (e.g., Meiō incident)\n2. **Author-date / `{{sfn}}` style** where short footnotes (`{{sfn|Author|Year|p=...}}`) point to full entries in a Sources / Works Cited section using the same `{{cite *}}` templates (e.g., Elizabeth Lyon)\n\nBoth patterns ultimately resolve to `{{cite *}}` templates with well-defined parameter names. The strategy is to extract **all** `{{cite *}}` templates from the wikitext regardless of where they appear, then deduplicate.\n\n## Pipeline steps\n\n### 1. Fetch wikitext via WikipediR\n\n```r\nlibrary(WikipediR)\npage <- page_content(\"en\", \"wikipedia\", page_name = \"...\", as_wikitext = TRUE)\n```\n\nThis returns the raw wikitext, which we parse in R.\n\n### 2. Parse all `{{cite *}}` templates from wikitext\n\nWrite a recursive-descent or regex-based parser that handles nested braces (Wikipedia templates can contain nested templates like `{{Google books|...|...}}`).\n\nExtract every `{{Cite book|...}}`, `{{Cite journal|...}}`, `{{Cite web|...}}`, `{{Cite ODNB|...}}`, `{{Cite encyclopedia|...}}`, `{{harvc|...}}` template. Parse each into a named list of key-value pairs.\n\nAlso handle:\n- `{{sfnRef|...}}` custom ref anchors (used for non-standard short-citation keys)\n- `{{harvc|...}}` (book chapters citing a parent book)\n- Bare `<ref>` tags that contain free text instead of templates (flag these for manual review)\n\n### 3. Map template types to Zotero/RIS item types\n\n| Wikipedia template | RIS type | Zotero itemType |\n|---|---|---|\n| `Cite book` / `harvc` | `BOOK` / `CHAP` | `book` / `bookSection` |\n| `Cite journal` | `JOUR` | `journalArticle` |\n| `Cite web` | `ELEC` | `webpage` |\n| `Cite news` | `NEWS` | `newspaperArticle` |\n| `Cite ODNB` | `ELEC` | `encyclopediaArticle` |\n| `Cite encyclopedia` | `ENCYC` | `encyclopediaArticle` |\n\nMap Wikipedia template parameters to RIS fields:\n\n| Wiki param | RIS tag | Notes |\n|---|---|---|\n| `last` / `last1`, `first` / `first1` | `AU` | Repeat for `last2`/`first2`, etc. |\n| `title` | `TI` | |\n| `date` / `year` | `PY` | |\n| `publisher` | `PB` | |\n| `location` | `CY` | |\n| `isbn` | `SN` | |\n| `doi` | `DO` | |\n| `url` | `UR` | |\n| `journal` | `JO` / `T2` | |\n| `volume` | `VL` | |\n| `issue` | `IS` | |\n| `pages` / `page` | `SP`/`EP` | Parse range |\n| `series` | `T3` | |\n| `edition` | `ET` | |\n| `language` | `LA` | |\n| `access-date` | `Y2` | |\n\n### 4. Build a reference data frame and deduplicate\n\nCreate a tibble with one row per unique source. Deduplication key: normalize `(last1, title, year)` — lowercase, strip punctuation/diacritics. For `{{sfn}}` references, the author-year key naturally groups multiple page citations to the same source.\n\nFlag any remaining near-duplicates using string distance on titles.\n\n### 5. Export to RIS\n\nWrite a function `refs_to_ris(df, file)` that produces a valid `.ris` file. Each record is delimited by `ER  -`.\n\n### 6. Create Zotero collection and import\n\nUsing the `c2z` package (already in use in the project):\n\n```r\nlibrary(c2z)\nzotero <- Zotero(user = TRUE, id = user_id, api = api_key)\n```\n\n- Create a new collection named after the Wikipedia article\n- Post items via `ZoteroPost()` (c2z can accept a data frame of items)\n- Before posting, query the target library for existing items matching the dedup key to avoid duplicates against items already in Zotero\n\n### 7. Reporting\n\nReturn the reference data frame to the user with columns:\n- `ref_key` (dedup key)\n- `item_type`, `authors`, `title`, `year`, `publisher`, `doi`, `url`\n- `n_citations` (how many times cited in the article)\n- `zotero_status` (\"imported\" / \"already_exists\" / \"failed\")\n\n## File structure\n\nSingle script: `wiki-refs-to-zotero.R` (or `.qmd` if we want rendered output). Helper functions at top, pipeline at bottom.\n\n## Dependencies\n\n- `WikipediR` — fetch wikitext\n- `c2z` — Zotero API\n- `stringr`, `purrr`, `dplyr`, `tidyr` — wrangling\n- `stringi` — Unicode normalization for dedup\n\n## Open questions\n\n1. **Zotero target**: Import to personal library or a group library? (The existing code uses both.) I'll default to personal library.\n2. **Non-template refs**: Some articles have bare `<ref>` tags with free text (e.g., `<ref>Temple records of Sumiyoshi-taisha.</ref>`). Plan: include these in the df as `itemType = \"document\"` with just a `title` field, flagged for manual review.\n3. **Primary source templates**: Templates like `{{Cite book |author=Jinson |title=Daijō'in Jisha Zōjiki ...}}` in the Works Cited section that are referenced by `{{sfn|Jinson|...}}` — these are already `{{Cite book}}` and will be parsed normally.\n4. **`harvc` (book chapters)**: These reference a parent book. Plan: create both a `bookSection` item and link it to the parent book entry.\n"},"elapsedMs":23}</script></tool-result></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01BSi4iaj9Y9qgUD8EXJ6k67"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"text","value":"[File: 22.2KB, 342 lines, showing lines 1-342]\n     1→# wiki-graph project memory\n     2→\n...[truncated, total 24915 chars]"},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"[File: 22.2KB, 342 lines, showing lines 1-342]\n# wiki-graph project memory\n\n## File system conventions\n\n- **All relevant project files live inside `~/Dropbox (Personal)/R/`**. Never search outside this path without explicit user permission.\n- Census data (Bolivia 2024 CPV) lives in `../bolivia-data/` relative to the wiki-graph project root, i.e. `~/Dropbox (Personal)/R/bolivia-data/`.\n\n## Project overview\n\nBolivia community geography project. The goal is to map INE community codes to\nIGM geographic point identifiers, enabling downstream linkage to locations\n(coordinates) and Wikidata entities.\n\nA second strand of analysis uses the **Bolivia 2024 Census** (CPV 2024) person-level\nmicrodata to study indigenous identity and language. See `bolivia-censo-2024.qmd`.\n\n## Key data files\n\n### Community geography (INE–IGM crosswalk)\n\n| File | Description |\n|------|-------------|\n| `data/CLASIF_UB_GEOG_COMUNIDAD.xlsx` | INE community list with 19,418 communities. Columns include `Codigo` (11-digit INE code), `CIUDAD/COMUNIDAD`, `DEPARTAMENTO`, `PROVINCIA`, `MUNICIPIO`, and numeric segment columns `DEP`, `PRO`, `MUN`. |\n| `data/localizacion_poblaciones_2016.json` | IGM GeoJSON point dataset: 23,891 settlement points. Key columns: `id_unico` (unique IGM identifier), `nombre_dep` (department), `nombre_c_1` (community name, uppercase), `tipo_area`, `tipo_pobla`. |\n| `data/igm_localizacion_2016/poblaciones.shp` | Same IGM data as shapefile (POINT geometry, WGS84). |\n| `data/etnicidad_tenencia/usca_final.shp` | USCA community-level multipolygon boundaries (MULTIPOLYGON, WGS84). 14,426 features. Key column: `cod10dig` (10-digit INE code — pad with leading zero to match `Codigo`). Also has `eth_tie_fi` and `usc_agreg` (ethnicity/land tenure classifications). |\n| `data/etnicidad_tenencia/usca_final.dbf` | Attribute table of the above (no geometry). |\n| `data/igm_localizacion_2016/poblaciones.dbf` | Attribute table of the IGM shapefile. No INE codes — department and settlement name only. |\n| `data/gadm41_BOL_3.gpkg` | GADM Bolivia level-3 (municipality) boundaries. Layer: `ADM_ADM_3`. Columns: `NAME_1` (department), `NAME_2` (province), `NAME_3` (municipality). |\n| `data/crosswalk_ine_igm.rds` | **Primary output.** Crosswalk from INE `Codigo` to IGM `id_unico`. Load with `readRDS(\"data/crosswalk_ine_igm.rds\")`. |\n| `data/crosswalk_ine_igm.csv` | Same crosswalk as CSV for interoperability. |\n\n### Bolivia 2024 Census (CPV 2024)\n\nAll files live under `../bolivia-data/Censo 2024/` (relative to the wiki-graph project root).\n\n| File | Description |\n|------|-------------|\n| `base_datos_csv_2024/Persona_CPV-2024.csv` | Person-level microdata. ~3 GB, semicolon-delimited. **11,365,333 rows × 118 columns.** All columns are integers. Open lazily: `open_dataset(..., format=\"csv\", delimiter=\";\")`. |\n| `base_datos_csv_2024/Vivienda_CPV-2024.csv` | Household-level microdata. |\n| `base_datos_csv_2024/Emigracion_CPV-2024.csv` | Emigration module. |\n| `base_datos_csv_2024/Mortalidad_CPV-2024.csv` | Mortality module. |\n| `base_datos_csv_2024/Persona_CPV-2024_sample10k.rds` | Pre-drawn random 10,000-row sample of person file (`set.seed(8143)`). Load with `readRDS()`. |\n| `base_datos_csv_2024/Diccionario de variables CPV 2024.xlsx` | Variable dictionary. Sheets: PERSONA, VIVIENDA, EMIGRA, MORTA. Contains value labels for all coded variables. |\n| `BOL-INE-CPV-2024.xml` | DDI codebook (XML). 184 variables with labels, question text, and descriptions. Parse with `xml2`; uses namespace `http://www.icpsr.umich.edu/DDI`. |\n| `BOL-INE-CPV-2024.json` | Same codebook in JSON format. |\n| `codebook_persona.rds` | Pre-parsed DDI codebook as tibble. Columns: `variable`, `label`, `description`, `question`. Load with `readRDS()`. |\n| `codebook_persona.csv` | Same codebook as CSV. |\n| `pueblo_cats.rds` | Lookup table for `p32_pueblo_cod`: 145 rows, columns `code` (int) and `label` (chr). Load with `readRDS()`. |\n| `pueblo_cats.csv` | Same as CSV. |\n| `lang_use.rds` | Pre-collected aggregate: `idioma_mat × idioma_mayor_uso`, all persons with both non-NA. 435 rows. |\n| `lang_use_age.rds` | Same, further broken down by `age_group` (5 bins: 0–14, 15–29, 30–44, 45–59, 60+). 1,319 rows. |\n| `lang_use_urban.rds` | `idioma_mat × idioma_mayor_uso × urbrur_label` aggregate. `urbrur_label`: \"Urban\" (urbrur=1) or \"Rural\" (urbrur=2), joined from Vivienda file on `idep+iprov+imun+i00`. 658 rows. |\n| `lang_use_identity.rds` | `idioma_mat × idioma_mayor_uso × identifies` aggregate. `identifies`: \"Self-identifies\" (p32_pueblo_per=1) or \"Does not identify\" (p32_pueblo_per=2). 616 rows. |\n| `lang_age_urban.rds` | `idioma_mat × idioma_mayor_uso × urbrur_label × age_group`. 1,969 rows. |\n| `lang_age_identity.rds` | `idioma_mat × idioma_mayor_uso × identifies × age_group`. 1,829 rows. |\n| `albo_vars.rds` | Person-level indicators (11,365,333 rows). Columns: `idep`, `iprov`, `imun`, `urbrur`, `albo_q1`, `albo_q2`, `albo_q3`, `albo_c`, `cel_q2`, `cel`. Load with `readRDS()`. |\n| `albo_vars.csv` | Same as CSV. |\n| `cel_geo.rds` | CEL geographic summary table (1,214 rows). Columns: `department`, `province`, `municipality` (INE codes, NA when aggregated), `urban_rural` (\"Urban\"/\"Rural\"/NA), `n_total`, `cel_0` … `cel_7` (% at each level, incl. 1.5 and 4.5), `cel_2plus`, `cel_4plus`, `cel_5plus` (% with CEL ≥ threshold). Covers: national, dept, prov, mun, and all four × urban/rural. Load with `readRDS()`. |\n| `cel_geo.csv` | Same as CSV. |\n\n#### Key person-level variables\n\n| Variable | Description |\n|---|---|\n| `idep` | Department code (1–9) |\n| `iprov` | Province code |\n| `imun` | Municipality code |\n| `p25_sexo` | Sex: 1 = Mujer, 2 = Hombre |\n| `p26_edad` | Age in years |\n| `p32_pueblo_per` | Indigenous self-identification (yes/no): 1 = Sí, 2 = No, 9 = Sin respuesta |\n| `p32_pueblo_cod` | Raw group code (145 categories incl. 9xx generic terms, 5xx foreign groups). Lookup: `pueblo_cats.rds` |\n| `p32_pueblos` | Derived CTAI-reconciled identity (58 categories, 98 = No se autoidentifica, 99 = Sin respuesta). Category labels in `pueblos_cats` tribble in Qmd. |\n| `idioma_mat` | Mother tongue code (83 categories). Key: 2=Aymara, 6=Castellano, 12=Guaraní, 27=Quechua, 998=No habla. Full labels in `idioma_cats` tribble in Qmd. |\n| `idioma_mayor_uso` | Language of greatest daily use (same coding as `idioma_mat`) |\n| `nivel_edu` | Educational attainment (derived, 19+ year olds) |\n| `condact_19` | Labour force status per 19th ICLS definition |\n\n#### Important coding notes\n\n- `p32_pueblo_cod` uses a different numbering scheme from `p32_pueblos`. Do not join them directly.\n- **`urbrur` is in the Vivienda file, not the Persona file.** Join on `idep + iprov + imun + i00` (dwelling number). All 11.4M persona rows match exactly (0 NAs).\n- **Afroboliviano language (code 94 in `idioma_mat`) is excluded from all linguistic retention analysis.** The census records it as a language category, but it refers to a Spanish variety spoken in the La Paz Yungas, not a structurally distinct language. Only ~450 people report it as a mother tongue (92.7% in La Paz). Afroboliviano as an *ethnic identity* (`p32_pueblos == 1`) is retained — 25,168 self-identifiers, 86% Castellano-speaking.\n- **Kabineña** (code 7 in `idioma_mat`) = **Cavineño** (code 7 in `p32_pueblos`): same people, different spelling conventions.\n- **Zamuco** (code 37 in `idioma_mat`) = **Ayoreo** (code 4 in `p32_pueblos`): Zamuco is the ISO language name; Ayoreo is the ethnonym.\n- NAs in `p32_pueblo_cod` are not missing data — they indicate respondents who answered \"No\" or gave no response to `p32_pueblo_per`.\n\n#### albo_vars variable definitions\n\nThe four boolean variables in `albo_vars.rds` implement Xavier Albó's conceptual framework for indigenous identity and language:\n\n| Variable | Question | Logic |\n|---|---|---|\n| `albo_q1` | Self-identifies with an indigenous or Afrobolivian group? | `p32_pueblo_per == 1` |\n| `albo_q2` | Does their spoken language match their declared identity? | Any of `p331/p332/p333` is in the set of idioma codes expected for `p32_pueblos`. Generic identities (Otras declaraciones = 57, Quechua-Aymara = 55, Más de una = 56) **and** groups with no census language equivalent (Afroboliviano = 1, Chuwi = 13, Paunaca = 37, Toromona = 47, foreign groups = 58) all count as YES if any indigenous language (codes 1–5, 7–37) is spoken. Always FALSE if `albo_q1` is FALSE. |\n| `albo_q3` | Learned an indigenous language as a child? | `p341_idiomat_cod` is not NA, not 6 (Castellano), and not 999 |\n| `albo_c` | Speaks Castellano? | Any of `p331/p332/p333` equals 6 |\n\n`cel_q2` differs from `albo_q2` only for non-identifiers (Q1=FALSE): `albo_q2` is always FALSE for non-identifiers, while `cel_q2` is TRUE if the person speaks any Bolivian indigenous language (codes 1–5 or 7–37 in p331/p332/p333). This allows non-identifiers to populate CEL levels 1, 2, and 3.\n\n`cel` — Condición Étnico-Lingüística (Albó & Romero): ordinal 0–7 with half-steps 1.5 and 4.5 for two combinations not in the original scale. See albo-restudy-2024.qmd for full definition.\n\n`p32_pueblos`-to-idioma mapping highlights: Chiquitano (12) → Bésiro (4); Ayoreo (4) → Zamuco (37); Andean sub-groups (Charka Qhara Qhara, Chichas, Jalq'a, Yampara, Lípez, Raqaypampa, Qullas, Killacas, Jach'a Carangas, Lupaca, Pakajaqi, Sora, Qhapaq Uma Suyu) → Quechua or Aymara as appropriate; Mojeño (29) → Mojeño Ignaciano or Trinitario; Joaquiniano (21) → Maropa (19). Afroboliviano (1), Chuwi (13), Paunaca (37), Toromona (47), foreign groups (58) → use \"any indigenous language\" rule (same as generic groups).\n\n#### Census analysis findings (as of Feb 2026)\n\n- **37.5%** of the population self-identifies as indigenous or afro-bolivian (`p32_pueblo_per == 1`).\n- Among self-identifiers, Quechua (39%) and Aymara (37%) dominate; 12.6% used generic terms (Campesino, Indígena, etc.) reclassified to \"Otras declaraciones\" in `p32_pueblos`.\n- **Language shift is pronounced and age-graded**: 76% of 60+ indigenous self-identifiers speak an indigenous mother tongue vs only 22% of 0–14 year olds.\n- **Self-identification rate declines with youth**: 49% of 60+ identify as indigenous vs 28% of 0–14 year olds.\n- **1.98M Castellano mother-tongue speakers** (23% of 8.5M) self-identify as indigenous — predominantly Aymara (11%) and Quechua (8%).\n- ~57,000 Quechua speakers identify with Andean sub-ethnic groups (Charka Qhara Qhara, Kallawaya, Chichas, Jalq'a, Yampara) rather than the pan-ethnic \"Quechua\" label.\n\n## INE code structure\n\n11-digit string (stored as character, with leading zero):\n```\n0 | DD | PP | MM | C | SSS\n  dept  prov  mun  canton  seq\n```\n- Positions 2–3: department (2 digits)\n- Positions 4–5: province (2 digits)\n- Positions 6–7: municipality (2 digits)\n- Position 8: canton (1 digit)\n- Positions 9–11: community sequence number (3 digits)\n\nThe USCA file uses a 10-digit form (no leading zero). Pad with `paste0(\"0\", cod10dig)` to match `Codigo`.\n\n## Crosswalk schema\n\nColumns: `Codigo`, `department`, `municipality`, `com_name`, `id_unico`, `match_status`, `n_geo`, `canton_split_group`\n\n### match_status values\n\n| Status | Meaning |\n|--------|---------|\n| `unique` | Direct 1-to-1 name match within department (13,998) |\n| `unique_via_spatial` | Ambiguous name resolved by GADM municipality boundary (4,077) |\n| `unique_via_name` | Ambiguous name resolved by proximity to municipality centroid (23) |\n| `unique_via_usca` | Ambiguous name resolved by USCA community polygon containment (142) |\n| `ambiguous_canton_split` | Same community listed under multiple canton codes due to administrative reorganisation; all sibling codes share the same IGM candidate pool (1,518 rows / 344 groups) |\n| `ambiguous_dispersed` | All IGM candidates are `tipo_area == \"dis\"` (dispersed settlement); the IGM dataset recorded multiple points for the same dispersed community. Treat all candidates as a valid coordinate pool rather than selecting one (967) |\n| `ambiguous` | Genuinely repeated name within same municipality; correct point cannot be determined automatically (64) |\n| `ambiguous_no_spatial` | Name recurs across municipalities; no spatial disambiguation succeeded (170) |\n| `unmatched` | No IGM point with matching name found (5) |\n\n### canton_split_group\n\nFor `ambiguous_canton_split` rows, this column holds the lowest `Codigo` among all sibling codes in the group. All siblings resolve to the same IGM candidate pool — this many-to-one mapping is expected and correct.\n\nTo build a deduplicated community key (one per physical place):\n```r\ncommunity_key = coalesce(canton_split_group, Codigo)\n```\n\n## Primary pipeline script\n\n`ine_community_mapping.R` — runs the full pipeline from raw data to crosswalk:\n1. Load INE Excel and IGM GeoJSON\n2. Normalize names (strip non-ASCII, uppercase, collapse punctuation)\n3. Join on department + name → `crosswalk_raw`\n4. Spatial join with GADM municipality boundaries → resolves most ambiguous cases\n5. Name-proximity fallback for points outside GADM polygons\n6. Spatial join with USCA community polygons → `unique_via_usca`\n7. Canton-split detection → `ambiguous_canton_split` + `canton_split_group`\n8. Dispersed-settlement reclassification → `ambiguous_dispersed` (all candidates `tipo_area == \"dis\"`)\n9. Export to `data/crosswalk_ine_igm.rds` and `.csv`\n10. Defines `ine_match_status(codigo)` lookup function (tolerates 10- or 11-digit codes)\n\n## Key relationships\n\n- `ine_geog_2013$Codigo` ↔ `crosswalk$Codigo` (join key: INE → crosswalk)\n- `crosswalk$id_unico` ↔ `igm_sf$id_unico` (join key: crosswalk → IGM point/coordinates)\n- `usca_sf$cod10dig` ↔ `Codigo` after `paste0(\"0\", cod10dig)` (INE code in USCA)\n- GADM `NAME_1`/`NAME_3` → INE `department`/`municipality` via manual name crosswalk in script (many spelling differences)\n\n## Canton splits\n\n344 groups of INE codes point to the same physical community because Bolivia's canton boundaries were reorganised after the 2001 census. 141 municipalities affected; Potosí has the most (36 municipalities). Within a group, the community sequence number (last 3 digits of `Codigo`) is usually preserved — only the canton digit changes.\n\n## Wikipedia reference extraction pipeline\n\nTwo parallel tools extract bibliographic references from Wikipedia citation templates and export them to Zotero:\n\n### Files\n\n| File | Description |\n|------|-------------|\n| `wiki-to-ris.html` | Browser-based JS/HTML tool. Fetches wikitext, extracts citation templates, outputs RIS format. No server required. |\n| `wiki-refs-to-zotero.R` | R pipeline. Same extraction logic plus c2z enrichment (DOI/ISBN lookups), deduplication, direct Zotero API posting, and graceful error handling (`safe_stage`). |\n| `wiki-refs-to-zotero-examples.R` | Usage examples for the R pipeline. |\n\n### Supported citation templates (English Wikipedia)\n\nBoth tools handle: `cite book`, `cite journal`, `cite web`, `cite news`, `cite encyclopedia`, `cite odnb`, `cite magazine`, `cite thesis`, `cite conference`, `cite report`, `cite press release`, `cite av media`, `cite podcast`, `cite speech`, `harvc`.\n\nThe **R version only** additionally handles:\n- `{{Citation}}` — generic template; item type inferred from parameters (`chapter=` → bookSection, `journal=` → journalArticle, default → book).\n- `{{Cite EB1911}}` — Encyclopædia Britannica 11th ed.; maps to encyclopediaArticle with fixed bookTitle.\n\n### Template → Zotero item type mapping\n\n| Template | Zotero itemType | RIS code |\n|----------|----------------|----------|\n| cite book | book | BOOK |\n| cite journal | journalArticle | JOUR |\n| cite web | webpage | ELEC |\n| cite news, cite press release | newspaperArticle | NEWS |\n| cite encyclopedia, cite odnb, cite eb1911 | encyclopediaArticle | ENCYC |\n| cite magazine | magazineArticle | MGZN |\n| cite thesis | thesis | THES |\n| cite conference | conferencePaper | CONF |\n| cite report | report | RPRT |\n| cite av media | videoRecording | VIDEO |\n| harvc, Citation+chapter | bookSection | CHAP |\n| Citation (no chapter) | book | BOOK |\n| fallback | document | GEN |\n\n### Brooklyn test case (6 Mar 2026)\n\nTested all three import methods against the Brooklyn Wikipedia article:\n\n| Source | Items | Unique items |\n|--------|------:|------:|\n| Pipeline (R) | 189 | 54 (mostly `document` from URL-less templates) |\n| CoiNS (Zotero translator) | 153 | 9 (web sources CoiNS scraped from page metadata) |\n| RIS (Zotero translator) | 131 | 1 (whitespace variant) |\n| Shared (all three) | 127 | — |\n\nCoiNS misclassifies most items as `book` or `journalArticle`. RIS preserves types faithfully but captures fewer items. The pipeline captures the most references but produces `document` for non-templated `<ref>` content.\n\n### Known issues & next steps\n\n1. **JS version needs `{{Citation}}` and `{{Cite EB1911}}`** — currently only in R. All templated citation types should be brought into the JS version.\n2. **R version should stop extracting bare (non-templated) `<ref>` content** — these are as often actual footnotes as they are bibliographic references. The `extract_bare_refs()` function should be removed or made opt-in.\n3. **French and Spanish citation templates** need to be mapped and added for multilingual support. See `data/wiki-citation-templates-multilingual.md` for the cross-language template mapping.\n4. **Zotero library index**: `check_library_for_refs()` downloads the entire library (~16k items, ~160 API requests) for duplicate checking. Should be replaced with a cached local index using version-based incremental sync, or per-item search.\n5. **`zotero_access_key` env var** lacks read permission on the personal library. Use `zotero_access_key_write` for all API calls.\n\n---\n\n## wiki-language-diversity-v2.qmd: Macrolanguage Mappings (7 Mar 2026)\n\n### Complete mapping set (added 3 new in Mar 2026)\n\nAll zero-article languages now mapped via `wp_to_bcp47_extra` (lines 84–123):\n\n**Chinese macrolanguage (zho):**\n- `cmn` → `zh` (Mandarin Chinese, 1.29B speakers)\n- `cjy` → `zh` (Jinyu Chinese, 63M)\n- `hsn` → `zh` (Xiang Chinese, 40M)\n\n**Arabic macrolanguage (ara):**\n- `arz` → `ar` (Egyptian Arabic, 78M)\n- `arq` → `ar` (Algerian Arabic, 36M)\n- `apd` → `ar` (Sudanese Arabic, 37M)\n- `aec` → `ar` (Saidi Arabic, 25M)\n- `acm`, `acw`, `acx`, `ayp`, `ayh`, `ayl`, `ayn`, `abh`, `acy` → `ar` (other dialects, ~50M combined)\n\n**Other languages:**\n- `azj` → `az` (North Azerbaijani, 24M)\n- `kpv` → `ku` (Southern Kurdish)\n- `xmv` → `mg` (Antankarana Malagasy, 25M) — **NEW (7 Mar 2026)**\n- `tts` → `th` (Northeastern Thai, 17M) — **NEW (7 Mar 2026)**\n- `lah` → `pnb` (Lahnda, 93M, maps to Western Punjabi) — **NEW (7 Mar 2026)**\n\n### Impact (7 Mar 2026 additions)\n\n| Language | Speakers | Wikipedia | Articles |\n|----------|----------|-----------|----------|\n| Antankarana Malagasy | 25M | Malagasy (mg) | 102,259 |\n| Northeastern Thai | 17M | Thai (th) | 180,368 |\n| Lahnda | 93M | Western Punjabi (pnb) | 75,213 |\n| **Total** | **135M** | — | **357,840** |\n\n### Documentation files\n\n- `documentation/ZERO_ARTICLES_REFERENCE.md` — Updated with three new languages\n- `documentation/WIKI_LANGUAGE_DIVERSITY_LATEST_ADDITIONS.md` — **NEW (7 Mar 2026)** — detailed notes on the three additions\n- `documentation/JOIN_LOGIC_CORRECTION_7MAR2026.md` — **NEW (7 Mar 2026)** — critical fix: map direction was reversed\n\n### Critical Fix: Join Logic (7 Mar 2026)\n\n**The Problem**: The join was backwards. `wp_to_bcp47_extra` was being applied to Wikipedia codes instead of linguameta codes.\n\n**Original (broken)**:\n```r\nwiki_joined <- wiki |> mutate(bcp47_lookup = coalesce(wp_to_bcp47_extra[wp_code_clean], wp_code_clean))\nlinguameta_with_wiki <- linguameta |> left_join(wiki_joined, by = c(\"bcp_47_code\" = \"bcp47_lookup\"))\n```\nResult: cmn tried to match to cmn (not zh) → 0 articles\n\n**Fixed**:\n```r\nlinguameta_with_wiki <- linguameta |>\n  mutate(bcp47_wiki_lookup = coalesce(wp_to_bcp47_extra[bcp_47_code], bcp_47_code)) |>\n  left_join(wiki_joined, by = c(\"bcp47_wiki_lookup\" = \"wp_code_clean\"))\n```\nResult: cmn→zh, lah→pnb, etc. Now correctly matches to Wikipedia\n\n**Impact**: All five languages (cmn, lah, xmv, tts, arz) now appear with proper article counts:\n- Mandarin Chinese: 1.5M articles\n- Egyptian Arabic: 1.3M articles\n- Lahnda: 75K articles\n- Northeastern Thai: 180K articles\n- Antankarana Malagasy: 102K articles\n\n### Comprehensive Mappings (7 Mar 2026 - Extended, two rounds)\n\nAfter systematic search of zero-article languages against available Wikipedia editions, **added 22 additional mappings** (282.8M speakers, 6.35M+ articles):\n\n**New mappings cover:**\n- **Uzbek**: Northern (28M) & Southern (4.2M) → Uzbek Wikipedia (334K articles)\n- **Persian/Pashto**: Iranian Persian (12M), Southern Pashto (14.9M), Central Pashto (6.5M) → Persian & Pashto Wikipedias\n- **Arabic**: Ta'Izzi-Adeni (12M) → Arabic Wikipedia (1.3M articles)\n- **Javanese**: Caribbean Javanese (82M) → Javanese Wikipedia (75K articles)\n- **Thai**: Northern (6.6M) & Southern (5.5M) → Thai Wikipedia (180K articles)\n- **Odia**: Odia (35M) → Odia Wikipedia (20.5K articles)\n- **And others**: Nepali, Swahili, German, Malay, Oromo, Mongolian, Dinka, Albanian\n\n**Impact**: Reduced zero-article languages from 87 to 65 (22 covered). Total speakers now with Wikipedia coverage increased by 283M.\n\n**Files created**: `documentation/COMPREHENSIVE_LANGUAGE_MAPPINGS_7MAR2026.md`\n\n### Second-round additions (7 Mar 2026 - continued)\n\nAfter deeper research into reversed identity mappings, Norwegian/Belarusian orthographic variants, and remaining dialect/macro mismatches, added 17 more:\n\n**Reversed identity mappings** (WP uses non-standard codes; these were previously broken):\n- `yue` → `zh-yue` (Yue Chinese/Cantonese, 79M → 149K articles)\n- `nan` → `zh-min-nan` (Min Nan Chinese, 42M → 434K articles)\n\n**Norwegian**: `nb` → `no` (Norwegian Bokmål, 10.5M → 679K articles). Belarusian `be` was already matched directly; `be-tarask` has no separate linguameta entry.\n\n**New dialect/macro mappings**: `fil`→`tl` (Filipino/Tagalog), `bho`→`bh` (Bhojpuri), `prs`→`fa` (Dari/Persian), `fuv`+`fuc`→`ff` (Fulfulde), `kng`→`kg` (Koongo), `gug`→`gn` (Guaraní), `sdh`→`ku` (Southern Kurdish), `hno`→`pnb` (N. Hindko), `czh`→`zh` (Huizhou Chinese), `bik`→`bcl` (Bikol), `mui`→`ms` (Musi)\n\n**Code fixes**: `kr`→`knc` (Kanuri — WP uses knc not kr); `kpv`→`kv` (Komi-Zyrian — was wrongly mapped to `ku`/Kurdish)\n\n**Additional**: `kok`→`gom` (Konkani → Goan Konkani), `tzm`→`zgh` (Tamazight → Standard Moroccan Amazigh)\n\n**Status after all rounds**: 87 → **47 zero-article languages** (46% reduction)\n"},"elapsedMs":50}</script></tool-result></div>

Here's the context from the last session in the **wiki-graph** project:

**Plan created (Mar 5, 2026):** Build `wiki-refs-to-zotero.R` — an R pipeline to extract Wikipedia citation templates, deduplicate them, and import into Zotero via `c2z`.

**What's been completed since then** (per the memory file):

- Both `wiki-refs-to-zotero.R` (R pipeline) and `wiki-to-ris.html` (browser JS tool) are built and working.
- Tested against the Brooklyn article (Mar 6): R pipeline captured 189 items; RIS and CoiNS Zotero translators got 153 and 131 respectively.

**Open issues to address:**
1. JS version missing `{{Citation}}` and `{{Cite EB1911}}` templates (R-only so far)
2. R version's `extract_bare_refs()` should be removed or made opt-in (bare `<ref>` tags are often footnotes, not bibliography)
3. French/Spanish citation template support — mapping doc at `data/wiki-citation-templates-multilingual.md`
4. Zotero library index is slow (~160 API requests); needs version-based incremental sync
5. Use `zotero_access_key_write` env var (not `zotero_access_key`) for API calls

The **wiki-language-diversity** analysis also continued through March 7, reducing zero-article languages from 87 to 47 via comprehensive macrolanguage mappings.

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01LzA83kstL2ZeQgV4CokSUS"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"text","value":"[File: 4.2KB, 96 lines, showing lines 1-96]\n     1→# _targets.R — ICE Detention Data Pipel...[truncated, total 3717 chars]"},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"[File: 4.2KB, 96 lines, showing lines 1-96]\n# _targets.R — ICE Detention Data Pipeline\n#\n# Run with: targets::tar_make()\n# Visualize: targets::tar_visnetwork()\n# Load a result: targets::tar_read(facilities_panel)\n\nlibrary(targets)\n\ntar_option_set(\n  packages = c(\"readxl\", \"dplyr\", \"stringr\", \"tidyr\", \"purrr\",\n               \"tibble\", \"httr\", \"stringdist\", \"glue\")\n)\n\n# Auto-source all functions in R/\ntar_source()\n\nlist(\n  # ── Metadata ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # Spreadsheet URLs, sheet names, header positions, etc.\n  tar_target(data_file_info, build_data_file_info()),\n\n  # ── Download (rarely run) ──────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # Downloads files that don't already exist locally.\n  # Set to cue = \"never\" so it only runs when you explicitly invalidate it:\n  #   tar_invalidate(raw_xlsx_files); tar_make()\n  tar_target(\n    raw_xlsx_files,\n    download_ice_spreadsheets(data_file_info),\n    format = \"file\",\n    cue = tar_cue(mode = \"never\")\n  ),\n\n  # ── Import headers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # Reads the two-row headers from each spreadsheet and produces\n  # a named list of clean variable name vectors.\n  tar_target(\n    clean_names_list,\n    build_clean_names(data_file_info)\n  ),\n\n  # ── Import raw data ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # Returns a named list of tibbles, one per fiscal year.\n  tar_target(\n    facilities_raw,\n    import_all_years(data_file_info, clean_names_list)\n  ),\n\n  # ── Clean ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # Name standardization, type coercion, Guantanamo patch.\n  tar_target(\n    facilities_clean,\n    clean_all_years(facilities_raw)\n  ),\n\n  # ── Aggregate ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # Row-level sums (ADP by classification, criminality, threat level)\n  # and facility_type_wiki classification.\n  tar_target(\n    facilities_aggregated,\n    aggregate_all_years(facilities_clean)\n  ),\n\n  # ── Facility crosswalk ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # Exact + fuzzy address matching across years → canonical facility IDs.\n  tar_target(\n    facility_crosswalk,\n    build_facility_crosswalk(facilities_aggregated)\n  ),\n\n  # ── Attach canonical IDs ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  tar_target(\n    facilities_keyed,\n    attach_canonical_ids(facilities_aggregated, facility_crosswalk)\n  ),\n\n  # ── Facility presence / trajectory ─────────────────────────────────────────\n  tar_target(\n    facility_presence,\n    build_facility_presence(facilities_keyed)\n  ),\n\n  # ── Panel dataset ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  tar_target(\n    facilities_panel,\n    build_panel(facilities_keyed)\n  ),\n\n  # ── Save outputs to data/ ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # Exports RDS + CSV files for external use.\n  tar_target(\n    saved_files,\n    save_outputs(facility_crosswalk, facility_presence, facilities_panel),\n    format = \"file\"\n  )\n)\n"},"elapsedMs":22}</script></tool-result></div>

\"data/FY21-detentionstats.xlsx\",\n      \"data/FY22-detentionStats.xlsx\",\n      \"data/FY23_detentionStats.xlsx\",\n      \"data/FY24_detentionStats.xlsx\",\n      \"data/FY25_detentionStats09242025.xlsx\",\n      \"data/FY26_detentionStats02022026.xlsx\"\n    ),\n    sheet_name = c(\n      \"Facilities FY19\", \"Facilities EOYFY20 \", \"Facilities FY21 YTD\",\n      \"Facilities FY22\", \"Facilities EOFY23\",\n      \"Facilities EOFY24\", \"Facilities FY25\", \"Facilities FY26\"\n    ),\n    header_rows = list(\n      c(5, 7), c(5, 7), c(5, 7), c(5, 7), c(4, 6),\n      c(5, 7), c(5, 7), c(9, 10)\n    ),\n    right_column = c(\"AE\", \"AE\", \"AE\", \"AD\", \"AG\", \"AB\", \"AB\", \"AA\")\n  )\n\n  info |>\n    dplyr::rowwise() |>\n    dplyr::mutate(\n which(LETTERS_PLUS == right_column)\n    ) |>\n    dplyr::select(-header_rows) |>\n    dplyr::ungroup()\n}\n"},"elapsedMs":5}</script></tool-result></div>

<div><tool-call id="toolu_01Qq72W687fHCqjoZYUX1ZcS"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"read","input":{"file_path":"/Users/bjorkjcr/Dropbox (Personal)/R/ice-detention/R/import.R"},"caption":"R/import.R"}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01Qq72W687fHCqjoZYUX1ZcS"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"text","value":"[File: 3.8KB, 103 lines, showing lines 1-103]\n     1→# Import facility data from ICE Excel...[truncated, total 4133 chars]"},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"[File: 3.8KB, 103 lines, showing lines 1-103]\n# Import facility data from ICE Excel spreadsheets.\n#\n# Two-step process:\n#   1. Read the two-row headers and combine into clean variable names.\n#   2. Read the data rows using those names.\n\n# ── Header extraction ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nheader_rows <- function(data_file_info_row) {\n  with(data_file_info_row, {\n    first_row <- readxl::read_excel(\n      local_file,\n      sheet = sheet_name,\n      range = readxl::cell_limits(\n        ul = c(first_header_row, 1),\n        lr = c(first_header_row, right_column_num)\n      ),\n      col_names = FALSE\n    )\n    second_row <- readxl::read_excel(\n      local_file,\n      sheet = sheet_name,\n      range = readxl::cell_limits(\n        ul = c(second_header_row, 1),\n        lr = c(second_header_row, right_column_num)\n      ),\n      col_names = FALSE\n    )\n    rbind(first_row, second_row)\n  })\n}\n\n# ── Variable name cleaning ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nclean_variable_names_from_header <- function(raw_headers) {\n  clean_names <- raw_headers |>\n    t() |>\n    as.data.frame() |>\n    tidyr::fill(V1) |>\n    dplyr::mutate(\n      V1 = stringr::str_trim(stringr::str_remove_all(V1, \"FY\\\\d\\\\d\")),\n      V1 = stringr::str_remove_all(V1, \":\"),\n      V2 = stringr::str_trim(stringr::str_remove_all(V2, \"FY\\\\d\\\\d\")),\n      V2 = stringr::str_remove_all(V2, \":\"),\n      V1 = dplyr::case_when(\n        stringr::str_detect(V1, \"This list\") ~ \"Facility\",\n        V1 == \"Facility Information\" ~ \"Facility\",\n        V1 == \"ADP Detainee Classification Level\" ~ \"ADP Detainee Classification\",\n        V1 == \"ADP Detainee Security Level\" ~ \"ADP Detainee Security\",\n        V1 == \"ADP ICE Threat Level\" ~ \"ADP\",\n        V1 == \"ADP Mandatory\" ~ \"ADP\",\n        V1 == \"Contract Facility Inspections Information\" ~ \"Inspections\",\n        TRUE ~ V1\n      ),\n      combined = paste(V1, V2),\n      var_name = tolower(combined),\n      var_name = stringr::str_replace_all(var_name, \"[^a-z0-9]+\", \"_\"),\n      var_name = stringr::str_remove_all(var_name, \"^_+|_+$\")\n    ) |>\n    dplyr::pull(var_name)\n\n  clean_names\n}\n\n# ── Build clean names for all years ──────────────────────────────────────────\n\nbuild_clean_names <- function(data_file_info) {\n  raw_headers_list <- purrr::map(seq_len(nrow(data_file_info)), function(i) {\n    header_rows(data_file_info[i, ])\n  })\n  names(raw_headers_list) <- data_file_info$year_name\n\n  clean_names_list <- purrr::map(raw_headers_list, clean_variable_names_from_header)\n  names(clean_names_list) <- data_file_info$year_name\n  clean_names_list\n}\n\n# ── Read facility data for one year ──────────────────────────────────────────\n\nread_facilities_data <- function(data_file_info_row, clean_names_list) {\n  with(data_file_info_row, {\n    readxl::read_xlsx(\n      local_file,\n      sheet = sheet_name,\n      range = readxl::cell_limits(\n        ul = c(first_data_row, 1),\n        lr = c(NA, right_column_num)\n      ),\n      col_names = clean_names_list[[year_name]]\n    )\n  })\n}\n\n# ── Import all years ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nimport_all_years <- function(data_file_info, clean_names_list) {\n  facilities_data_list <- purrr::map(seq_len(nrow(data_file_info)), function(i) {\n    read_facilities_data(data_file_info[i, ], clean_names_list)\n  })\n  names(facilities_data_list) <- data_file_info$year_name\n  facilities_data_list\n}\n"},"elapsedMs":59}</script></tool-result></div>

<div><tool-call id="toolu_01BJmkBYMLveT7bTAtJ4uxBV"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"read","input":{"file_path":"/Users/bjorkjcr/Dropbox (Personal)/R/ice-detention/R/clean.R"},"caption":"R/clean.R"}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01BJmkBYMLveT7bTAtJ4uxBV"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"text","value":"[File: 5.9KB, 158 lines, showing lines 1-158]\n     1→# Clean facility data: name standardi...[truncated, total 6767 chars]"},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"[File: 5.9KB, 158 lines, showing lines 1-158]\n# Clean facility data: name standardization, type coercion, and patches.\n\n# ── Facility name cleaning ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nclean_facility_names <- function(x) {\n  x <- stringr::str_to_title(x)\n\n  # Typos and truncations\n  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, \"Facili\\\\b\", \"Facility\")\n  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, \"Processsing\", \"Processing\")\n\n  # Abbreviation expansions\n  expansions <- c(\n    \"\\\\bDept\\\\.?\\\\b\" = \"Department\",\n    \"\\\\bCtr\\\\.?\\\\b\"  = \"Center\",\n    \"\\\\bCorr\\\\.?\\\\b\" = \"Correctional\",\n    \"\\\\bInst\\\\.?\\\\b\" = \"Institution\",\n    \"\\\\bFed\\\\.?\\\\b\"  = \"Federal\",\n    \"\\\\bDet\\\\.?\\\\b\"  = \"Detention\",\n    \"\\\\bFac\\\\.?\\\\b\"  = \"Facility\",\n    \"\\\\bCo\\\\.?\\\\b\"   = \"County\"\n  )\n  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, expansions)\n\n  # Acronym restoration\n  acronyms <- c(\n    \"\\\\bOf\\\\b\" = \"of\",\n    \"\\\\bUs\\\\b\" = \"US\",\n    \"\\\\bIce\\\\b\" = \"ICE\",\n    \"\\\\bEro\\\\b\" = \"ERO\",\n    \"\\\\bMdc\\\\b\" = \"MDC\",\n    \"\\\\bCca\\\\b\" = \"CCA\",\n    \"\\\\bFci\\\\b\" = \"FCI\",\n    \"\\\\bFdc\\\\b\" = \"FDC\",\n    \"\\\\bJtf\\\\b\" = \"JTF\",\n    \"\\\\bSsm\\\\b\" = \"SSM\",\n    \"\\\\bTgk\\\\b\" = \"TGK\",\n    \"\\\\bIpc\\\\b\" = \"IPC\",\n    \"\\\\bSpc\\\\b\" = \"SPC\",\n    \"\\\\bIah\\\\b\" = \"IAH\",\n    \"\\\\bClipc\\\\b\" = \"CLIPC\",\n    \"\\\\bDigsa\\\\b\" = \"DIGSA\",\n    \"\\\\bIgsa\\\\b\"  = \"IGSA\"\n  )\n  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, acronyms)\n\n  # State abbreviation expansions\n  states <- c(\n    \"\\\\(Fl\\\\)\" = \"(Florida)\",\n    \"\\\\(In\\\\)\" = \"(Indiana)\",\n    \"\\\\(Mo\\\\)\" = \"(Missouri)\",\n    \"\\\\(Mt\\\\)\" = \"(Montana)\",\n    \"\\\\(Ne\\\\)\" = \"(Nebraska)\",\n    \"\\\\(Ny\\\\)\" = \"(New York)\",\n    \"\\\\(Tx\\\\)\" = \"(Texas)\",\n    \"\\\\(Ut\\\\)\" = \"(Utah)\",\n    \", Nm\\\\b\"  = \", New Mexico\",\n    \", Mt\\\\b\"  = \", Montana\"\n  )\n  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, states)\n\n  # Facility-specific name fixes\n  names_map <- c(\n    \"CCA, Florence Correctional Center\" = \"Central Arizona Florence Correctional Complex\",\n    \"Eloy Federal Contract Facility\" = \"Eloy Detention Center\",\n    \"T Don Hutto Detention Center\" = \"T. Don Hutto Detention Center\",\n    \"Torrance/Estancia, New Mexico\" = \"Torrance County Detention Facility\",\n    \"Houston Contract Detention Facility\" = \"Houston Processing Center\",\n    \"Elizabeth Contract Detention Facility\" = \"Elizabeth Detention Center\",\n    \"Folkston D Ray ICE Processing Center\" = \"Folkston ICE Processing Center\",\n    \"Robert A Deyton Detention Facility\" = \"Robert A. Deyton Detention Center\",\n    \"ERO El Paso Camp East Montana\" = \"Camp East Montana\",\n    \"Port Isabel SPC\" = \"Port Isabel Detention Center\",\n    \"Bluebonnet Detention Facility\" = \"Bluebonnet Detention Center\"\n  )\n  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, names_map)\n\n  x\n}\n\n# ── City name fixes ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nfix_city_names <- function(x) {\n  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, \"Ft.lauderdale\", \"Fort Lauderdale\")\n  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, \"Ft. Lauderdale\", \"Fort Lauderdale\")\n  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, \"Cottonwood Fall\", \"Cottonwood Falls\")\n  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, \"Sault Ste Marie\", \"Sault Ste. Marie\")\n  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, \"Bunkerhill\", \"Bunker Hill\")\n  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, \"Mcelhattan\", \"McElhattan\")\n  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, \"Mcfarland\", \"McFarland\")\n  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, \"Mccook\", \"McCook\")\n  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, \"Mccall\", \"McCall\")\n  x\n}\n\n# ── Main cleaning function ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nclean_facilities_data <- function(facilities_data) {\n  numerical_columns <- c(\n    \"facility_average_length_of_stay_alos\",\n    \"adp_detainee_classification_level_a\",\n    \"adp_detainee_classification_level_b\",\n    \"adp_detainee_classification_level_c\",\n    \"adp_detainee_classification_level_d\",\n    \"adp_criminality_male_crim\",\n    \"adp_criminality_male_non_crim\",\n    \"adp_criminality_female_crim\",\n    \"adp_criminality_female_non_crim\",\n    \"adp_ice_threat_level_1\",\n    \"adp_ice_threat_level_2\",\n    \"adp_ice_threat_level_3\",\n    \"adp_no_ice_threat_level\",\n    \"adp_mandatory\",\n    \"inspections_guaranteed_minimum\"\n  )\n  numerical_columns_present <- numerical_columns[numerical_columns %in% names(facilities_data)]\n\n  facilities_data_clean <- facilities_data |>\n    dplyr::mutate(\n      dplyr::across(\n        .cols = facility_average_length_of_stay_alos:adp_mandatory,\n        .fns = ~ round(as.numeric(.), 1)\n      ),\n      inspections_guaranteed_minimum = as.integer(inspections_guaranteed_minimum),\n      inspections_last_inspection_type = dplyr::case_when(\n        inspections_last_inspection_type %in% c(\"PRE-OCCUPANCY\", \"Pre-Occupancy\") ~ \"PREOCC\",\n        TRUE ~ inspections_last_inspection_type\n      ),\n      facility_name = clean_facility_names(facility_name),\n      facility_city = stringr::str_to_title(facility_city),\n      facility_address = stringr::str_to_title(facility_address),\n      facility_male_female = stringr::str_to_title(facility_male_female),\n      facility_city = fix_city_names(facility_city),\n      dplyr::across(\n        .cols = -dplyr::all_of(numerical_columns_present),\n        .fns = as.character\n      )\n    )\n\n  # Guantanamo patch\n  guantanamo_patch <- tibble::tibble(\n    facility_name = \"JTF Camp Six\",\n    facility_city = \"Guantanamo Bay\",\n    facility_state = \"Cuba\",\n    facility_address = \"Bldg 2144, U.S. Naval Station Guantanamo Bay\"\n  )\n  facilities_data_clean <- facilities_data_clean |>\n    dplyr::rows_update(guantanamo_patch, by = \"facility_name\", unmatched = \"ignore\")\n\n  facilities_data_clean\n}\n\n# ── Clean all years ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nclean_all_years <- function(facilities_data_list) {\n  purrr::map(facilities_data_list, clean_facilities_data)\n}\n"},"elapsedMs":57}</script></tool-result></div>



<div><tool-call id="toolu_01S6DQJaiiZvFpRHi5dJEt7K"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"read","input":{"file_path":"/Users/bjorkjcr/Dropbox (Personal)/R/ice-detention/R/aggregate.R"},"caption":"R/aggregate.R"}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01S6DQJaiiZvFpRHi5dJEt7K"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"text","value":"[File: 2.2KB, 49 lines, showing lines 1-49]\n     1→# Aggregate facility data: row-level su...[truncated, total 2677 chars]"},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"[File: 2.2KB, 49 lines, showing lines 1-49]\n# Aggregate facility data: row-level sums and derived classifications.\n\naggregate_facilities_data <- function(facilities_data) {\n  facilities_data |>\n    dplyr::mutate(\n      sum_classification_levels = rowSums(\n        dplyr::select(dplyr::pick(dplyr::everything()),\n                      dplyr::starts_with(\"adp_detainee_classification_level_\")),\n        na.rm = TRUE\n      ),\n      sum_criminality_levels = rowSums(\n        dplyr::select(dplyr::pick(dplyr::everything()),\n                      dplyr::starts_with(\"adp_criminality_\")),\n        na.rm = TRUE\n      ),\n      sum_threat_levels = rowSums(\n        dplyr::select(dplyr::pick(dplyr::everything()),\n                      dplyr::starts_with(\"adp_ice_threat_level_\") |\n                        dplyr::starts_with(\"adp_no_ice_threat_level\")),\n        na.rm = TRUE\n      )\n    ) |>\n    dplyr::mutate(\n      share_non_crim = (adp_criminality_male_non_crim + adp_criminality_female_non_crim) /\n        sum_criminality_levels,\n      share_no_threat = adp_no_ice_threat_level / sum_threat_levels\n    ) |>\n    dplyr::mutate(\n      facility_type_wiki = dplyr::case_when(\n        stringr::str_detect(facility_type_detailed, \"IGSA\") ~ \"Jail\",\n        stringr::str_detect(facility_type_detailed, \"USMS IGA\") ~ \"Jail\",\n        stringr::str_detect(facility_type_detailed, \"DIGSA\") ~ \"Dedicated Migrant Detention Center\",\n        stringr::str_detect(facility_type_detailed, \"STATE\") ~ \"State Migrant Detention Center\",\n        stringr::str_detect(facility_type_detailed, \"BOP\") ~ \"Federal Prison\",\n        stringr::str_detect(facility_type_detailed, \"Family\") ~ \"Private Family Detention Center\",\n        stringr::str_detect(facility_type_detailed, \"CDF\") ~ \"Private Migrant Detention Center\",\n        stringr::str_detect(facility_type_detailed, \"USMS CDF\") ~ \"Private Migrant Detention Center\",\n        stringr::str_detect(facility_type_detailed, \"SPC\") ~ \"ICE Migrant Detention Center\",\n        stringr::str_detect(facility_type_detailed, \"STAGING\") ~ \"ICE Short-Term Migrant Detention Center\",\n        stringr::str_detect(facility_type_detailed, \"DOD\") ~ \"Military Detention Center\",\n        TRUE ~ \"Other\"\n      )\n    )\n}\n\naggregate_all_years <- function(facilities_data_list) {\n  purrr::map(facilities_data_list, aggregate_facilities_data)\n}\n"},"elapsedMs":13}</script></tool-result></div>

<div><tool-call id="toolu_01QPw4KfVYRT1RdBWAPXxD8D"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"read","input":{"file_path":"/Users/bjorkjcr/Dropbox (Personal)/R/ice-detention/R/crosswalk.R"},"caption":"R/crosswalk.R"}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01QPw4KfVYRT1RdBWAPXxD8D"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"text","value":"[File: 12.9KB, 297 lines, showing lines 1-297]\n     1→# Build a canonical facility crosswa...[truncated, total 14503 chars]"},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"[File: 12.9KB, 297 lines, showing lines 1-297]\n# Build a canonical facility crosswalk across all years.\n#\n# Matching strategy (two passes):\n#   1. Exact match on facility_name + facility_city + facility_state\n#   2. Within the same ZIP code, fuzzy address string similarity (OSA >= 0.80)\n#      resolves cases where the same physical facility appears under a different\n#      name in different years.\n#\n# Returns a list with two elements:\n#   $crosswalk  — maps every (name, city, state) variant to canonical_id/name\n#   $presence   — one row per canonical facility; TRUE/FALSE for each fiscal year\n\nyear_order <- c(\"FY19\", \"FY20\", \"FY21\", \"FY22\", \"FY23\", \"FY24\", \"FY25\", \"FY26\")\n\n# ── Do-not-merge pairs ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# Genuinely distinct facilities that share a ZIP and similar addresses.\n\ndo_not_merge_pairs <- function() {\n  tibble::tribble(\n    ~name_a,                              ~name_b,\n    \"Folkston Main IPC\",                  \"Annex - Folkston IPC\",\n    \"Folkston Main IPC\",                  \"Folkston Annex IPC\",\n    \"Main - Folkston IPC (D Ray James)\",  \"Annex - Folkston IPC\",\n    \"Main - Folkston IPC (D Ray James)\",  \"Folkston Annex IPC\",\n    \"Joe Corley Processing Center\",       \"Montgomery ICE Processing Center\",\n    \"Joe Corley ICE Processing Center\",   \"Montgomery ICE Processing Center\"\n  )\n}\n\n# ── Canonical name overrides ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\ncanonical_name_overrides_table <- function() {\n  tibble::tribble(\n    ~lookup_name,                                          ~canonical_name,                                        ~canonical_city,  ~canonical_state,\n    \"Adams County Detention Center\",                       \"Adams County Detention Center (Mississippi)\",           \"Natchez\",        \"MS\",\n    \"Phelps County Jail\",                                  \"Phelps County Jail (Nebraska)\",                         \"Holdrege\",       \"NE\",\n    \"Nye County Detention Center, Southern (Pahrump)\",     \"Nye County Detention Center (Nevada)\",                  \"Pahrump\",        \"NV\",\n    \"Baker County Sheriff Department.\",                    \"Baker County Sheriff's Office\",                         \"Macclenny\",      \"FL\",\n    \"Buffalo (Batavia) Service Processing Center\",         \"Buffalo (Batavia) Service Processing Center\",           \"Batavia\",        \"NY\",\n    \"Farmville Detention Center\",                          \"Farmville Detention Center\",                            \"Farmville\",      \"VA\",\n    \"Northwest ICE Processing Center\",                     \"Northwest ICE Processing Center\",                       \"Tacoma\",         \"WA\"\n  )\n}\n\n# ── Union-Find helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nnew_union_find <- function(ids) {\n  parent <- stats::setNames(ids, ids)\n  env <- new.env(parent = emptyenv())\n  env$parent <- parent\n  env\n}\n\nuf_find <- function(uf, x) {\n  while (uf$parent[as.character(x)] != x) x <- uf$parent[as.character(x)]\n  x\n}\n\nuf_union <- function(uf, a, b) {\n  ra <- uf_find(uf, a)\n  rb <- uf_find(uf, b)\n  if (ra != rb) uf$parent[as.character(ra)] <- rb\n}\n\n# ── Main crosswalk builder ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nbuild_facility_crosswalk <- function(facilities_data_list) {\n  # Pool all distinct (name, city, state, address, zip) combos\n  all_facilities_norm <- facilities_data_list |>\n    purrr::imap_dfr(\\(df, yr) df |>\n      dplyr::select(facility_name, facility_city, facility_state,\n                    facility_address, facility_zip) |>\n      dplyr::mutate(year = yr)) |>\n    dplyr::mutate(\n      zip5      = stringr::str_pad(facility_zip, width = 5, side = \"left\", pad = \"0\"),\n      addr_norm = stringr::str_to_upper(stringr::str_trim(stringr::str_squish(facility_address)))\n    )\n\n  # Pass 1: provisional ID per exact name+city+state combo\n  facility_key_exact <- all_facilities_norm |>\n    dplyr::select(facility_name, facility_city, facility_state, addr_norm, zip5) |>\n    dplyr::distinct() |>\n    dplyr::group_by(facility_name, facility_city, facility_state) |>\n    dplyr::summarise(addr_norm = dplyr::first(addr_norm),\n                     zip5 = dplyr::first(zip5),\n                     .groups = \"drop\") |>\n    dplyr::mutate(facility_id = dplyr::row_number())\n\n  # Pass 2: fuzzy address merge within same ZIP\n  merge_candidates <- facility_key_exact |>\n    dplyr::filter(!is.na(addr_norm), !is.na(zip5)) |>\n    dplyr::group_by(zip5) |>\n    dplyr::filter(dplyr::n() > 1) |>\n    dplyr::group_modify(\\(grp, key) {\n      n <- nrow(grp)\n      pairs <- expand.grid(i = seq_len(n), j = seq_len(n)) |> dplyr::filter(i < j)\n      pairs |> dplyr::transmute(\n        facility_id_a = grp$facility_id[i],\n        facility_id_b = grp$facility_id[j],\n        addr_a        = grp$addr_norm[i],\n        addr_b        = grp$addr_norm[j],\n        addr_dist     = stringdist::stringdist(addr_a, addr_b, method = \"osa\"),\n        addr_sim      = 1 - addr_dist / pmax(nchar(addr_a), nchar(addr_b))\n      )\n    }) |>\n    dplyr::ungroup()\n\n  strong_addr_matches <- merge_candidates |> dplyr::filter(addr_sim >= 0.80)\n\n  # Apply do-not-merge exclusions\n  dnm <- do_not_merge_pairs() |>\n    dplyr::left_join(facility_key_exact |> dplyr::select(facility_name, facility_id),\n                     by = c(\"name_a\" = \"facility_name\")) |>\n    dplyr::rename(id_a = facility_id) |>\n    dplyr::left_join(facility_key_exact |> dplyr::select(facility_name, facility_id),\n                     by = c(\"name_b\" = \"facility_name\")) |>\n    dplyr::rename(id_b = facility_id)\n\n  strong_addr_matches <- strong_addr_matches |>\n    dplyr::anti_join(dnm, by = c(\"facility_id_a\" = \"id_a\", \"facility_id_b\" = \"id_b\")) |>\n    dplyr::anti_join(dnm, by = c(\"facility_id_a\" = \"id_b\", \"facility_id_b\" = \"id_a\"))\n\n  # Union-Find to cluster facility_ids\n  uf <- new_union_find(facility_key_exact$facility_id)\n  for (i in seq_len(nrow(strong_addr_matches))) {\n    uf_union(uf, strong_addr_matches$facility_id_a[i],\n             strong_addr_matches$facility_id_b[i])\n  }\n\n  canonical_map <- tibble::tibble(facility_id = facility_key_exact$facility_id) |>\n    dplyr::mutate(canonical_id = purrr::map_int(facility_id, \\(x) uf_find(uf, x)))\n\n  # Choose canonical name: most-recent year's name per cluster\n  all_facilities_keyed <- all_facilities_norm |>\n    dplyr::left_join(\n      facility_key_exact |> dplyr::select(facility_name, facility_city, facility_state, facility_id),\n      by = c(\"facility_name\", \"facility_city\", \"facility_state\")\n    ) |>\n    dplyr::left_join(canonical_map, by = \"facility_id\")\n\n  canonical_names <- all_facilities_keyed |>\n    dplyr::mutate(year_rank = match(year, year_order)) |>\n    dplyr::group_by(canonical_id) |>\n    dplyr::slice_max(year_rank, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) |>\n    dplyr::ungroup() |>\n    dplyr::select(canonical_id,\n                  canonical_name = facility_name,\n                  canonical_city = facility_city,\n                  canonical_state = facility_state)\n\n  # Apply manual overrides\n  overrides <- canonical_name_overrides_table() |>\n    dplyr::left_join(facility_key_exact |> dplyr::select(facility_name, facility_id),\n                     by = c(\"lookup_name\" = \"facility_name\")) |>\n    dplyr::left_join(canonical_map, by = \"facility_id\") |>\n    dplyr::select(canonical_id, canonical_name, canonical_city, canonical_state) |>\n    dplyr::filter(!is.na(canonical_id))\n\n  canonical_names <- canonical_names |>\n    dplyr::rows_update(overrides, by = \"canonical_id\", unmatched = \"ignore\")\n\n  # Final crosswalk\n  facility_crosswalk <- facility_key_exact |>\n    dplyr::left_join(canonical_map, by = \"facility_id\") |>\n    dplyr::left_join(canonical_names, by = \"canonical_id\") |>\n    dplyr::select(facility_id, canonical_id, canonical_name, canonical_city, canonical_state,\n                  facility_name, facility_city, facility_state)\n\n  message(glue::glue(\n    \"Canonical facilities: {dplyr::n_distinct(facility_crosswalk$canonical_id)}\",\n    \" (from {nrow(facility_key_exact)} exact name+city+state variants,\",\n    \" {nrow(strong_addr_matches)} address-based merges)\"\n  ))\n\n  facility_crosswalk\n}\n\n# ── Attach canonical IDs to each year's data ────────────────────────────────\n\nattach_canonical_ids <- function(facilities_data_list, facility_crosswalk) {\n  purrr::imap(facilities_data_list, \\(df, yr) {\n    df |>\n      dplyr::left_join(\n        facility_crosswalk |>\n          dplyr::select(facility_name, facility_city, facility_state,\n                        canonical_id, canonical_name),\n        by = c(\"facility_name\", \"facility_city\", \"facility_state\")\n      )\n  })\n}\n\n# ── Facility presence and trajectory ─────────────────────────────────────────\n\nbuild_facility_presence <- function(facilities_keyed) {\n  year_cols_present <- year_order[year_order %in% names(facilities_keyed)]\n  if (length(year_cols_present) == 0) {\n    year_cols_present <- year_order\n  }\n  first_year       <- head(year_cols_present, 1)\n  most_recent_year <- tail(year_cols_present, 1)\n\n  presence <- facilities_keyed |>\n    purrr::imap_dfr(\\(df, yr) df |>\n      dplyr::select(canonical_id, canonical_name) |>\n      dplyr::distinct() |>\n      dplyr::mutate(year = yr)) |>\n    dplyr::mutate(present = TRUE) |>\n    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = year, values_from = present,\n                       values_fill = FALSE, names_sort = FALSE) |>\n    dplyr::select(canonical_id, canonical_name, dplyr::any_of(year_cols_present)) |>\n    dplyr::mutate(n_years = rowSums(dplyr::across(dplyr::any_of(year_cols_present))))\n\n  # first_seen / last_seen\n  presence <- presence |>\n    dplyr::rowwise() |>\n    dplyr::mutate(\n      first_seen = year_cols_present[dplyr::c_across(dplyr::any_of(year_cols_present))][1],\n      last_seen  = tail(year_cols_present[dplyr::c_across(dplyr::any_of(year_cols_present))], 1)\n    ) |>\n    dplyr::ungroup()\n\n  # trajectory\n  presence <- presence |>\n    dplyr::mutate(\n      trajectory = dplyr::case_when(\n        first_seen == first_year & last_seen == most_recent_year &\n          n_years == length(year_cols_present) ~ \"continuous\",\n        first_seen == first_year & last_seen == most_recent_year ~ \"persistent_gaps\",\n        first_seen == first_year & last_seen != most_recent_year ~ \"closed\",\n        first_seen != first_year & last_seen == most_recent_year ~ \"new\",\n        TRUE ~ \"transient\"\n      )\n    )\n\n  # n_years_current: consecutive years ending at most_recent_year\n  presence <- presence |>\n    dplyr::mutate(\n      n_years_current = apply(\n        dplyr::pick(dplyr::all_of(year_order[year_order %in% names(presence)])),\n        1, function(row) {\n          if (!isTRUE(row[most_recent_year])) return(0L)\n          count <- 0L\n          for (yr in rev(year_order[year_order %in% names(presence)])) {\n            if (isTRUE(row[yr])) count <- count + 1L else break\n          }\n          count\n        })\n    )\n\n  # n_years_initial: consecutive years from first_year\n  presence <- presence |>\n    dplyr::mutate(\n      n_years_initial = apply(\n        dplyr::pick(dplyr::all_of(year_order[year_order %in% names(presence)])),\n        1, function(row) {\n          if (!isTRUE(row[first_year])) return(0L)\n          count <- 0L\n          for (yr in year_order[year_order %in% names(presence)]) {\n            if (isTRUE(row[yr])) count <- count + 1L else break\n          }\n          count\n        })\n    )\n\n  presence\n}\n\n# ── Build panel dataset ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nbuild_panel <- function(facilities_keyed) {\n  facilities_keyed |>\n    purrr::imap_dfr(\\(df, yr) df |> dplyr::mutate(fiscal_year = yr)) |>\n    dplyr::relocate(fiscal_year, canonical_id, canonical_name,\n                    facility_name, facility_city, facility_state)\n}\n\n# ── Save outputs to data/ ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nsave_outputs <- function(facility_crosswalk, facility_presence, facilities_panel) {\n  paths <- c(\n    \"data/facility_crosswalk.rds\",\n    \"data/facility_crosswalk.csv\",\n    \"data/facility_presence.rds\",\n    \"data/facility_presence.csv\",\n    \"data/facilities_panel.rds\",\n    \"data/facilities_panel.csv\"\n  )\n  saveRDS(facility_crosswalk, paths[1])\n  write.csv(facility_crosswalk, paths[2], row.names = FALSE)\n  saveRDS(facility_presence, paths[3])\n  write.csv(facility_presence, paths[4], row.names = FALSE)\n  saveRDS(facilities_panel, paths[5])\n  write.csv(facilities_panel, paths[6], row.names = FALSE)\n  message(\"Saved: \", paste(basename(paths), collapse = \", \"))\n  paths\n}\n"},"elapsedMs":57}</script></tool-result></div>

<div><tool-call id="toolu_01BqXJhQhW9C6WS8PiQuq1jZ"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"read","input":{"file_path":"/Users/bjorkjcr/Dropbox (Personal)/R/ice-detention/R/download.R"},"caption":"R/download.R"}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01BqXJhQhW9C6WS8PiQuq1jZ"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"text","value":"[File: 674B, 22 lines, showing lines 1-22]\n     1→# Download ICE detention spreadsheets fr...[truncated, total 871 chars]"},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"[File: 674B, 22 lines, showing lines 1-22]\n# Download ICE detention spreadsheets from ice.gov.\n#\n# Only downloads files that don't already exist locally.\n# Returns the vector of local file paths (for targets file tracking).\n\ndownload_ice_spreadsheets <- function(data_file_info) {\n  for (i in seq_len(nrow(data_file_info))) {\n    local <- data_file_info$local_file[i]\n    if (!file.exists(local)) {\n      message(\"Downloading \", basename(local), \" ...\")\n      httr::GET(\n        data_file_info$url[i],\n        httr::write_disk(local, overwrite = FALSE)\n      )\n    } else {\n      message(\"Already exists: \", basename(local))\n    }\n  }\n  # Return paths so targets can track them as files\n  data_file_info$local_file\n}\n"},"elapsedMs":53}</script></tool-result></div>

<div><tool-call id="toolu_01GH3EFBsxJYjCkXcUNytaaa"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"write","input":{"file_path":"/Users/bjorkjcr/Dropbox (Personal)/R/ice-detention/AGENTS.md","content":"# ice-detention project memory\n\n## Project overview\n\n`targets`-based R pipeline for import...[truncated, total 7333 chars]"},"caption":"AGENTS.md","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"# ice-detention project memory\n\n## Project overview\n\n`targets`-based R pipeline for importing, cleaning, and harmonizing 8 years of U.S. ICE detention facility statistics (FY 2019–FY 2026). Extracted and refactored from `import-ice-detention.qmd` in the `wiki-graph` project.\n\n## File system conventions\n\n- Project root: `~/Dropbox (Personal)/R/ice-detention/`\n- Raw data and derived outputs in `data/`\n- All pipeline functions in `R/` (auto-sourced by `tar_source()`)\n- Use `here::here()` for paths in scripts outside `_targets.R`\n\n## Data sources\n\nAll raw spreadsheets live in `data/`:\n\n| File | Fiscal Year |\n|------|-------------|\n| `FY19-detentionstats.xlsx` | FY 2019 |\n| `FY20-detentionstats.xlsx` | FY 2020 |\n| `FY21-detentionstats.xlsx` | FY 2021 |\n| `FY22-detentionStats.xlsx` | FY 2022 |\n| `FY23_detentionStats.xlsx` | FY 2023 |\n| `FY24_detentionStats.xlsx` | FY 2024 |\n| `FY25_detentionStats09242025.xlsx` | FY 2025 |\n| `FY26_detentionStats02022026.xlsx` | FY 2026 |\n\nSource URL pattern: `https://www.ice.gov/doclib/detention/FY{nn}-detentionstats.xlsx`\n\nEach file has a \"Facilities FY{nn}\" sheet with a 2-row merged header. Header row positions and rightmost data column vary by year — all encoded in `build_data_file_info()`.\n\n## Derived data outputs (in `data/`)\n\n| File | Description |\n|------|-------------|\n| `facility_crosswalk.rds/.csv` | Maps every (name, city, state) variant to `canonical_id` / `canonical_name` |\n| `facility_presence.rds/.csv` | One row per canonical facility; FY19–FY26 booleans + trajectory classification |\n| `facilities_panel.rds/.csv` | Long panel: one row per facility per year, all ~30 measurement variables |\n| `facilities_fy26_wiki_matches.rds` | FY26 rows with Wikipedia slug and management columns added |\n| `contractors-patch-fy26.csv` | Manual management company corrections for 98 FY26 facilities |\n| `ice-detention-data-dictionary-FY26.md/.csv` | Variable definitions and acronym guide |\n\n## `_targets.R` pipeline targets\n\n| Target | Function | Description |\n|--------|----------|-------------|\n| `data_file_info` | `build_data_file_info()` | Tibble of file paths, sheet names, header row positions |\n| `raw_xlsx_files` | `download_ice_spreadsheets()` | Downloads missing files (cue = \"never\") |\n| `clean_names_list` | `build_clean_names()` | Named list of variable name vectors, one per year |\n| `facilities_raw` | `import_all_years()` | Named list of raw tibbles, one per year |\n| `facilities_clean` | `clean_all_years()` | Name/city standardization, type coercion, Guantanamo patch |\n| `facilities_aggregated` | `aggregate_all_years()` | Row-level ADP sums, `share_non_crim`, `share_no_threat`, `facility_type_wiki` |\n| `facility_crosswalk` | `build_facility_crosswalk()` | Exact + fuzzy address matching → canonical IDs |\n| `facilities_keyed` | `attach_canonical_ids()` | Panel data with `canonical_id` / `canonical_name` joined |\n| `facility_presence` | `build_facility_presence()` | Per-facility year presence + trajectory label |\n| `facilities_panel` | `build_panel()` | Long format panel dataset |\n| `saved_files` | `save_outputs()` | Writes RDS + CSV to `data/`; format = \"file\" |\n\n## R/ function files\n\n| File | Key functions |\n|------|---------------|\n| `metadata.R` | `build_data_file_info()` |\n| `download.R` | `download_ice_spreadsheets()` |\n| `import.R` | `header_rows()`, `clean_variable_names_from_header()`, `build_clean_names()`, `read_facilities_data()`, `import_all_years()` |\n| `clean.R` | `clean_facility_names()`, `fix_city_names()`, `clean_facilities_data()`, `clean_all_years()` |\n| `aggregate.R` | `aggregate_facilities_data()`, `aggregate_all_years()` |\n| `crosswalk.R` | `build_facility_crosswalk()`, `attach_canonical_ids()`, `build_facility_presence()`, `build_panel()`, `save_outputs()` |\n| `add-wikipedia-matches.R` | `add_wikipedia_matches(df, name_col, lang=\"en\")` — uses WikipediR / MediaWiki API, rate-limited |\n| `gh-copy-assets.R` | JS copy-to-clipboard helper for HTML output |\n\n## Key variables\n\nAll column names are snake_case derived from the 2-row Excel headers.\n\n| Variable | Description |\n|----------|-------------|\n| `facility_name`, `facility_city`, `facility_state`, `facility_zip`, `facility_address` | Facility identity |\n| `facility_type_detailed` | Raw ICE type code (IGSA, DIGSA, SPC, BOP, CDF, etc.) |\n| `facility_type_wiki` | Derived readable type for Wikipedia use |\n| `facility_average_length_of_stay_alos` | Average length of stay |\n| `adp_detainee_classification_level_{a-d}` | ADP by security level |\n| `adp_criminality_male_crim/non_crim`, `adp_criminality_female_crim/non_crim` | ADP by criminality × gender |\n| `adp_ice_threat_level_{1-3}`, `adp_no_ice_threat_level` | ADP by ICE threat level |\n| `adp_mandatory` | Mandatory detention ADP |\n| `inspections_guaranteed_minimum` | Contracted bed capacity (integer) |\n| `inspections_last_inspection_type`, `..._end_date`, `..._standard`, `..._final_rating` | Inspection info |\n| `share_non_crim`, `share_no_threat` | Derived: non-criminal / no-threat shares |\n| `canonical_id`, `canonical_name` | Post-crosswalk facility identifiers |\n\n## Facility type → wiki classification\n\n| `facility_type_detailed` contains | `facility_type_wiki` |\n|-----------------------------------|----------------------|\n| IGSA | Jail |\n| USMS IGA | Jail |\n| DIGSA | Dedicated Migrant Detention Center |\n| STATE | State Migrant Detention Center |\n| BOP | Federal Prison |\n| Family | Private Family Detention Center |\n| CDF / USMS CDF | Private Migrant Detention Center |\n| SPC | ICE Migrant Detention Center |\n| STAGING | ICE Short-Term Migrant Detention Center |\n| DOD | Military Detention Center |\n\n## Crosswalk logic\n\n**Pass 1:** Exact match on `(facility_name, facility_city, facility_state)` → provisional `facility_id`.\n\n**Pass 2:** Within the same ZIP code, compute OSA string similarity on `facility_address`. Pairs with similarity ≥ 0.80 are merged via union-find, except for pairs in `do_not_merge_pairs()`.\n\n**Canonical name:** Most recent year's name for each cluster (ties broken by `year_order`).\n\n**Manual overrides:** `canonical_name_overrides_table()` in `crosswalk.R` — 7 entries correcting ambiguous state suffixes, punctuation, etc.\n\n**Trajectory labels** (in `facility_presence`):\n- `continuous` — present every year FY19–FY26\n- `persistent_gaps` — present in FY19 and FY26 but with gaps\n- `closed` — present in FY19, absent from FY26\n- `new` — absent from FY19, present in FY26\n- `transient` — absent from both FY19 and FY26\n\n## What is NOT yet ported from the original Qmd\n\nThe following sections of `import-ice-detention.qmd` (wiki-graph) have not yet been added as targets:\n\n1. **Wikipedia harmonization** — fuzzy matching of ICE facilities to the Wikipedia \"List of immigrant detention sites\" article; adds `wiki_slug` and `management` columns. Uses `add_wikipedia_matches()` already in `R/`.\n2. **Wikitext table generation** — `get_wikitable()` converts the panel to MediaWiki markup.\n3. **Visualizations** — treemaps (by facility type; by state × type) and three interactive Leaflet maps.\n\n## Dependencies\n\nCore: `readxl`, `dplyr`, `stringr`, `tidyr`, `purrr`, `tibble`, `httr`, `stringdist`, `glue`, `targets`\n\nFor Wikipedia targets (not yet added): `WikipediR`, `rvest`\n\nFor visualization targets (not yet added): `treemapify`, `ggplot2`, `mapview`, `leaflet`\n"}}</script></tool-call></div>

Written `AGENTS.md` to the project root. Here's a summary of what's been captured:

**Pipeline status — fully ported to targets:**
All core stages are done: metadata → download → import → clean → aggregate → crosswalk → panel → save outputs. The `R/` functions match what was in the original Qmd.

**Not yet ported (noted in the memory file):**
1. **Wikipedia harmonization** — matching facilities to the "List of immigrant detention sites" Wikipedia article, adding `wiki_slug` and `management`. The function (`add_wikipedia_matches`) is already in `R/`, just not wired into `_targets.R`.
2. **Wikitext table generation** — `get_wikitable()` for producing MediaWiki markup.
3. **Visualizations** — treemaps and three Leaflet maps.

There's no `renv.lock` file, so there's nothing to restore from. renv is activated but the library is effectively empty.

You have two options:

**Option 1 — Disable renv** (simpler, if you don't need package isolation for this project). Delete or comment out the `.Rprofile` line and restart R:
```r
# source("renv/activate.R")   # comment this out
```
Then the project will use your regular system library where everything is already installed.

**Option 2 — Initialize the renv library** by installing the needed packages into it:
```r
renv::install(c("readxl", "dplyr", "stringr", "tidyr", "purrr",
                "tibble", "httr", "stringdist", "glue", "targets"))
renv::snapshot()  # saves a lockfile for reproducibility
```

Given that this project was migrated from `wiki-graph` (which presumably uses your system library), Option 1 is probably the path of least resistance unless you have a specific reason for isolation. Did you intend to set up renv here, or did it get created by default?

The full set of packages needed, combining `_targets.R` declarations plus `add-wikipedia-matches.R`:

- From pipeline: `readxl`, `dplyr`, `stringr`, `tidyr`, `purrr`, `tibble`, `httr`, `stringdist`, `glue`
- From `add-wikipedia-matches.R`: `WikipediR`, `jsonlite`
- `htmltools` is a `targets` dependency that wasn't pulled in automatically

Run this to install everything at once:

```r
renv::install(c(
  "htmltools",
  "readxl", "dplyr", "stringr", "tidyr", "purrr",
  "tibble", "httr", "stringdist", "glue",
  "WikipediR", "jsonlite"
))
```

Then snapshot once it succeeds:

```r
renv::snapshot()
```

That will write a `renv.lock` file capturing the exact versions — the main point of using renv.

The `_targets/` store is created by running the pipeline — it doesn't exist yet. Run:

```r
tar_make()
```

That executes the pipeline and builds the store. Once it completes, `tar_load_everything()` will work.

- `coordinates`: 25°51′42″N 080°53′49″W — missing from our dataset entirely

, only the first fiscal year it appears
