Now simplify `geocode_facilities()` — it just adds an address string and calls `mutate_geocode`:

<div><tool-call id="toolu_017v6FMXoYm9ByHngKzzUHn6"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"edit","input":{"file_path":"/Users/bjorkjcr/Dropbox (Personal)/R/ice-detention/R/geocode.R","old_string":"geocode_facilities <- function(facilities_panel) {\n  # One row per canonical facility, usi...[truncated, total 822 chars]","new_string":"geocode_facilities <- function(canonical_facilities) {\n  to_geocode <- canonical_facilitie...[truncated, total 430 chars]"},"caption":"R/geocode.R","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"@@ -7,24 +7,16 @@\n # Requires: register_google(key = Sys.getenv(\"google_maps_api_key\")) called\n # before tar_make(), or set in _targets.R.\n \n-geocode_facilities <- function(facilities_panel) {\n-  # One row per canonical facility, using the most recent year's address\n-  to_geocode <- facilities_panel |>\n-    dplyr::mutate(year_rank = match(fiscal_year, year_order)) |>\n-    dplyr::group_by(canonical_id) |>\n-    dplyr::slice_max(year_rank, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) |>\n-    dplyr::ungroup() |>\n+geocode_facilities <- function(canonical_facilities) {\n+  to_geocode <- canonical_facilities |>\n     dplyr::mutate(\n-      address_string = dplyr::case_when(\n-        !is.na(facility_address) ~\n-          paste(facility_address, facility_city, facility_state, facility_zip,\n-                \"USA\", sep = \", \"),\n-        TRUE ~\n-          paste(facility_city, facility_state, \"USA\", sep = \", \")\n+      address_string = dplyr::if_else(\n+        !is.na(facility_address),\n+        paste(facility_address, facility_city, facility_state, facility_zip,\n+              \"USA\", sep = \", \"),\n+        paste(facility_city, facility_state, \"USA\", sep = \", \")\n       )\n-    ) |>\n-    dplyr::select(canonical_id, canonical_name,\n-                  facility_city, facility_state, address_string)\n+    )\n \n   ggmap::mutate_geocode(to_geocode, address_string)\n }\n","language":"diff"}}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_017v6FMXoYm9ByHngKzzUHn6"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"text","value":"Updated /Users/bjorkjcr/Dropbox (Personal)/R/ice-detention/R/geocode.R"},"elapsedMs":169}</script></tool-result></div>



<div><tool-call id="toolu_015qAArNFVqLLekCk7yfy9cz"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"todoWrite","input":{"todos":[{"content":"Add build_canonical_facilities() function to crosswalk.R","status":"completed"},{"content":"Simplify geocode_facilities() to accept canonical_facilities directly","status":"completed"},{"content":"Update _targets.R with the new canonical_facilities target","status":"in_progress"},{"content":"Update AGENTS.md to add wiki-graph access note","status":"pending"}]},"caption":"2 done, 1 active, 1 pending"}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01HEJhx4Qf9YCiZuSpGDg13r"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"text","value":"[File: 5.0KB, 111 lines, showing lines 1-111]\n     1→# _targets.R — ICE Detention Data Pip...[truncated, total 4567 chars]"},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"[File: 5.0KB, 111 lines, showing lines 1-111]\n# _targets.R — ICE Detention Data Pipeline\n#\n# Run with: targets::tar_make()\n# Visualize: targets::tar_visnetwork()\n# Load a result: targets::tar_read(facilities_panel)\n\nlibrary(targets)\n\ntar_option_set(\n  packages = c(\"readxl\", \"dplyr\", \"stringr\", \"tidyr\", \"purrr\",\n               \"tibble\", \"httr\", \"stringdist\", \"glue\", \"ggmap\")\n)\n\n# Google Maps API registration (required for geocode_facilities target)\nggmap::register_google(key = Sys.getenv(\"google_maps_api_key\"))\n\n# Auto-source all functions in R/\ntar_source()\n\nlist(\n  # ── Metadata ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # Spreadsheet URLs, sheet names, header positions, etc.\n  tar_target(data_file_info, build_data_file_info()),\n\n  # ── Download (rarely run) ──────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # Downloads files that don't already exist locally.\n  # Set to cue = \"never\" so it only runs when you explicitly invalidate it:\n  #   tar_invalidate(raw_xlsx_files); tar_make()\n  tar_target(\n    raw_xlsx_files,\n    download_ice_spreadsheets(data_file_info),\n    format = \"file\",\n    cue = tar_cue(mode = \"never\")\n  ),\n\n  # ── Import headers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # Reads the two-row headers from each spreadsheet and produces\n  # a named list of clean variable name vectors.\n  tar_target(\n    clean_names_list,\n    build_clean_names(data_file_info)\n  ),\n\n  # ── Import raw data ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # Returns a named list of tibbles, one per fiscal year.\n  tar_target(\n    facilities_raw,\n    import_all_years(data_file_info, clean_names_list)\n  ),\n\n  # ── Clean ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # Name standardization, type coercion, Guantanamo patch.\n  tar_target(\n    facilities_clean,\n    clean_all_years(facilities_raw)\n  ),\n\n  # ── Aggregate ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # Row-level sums (ADP by classification, criminality, threat level)\n  # and facility_type_wiki classification.\n  tar_target(\n    facilities_aggregated,\n    aggregate_all_years(facilities_clean)\n  ),\n\n  # ── Facility crosswalk ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # Exact + fuzzy address matching across years → canonical facility IDs.\n  tar_target(\n    facility_crosswalk,\n    build_facility_crosswalk(facilities_aggregated)\n  ),\n\n  # ── Attach canonical IDs ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # Adds a facility_id column to the aggregated data, using the crosswalk.\n  tar_target(\n    facilities_keyed,\n    attach_canonical_ids(facilities_aggregated, facility_crosswalk)\n  ),\n\n  # ── Facility presence / trajectory ─────────────────────────────────────────\n  # For each facility, which years was it present in the data? When did it open/close?\n  tar_target(\n    facility_presence,\n    build_facility_presence(facilities_keyed)\n  ),\n\n  # ── Panel dataset ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  tar_target(\n    facilities_panel,\n    build_panel(facilities_keyed)\n  ),\n\n  # ── Geocode facilities ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # Calls Google Maps API. Set cue = \"never\" to avoid re-geocoding on each run.\n  # To re-run: targets::tar_invalidate(facilities_geocoded); tar_make()\n  # Requires google_maps_api_key in .Renviron and register_google() called first.\n  tar_target(\n    facilities_geocoded,\n    geocode_facilities(facilities_panel),\n    cue = tar_cue(mode = \"never\")\n  ),\n\n  # ── Save outputs to data/ ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # Exports RDS + CSV files for external use.\n  tar_target(\n    saved_files,\n    save_outputs(facility_crosswalk, facility_presence, facilities_panel),\n    format = \"file\"\n  )\n)\n"},"elapsedMs":80}</script></tool-result></div>



<div><tool-call id="toolu_01S642RRtJkic4ajwfW548Lt"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"edit","input":{"file_path":"/Users/bjorkjcr/Dropbox (Personal)/R/ice-detention/_targets.R","old_string":"  # ── Geocode facilities ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # Calls ...[truncated, total 433 chars]","new_string":"  # ── Canonical facility list ────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # One ro...[truncated, total 755 chars]"},"caption":"_targets.R","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"@@ -90,13 +90,21 @@\n     build_panel(facilities_keyed)\n   ),\n \n+  # ── Canonical facility list ────────────────────────────────────────────────\n+  # One row per canonical facility with most recent address columns.\n+  # This is the authoritative input for geocoding and infobox generation.\n+  tar_target(\n+    canonical_facilities,\n+    build_canonical_facilities(facilities_panel)\n+  ),\n+\n   # ── Geocode facilities ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n   # Calls Google Maps API. Set cue = \"never\" to avoid re-geocoding on each run.\n   # To re-run: targets::tar_invalidate(facilities_geocoded); tar_make()\n   # Requires google_maps_api_key in .Renviron and register_google() called first.\n   tar_target(\n     facilities_geocoded,\n-    geocode_facilities(facilities_panel),\n+    geocode_facilities(canonical_facilities),\n     cue = tar_cue(mode = \"never\")\n   ),\n \n","language":"diff"}}</script></tool-call></div>

All four changes done. To summarize what changed and why:

The coordinate misalignment was entirely due to stale session objects — the canonical_ids renumbered when `tar_make()` rebuilt the pipeline after the junk row cleanup, but the old objects were still in memory. `tar_load_everything()` fixed it.

On the design fix: `build_canonical_facilities()` now lives in `crosswalk.R` as a proper pipeline step, producing a clean 348-row table that `geocode_facilities()` accepts directly. The geocoder no longer needs to know anything about fiscal year ordering or how to deduplicate the panel — that logic is where it belongs, upstream. The pipeline dependency is also now explicit in `_targets.R`: `facilities_panel → canonical_facilities → facilities_geocoded`.

Note that since `canonical_facilities` is a new target that feeds into `facilities_geocoded` (which is `cue = "never"`), the next `tar_make()` will build `canonical_facilities` but skip re-geocoding.


<span class="suggestion">Run `tar_make()` to build the `canonical_facilities` target and verify the pipeline is consistent.</span>


<span class="suggestion">Wire `canonical_facilities` into `generate_infobox()` as well, replacing the `facilities_panel` + year-ranking logic currently in the `wikipedia.R` lookups.</span>





## User

Run tar_make() to build the canonical_facilities target and verify the pipeline is consistent.

<div><thinking id="thinking-1773193346882-uxgzy">
The `tar_make()` is being blocked because targets is trying to append to `~/.Renviron`. This is likely because the `register_google()` call in `_targets.R` is being run, and something in the ggmap package is trying to save the API key to `.Renviron`. Actually, looking at the ggmap docs - `register_google()` has a `write` parameter that if TRUE will write to `.Renviron`. By default in newer versions, it might try to write.

The issue is that the targets pipeline is running `ggmap::register_google(key = Sys.getenv("google_maps_api_key"))` at the top level of `_targets.R`, and the permission rules are blocking file writes to `~/.Renviron`.


The fix is to either call `register_google()` with `write = FALSE` explicitly, move the registration inside the `geocode_facilities()` function where it's actually needed, or remove the call from the top level of `_targets.R` entirely since the default behavior shouldn't be writing to `.Renviron` anyway. ron'" suggests that the tool execution itself is being blocked by permission rules, not by ggmap. The `runCode` tool's permission rules are preventing `tar_make()` from running because it internally tries to append to `~/.Renviron`.

Hmm, but `tar_make()` should work fine. Let me think...

Actually, I think the issue is that `ggmap::register_google()` calls something that touches `.Renviron`. Looking at ggmap source, newer versions of `register_google()` may try to save the key persistently. The `write` parameter in recent ggmap versions might default to `TRUE` for API key persistence.

The solution: move `register_google()` inside `geocode_facilities()` so it only runs when that target is explicitly triggered, rather than at the top level of `_targets.R` where it causes permission issues.
</thinking></div>

The error is from `ggmap::register_google()` running at the top level of `_targets.R` — it's trying to persist the key to `~/.Renviron`. The fix is to move the registration call inside `geocode_facilities()` where it only runs when that specific target is built.

But the question is - when would `tar_make()` try to write to `~/.Renviron`? Since `facilities_geocoded` has `cue = "never"` and already exists in the store, it shouldn't call `geocode_facilities()` and thus shouldn't call `register_google()`.

`ggmap` in the `tar_option_set(packages = ...)` list is likely what's triggering this — targets loads all listed packages in every worker process at startup, and `ggmap` apparently touches `.Renviron` on load. Since geocoding won't re-run (`cue = "never"`), remove it from the global package list:

</thinking></div>

The block seems to be a permission rule triggered by something `tar_make()` does in its subprocess environment, not specifically `ggmap`. Let me try building the target directly to verify the new function works and confirm consistency:

@param presence     facility_presence data frame\n-#' @param geocoded     facilities_geocoded data frame\n-#' @param crosswalk    facility_crosswalk data frame (used for former names)\n-#' @param wiki_matches optional data frame with wiki_slug and management columns,\n-#'                     keyed on facility_name (from facilities_fy26_wiki_matches.rds)\n+#' @param cid                  canonical_id integer\n+#' @param canonical_facilities one-row-per-facility table from build_canonical_facilities()\n+#' @param presence             facility_presence data frame\n+#' @param geocoded wiki_matches = NULL) {\n \n-  # ── Most recent panel row for this facility ────────────────────────────\n-  row <- panel |>\n-    dplyr::filter(canonical_id == cid) |>\n-    dplyr::mutate(year_rank = canonical_facilities |> dplyr::filter(canonical_id == cid)\n+  if (nrow(row) == 0) stop(\"canonical_id \", cid, \" not found in canonical_facilities\")\n \n-  if (nrow(row) == 0) stop(\"canonical_id \", cid, \" not found in panel\")\n+  # ── Most recent panel row (for ADP, capacity, facility type) ──────────\n+  if (!is.null(panel)) {\n+    panel_row <- panel |>\n+      dplyr::filter(canonical_id == cid) |>\n+      dplyr::mutate(year_rank = match(fiscal_year, year_order)) |>\n+      dplyr::slice_max(year_rank, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE)\n+  } else {\n+    panel_row <- dplyr::tibble()\n+  }\n \n+  get_panel <- function(col) {\n+    if (nrow(panel_row) == 0 || !col %in% names(panel_row)) return(NA)\n+    panel_row[[col]][1]\n+  }\n+\n   # ── Status (operational vs. closed) 1)\n","language":"diff"}}</script></tool-call></div>

All 13 data targets are in the store with valid timestamps. The pipeline is fully consistent. `canonical_facilities` slotted in cleanly between `facilities_panel` (17:27) and `facilities_geocoded` (17:28), which is exactly right chronologically.

The `generate_infobox()` signature is now cleaner — it takes `canonical_facilities` directly for identity and address fields, and `facilities_panel` optionally for measurements (ADP, capacity, facility type). The address-reduction logic lives exactly once, in `build_canonical_facilities()`.

## User

For certain data files, keeping prior versions in the archive is desirable for both reproducibility and error checking. My custom utility function for this is in the opened file "update-versioned-archive.R" 

Let's examine it and consider if we can mend the first three known issues as mentioned in the comments.


## Databot



<div><tool-call id="toolu_01EA2CnNf2RmKrGk57do4DRJ"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"read","input":{"file_path":"/Users/bjorkjcr/Dropbox (Personal)/R/ice-detention/R/update-versioned-archive.R"},"caption":"R/update-versioned-archive.R"}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01EA2CnNf2RmKrGk57do4DRJ"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"text","value":"[File: 3.1KB, 71 lines, showing lines 1-71]\n     1→# update-versioned-archive.R\n     2→# C...[truncated, total 3674 chars]"},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"[File: 3.1KB, 71 lines, showing lines 1-71]\n# update-versioned-archive.R\n# Created by Carwil Bjork-James on April 1, 2021\n#\n# This script handles archiving each version of the dataset that we import. \n# \n# Known issues: \n# 1. granularity is one archive per day \n# 2. Assumes that the most recent dated version is equal to the current version\n#    (as it should be if created by update_versioned_archive())\n# 3. Currently there is no metadata stored other than the date in the filename\n# 4. We haven't yet created any functional tools to load and verify data from \n#    these RDS files, though readr::read_rds() may be adequate.\n\n# function to add the current date to a filepath before the extension\n\nappend_current_date <- function(filename=\"filename.ext\"){\n  path <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(filename)\n  extension <- tools::file_ext(filename)\n  dated_filename <- paste(path, \"-\", Sys.Date(), \".\", extension, sep=\"\")\n}\n\n# function to check if a variable that matches its saved version,\n# and if it does not, replace the saved version and save a dated copy\n#\n# This function returns the name of the dated file, allowing it to be used\n# in the reproducibility metadata for any and all charts and reports.\n\nupdate_versioned_archive <- function(df, filename=\"data/filename.ext\", compress = \"gz\") {\n  path <- dirname(filename)\n  filename_base <- basename(filename)\n  filename_ext <- tools::file_ext(filename)\n  filename_full <- here::here(path, filename_base)\n  \n  # lowercase version of filename_ext\n  if (tolower(filename_ext) != \"rds\") {\n    warning(\"update_versioned_archive is designed to work with RDS files only.\")\n    return(paste0(filename,\" (archive unsuccessful)\"))\n  }\n    \n  if (!file.exists(filename_full)) {\n    # if there is no archive of the file yet, create a first archive\n    readr::write_rds(df, file=filename_full, compress = compress)\n    filename.dated <- append_current_date(filename_full)\n    readr::write_rds(df, file=filename.dated, compress = compress)\n  } else{\n    # otherwise do a comparison with the saved version\n    df.saved <-  readr::read_rds(filename_full)\n    if(isTRUE(dplyr::all_equal(df, df.saved))) {\n        # if the newly imported df is the same as the saved one, do nothing.\n        # \n        # and also save the filename of newest version  \n        df.filelist <- file.info(list.files(dirname(filename_full), \n                                            pattern = paste(basename(tools::file_path_sans_ext(filename_base)), \"-\", sep=\"\"), \n                                            full.names = T)) \n        filename.dated <- rownames(df.filelist)[which.max(df.filelist$mtime)]\n    }else{\n      # if the newly imported dataframe is different, overwrite the current file\n      readr::write_rds(df, file=filename_full, compress = compress)\n      # and also save a copy with today's date\n      filename.dated <- append_current_date(filename_full)\n      readr::write_rds(df, file=filename.dated, compress = compress) \n    }\n    rm(df.saved) # and remove the old copy from memory\n  }\n  filename_base.dated <- basename(filename.dated)\n  filename.dated <- paste0(path, .Platform$file.sep, filename_base.dated)\n  \n  return(filename.dated)\n}\n\n"},"elapsedMs":40}</script></tool-result></div>



<div><thinking id="thinking-1773194302056-sgw1o">
Let me analyze the three known issues that can be fixed:

1. **Granularity is one archive per day** — if you call `update_versioned_archive()` twice on the same day with different data, the second call overwrites the dated file. The fix would be to add a timestamp (or a sequence suffix) to ensure uniqueness within a day.

2. **Assumes that the most recent dated version is equal to the current version** — the logic when the files are equal finds the newest dated archive by `mtime` and returns it. But this assumption could break if files were modified externally. A more robust check would compare the actual content of the undated file against the newest dated archive. However, this could also be addressed by storing a hash/checksum.


3. **No metadata stored beyond the filename date** — I could write a companion metadata file alongside each archive to capture details like creator, creation timestamp, dimensions, and other relevant information.

4. **No tools to load and verify archives** — this fourth issue hasn't been addressed yet.

For the first issue, the core problem is that calling the function twice in the same day with different data causes the second call to overwrite the first, since the date-based filename remains the same.

The fix is straightforward: check if the dated file already exists, and if it does, append a counter suffix like `-2`, `-3`, etc. to create a unique filename. This way, multiple archives from the same day won't collide.

Looking at the actual flow, I realize the function already has some logic to handle this — it checks if the undated file exists and whether the data has changed — but the granularity issue still surfaces when running it multiple times in a single day with different data.

There's also a deeper assumption baked in: when the data hasn't changed, the code finds the most recent dated file by modification time and assumes it matches the current undated file. But if someone manually edits the undated file, that assumption breaks down. I should probably verify the undated file's content against the newest dated archive using a hash or checksum to ensure they're actually in sync. , a simpler fix for issue 2: when we determine data is the same as the undated file, also verify the undated file matches the newest dated archive before returning the dated filename. If they don't match, treat it as changed.

But actually, the simplest fix: the "no change" path should compare to the *newest dated file* rather than (or in addition to) the undated file. That would eliminate the assumption.

Wait, I think the existing behavior is actually reasonable: the undated file IS the canonical "current" file. The dated files are archives. The function compares incoming `df` against the undated current, which is correct. The issue is: what if someone manually modified the undated file without going through this function? Then the undated file doesn't match any dated archive.

A pragmatic fix: in the "no change" branch, verify the undated file content actually matches the dated file we're about to return—if not, archive the undated file with today's date first.

For issue 3, I should store richer metadata alongside each archive: timestamp, row/column counts, column names, file hash, and maybe R version info in a companion JSON file.

For issue 1, I need to switch from date-only to datetime granularity using a format like `%Y-%m-%d_%H%M%S`.

Writing the fixed version now... Writing the archive functions... Still writing archive logic... Writing the archive logic...

Now I'm realizing I need to handle the function naming carefully — `append_current_date()` is the original function for backward compatibility, but I'm using `append_current_datetime()` in the new code, so I should either rename everything consistently or keep both. Since this utility function is only called internally within `update_versioned_archive()`, I can safely rename it. I also need to replace the deprecated `dplyr::all_equal()` call with `all.equal()` from base R. I need to switch from the deprecated `dplyr::all_equal()` to `isTRUE(all.equal())` for the comparison, and I should also add metadata tracking to distinguish between first archives, new versions, and undated drift corrections.
</thinking></div>

The three fixable issues are:

1. **Granularity**: `append_current_date()` uses only the date — two runs on the same day with different data overwrite the same dated archive. Fix: use a datetime stamp.
2. **Stale undated assumption**: in the "no change" branch, it finds the newest dated file by `mtime` and returns it, but never checks that the undated file actually matches that archive (a manual edit would silently break the chain). Fix: compare undated file against newest dated archive; re-archive if they've drifted.
3. **No metadata beyond date**: the archive carries no structural information. Fix: write a sidecar `.txt` alongside each new dated archive with row/column counts, column names, and an MD5 hash.

There's also a fourth small thing worth fixing while we're here: `dplyr::all_equal()` is deprecated — the replacement is base `all.equal()`.

<div><tool-call id="toolu_01L3hjsQatiA3r14j5wTs6iR"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"write","input":{"file_path":"/Users/bjorkjcr/Dropbox (Personal)/R/ice-detention/R/update-versioned-archive.R","content":"# update-versioned-archive.R\n# Created by Carwil Bjork-James on April 1, 2021\n#\n# This scr...[truncated, total 5185 chars]","overwrite":true},"caption":"R/update-versioned-archive.R","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"# update-versioned-archive.R\n# Created by Carwil Bjork-James on April 1, 2021\n#\n# This script handles archiving each version of the dataset that we import.\n#\n# Known issues:\n# 1. [FIXED] Granularity is one archive per day — upgraded to datetime (HH:MM:SS),\n#    so multiple updates on the same day each get a distinct archive file.\n# 2. [FIXED] Assumes that the most recent dated version is equal to the current\n#    version. Now verified explicitly: if the undated file has drifted from its\n#    newest dated archive (e.g. manual edit), the undated file is re-archived.\n# 3. [FIXED] No metadata stored other than the date. A sidecar .txt file is now\n#    written alongside each new dated archive with timestamp, dimensions, column\n#    names, and MD5 hash.\n# 4. We haven't yet created any functional tools to load and verify data from\n#    these RDS files, though readr::read_rds() may be adequate.\n\n# ── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n# Inserts a datetime stamp into a filepath before the extension.\n# e.g. \"data/panel.rds\" → \"data/panel-2026-03-10_214501.rds\"\nappend_current_datetime <- function(filename = \"filename.ext\") {\n  path      <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(filename)\n  extension <- tools::file_ext(filename)\n  stamp     <- format(Sys.time(), \"%Y-%m-%d_%H%M%S\")\n  paste0(path, \"-\", stamp, \".\", extension)\n}\n\n# Writes a plain-text sidecar metadata file alongside a newly dated archive.\nwrite_archive_metadata <- function(df, filename_dated) {\n  meta_path <- paste0(tools::file_path_sans_ext(filename_dated), \".txt\")\n  lines <- c(\n    paste(\"Archived:    \", format(Sys.time(), \"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z\")),\n    paste(\"File:        \", basename(filename_dated)),\n    paste(\"Rows:        \", nrow(df)),\n    paste(\"Columns:     \", ncol(df)),\n    paste(\"Column names:\", paste(names(df), collapse = \", \")),\n    paste(\"MD5:         \", tools::md5sum(filename_dated))\n  )\n  writeLines(lines, meta_path)\n  invisible(meta_path)\n}\n\n# ── Main function ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n# Checks if df differs from its saved version, and if so, overwrites the\n# canonical file and saves a new dated archive with a sidecar metadata file.\n#\n# Returns the path to the dated archive file (relative to project root),\n# suitable for use in reproducibility metadata.\n\nupdate_versioned_archive <- function(df, filename = \"data/filename.ext\",\n                                     compress = \"gz\") {\n  path          <- dirname(filename)\n  filename_base <- basename(filename)\n  filename_ext  <- tools::file_ext(filename)\n  filename_full <- here::here(path, filename_base)\n\n  if (tolower(filename_ext) != \"rds\") {\n    warning(\"update_versioned_archive is designed to work with RDS files only.\")\n    return(paste0(filename, \" (archive unsuccessful)\"))\n  }\n\n  # Write undated + dated archive, plus metadata sidecar. Returns dated path.\n  archive_new <- function(df) {\n    readr::write_rds(df, file = filename_full, compress = compress)\n    filename_dated <- append_current_datetime(filename_full)\n    readr::write_rds(df, file = filename_dated, compress = compress)\n    write_archive_metadata(df, filename_dated)\n    filename_dated\n  }\n\n  # Find existing dated .rds archives for this file (not sidecar .txt files).\n  find_dated_archives <- function() {\n    stem  <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(filename_base)\n    files <- list.files(\n      dirname(filename_full),\n      pattern    = paste0(\"^\", stem, \"-\\\\d{4}-\\\\d{2}-\\\\d{2}\"),\n      full.names = TRUE\n    )\n    files[tools::file_ext(files) == \"rds\"]\n  }\n\n  if (!file.exists(filename_full)) {\n    # No file yet — create the first archive.\n    filename_dated <- archive_new(df)\n\n  } else {\n    df_saved <- readr::read_rds(filename_full)\n\n    if (isTRUE(all.equal(df, df_saved))) {\n      # Data matches the undated file. Now verify the undated file also matches\n      # its newest dated archive (guards against manual edits — issue 2).\n      dated <- find_dated_archives()\n\n      if (length(dated) == 0) {\n        # Undated file exists but has no dated archive — create one now.\n        filename_dated <- append_current_datetime(filename_full)\n        readr::write_rds(df, file = filename_dated, compress = compress)\n        write_archive_metadata(df, filename_dated)\n\n      } else {\n        newest_dated <- dated[which.max(file.info(dated)$mtime)]\n        df_newest    <- readr::read_rds(newest_dated)\n\n        if (!isTRUE(all.equal(df_saved, df_newest))) {\n          # Undated file has drifted from its newest archive — re-archive it.\n          filename_dated <- append_current_datetime(filename_full)\n          readr::write_rds(df, file = filename_dated, compress = compress)\n          write_archive_metadata(df, filename_dated)\n        } else {\n          # Everything consistent — return the existing newest dated archive.\n          filename_dated <- newest_dated\n        }\n        rm(df_newest)\n      }\n\n    } else {\n      # Data changed — overwrite undated file and create new dated archive.\n      filename_dated <- archive_new(df)\n    }\n\n    rm(df_saved)\n  }\n\n  # Return path relative to project root (consistent with input convention).\n  file.path(path, basename(filename_dated))\n}\n"}}</script></tool-call></div>

The third call returned the wrong file — all three runs happened within the same second, so `archive_new()` for `df2` generated the identical datetime filename as the first call and silently overwrote it. The datetime stamp needs a collision guard:

<div><tool-call id="toolu_01JEyyxTWhVNKh6sDZLP8fYt"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"edit","input":{"file_path":"/Users/bjorkjcr/Dropbox (Personal)/R/ice-detention/R/update-versioned-archive.R","old_string":"# Inserts a datetime stamp into a filepath before the extension.\n# e.g. \"data/panel.rds\" →...[truncated, total 380 chars]","new_string":"# Inserts a datetime stamp into a filepath before the extension, ensuring the\n# result is ...[truncated, total 693 chars]"},"caption":"R/update-versioned-archive.R","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"@@ -17,13 +17,21 @@\n \n # ── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n \n-# Inserts a datetime stamp into a filepath before the extension.\n+# Inserts a datetime stamp into a filepath before the extension, ensuring the\n+# result is a filename that does not already exist. If the plain timestamped\n+# name is taken (two calls in the same second), appends -2, -3, etc.\n # e.g. \"data/panel.rds\" → \"data/panel-2026-03-10_214501.rds\"\n append_current_datetime <- function(filename = \"filename.ext\") {\n   path      <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(filename)\n   extension <- tools::file_ext(filename)\n   stamp     <- format(Sys.time(), \"%Y-%m-%d_%H%M%S\")\n-  paste0(path, \"-\", stamp, \".\", extension)\n+  candidate <- paste0(path, \"-\", stamp, \".\", extension)\n+  i <- 2\n+  while (file.exists(candidate)) {\n+    candidate <- paste0(path, \"-\", stamp, \"-\", i, \".\", extension)\n+    i <- i + 1\n+  }\n+  candidate\n }\n \n # Writes a plain-text sidecar metadata file alongside a newly dated archive.\n","language":"diff"}}</script></tool-call></div>

All four cases work correctly:

- **Call 1**: new file → creates undated + dated archive + metadata sidecar
- **Call 2**: same data → returns existing dated archive (no write)
- **Call 3**: changed data → creates new dated archive + sidecar; different timestamp from call 1 even though both happened within a second apart
- **Call 4**: same as call 3's data → correctly returns call 3's archive

Clean up the test directory:

<div><tool-call id="toolu_01KcDsP6Qq7pfdNuqFFXaioy"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"runCode","input":{"code":"\nunlink(here::here(\"data/archive-test\"), recursive = TRUE)\n","intent":"Clean up test directory"},"intent":"Clean up test directory","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"\nunlink(here::here(\"data/archive-test\"), recursive = TRUE)\n","language":"r"}}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01KcDsP6Qq7pfdNuqFFXaioy"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"content","value":[{"type":"text","text":"Execution time: 36ms"}]},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":""},"elapsedMs":126}</script></tool-result></div>

All three issues are resolved, plus the `dplyr::all_equal()` deprecation. The key changes:

| Issue | Fix |
|---|---|
| Day-level granularity | `append_current_datetime()` uses `%Y-%m-%d_%H%M%S`; collision guard appends `-2`, `-3`, etc. if needed |
| Stale undated assumption | "no change" branch now loads the newest dated archive and compares it to the undated file; re-archives if they've drifted |
| No metadata | `write_archive_metadata()` writes a `.txt` sidecar with timestamp, rows, columns, column names, and MD5 hash |
| `dplyr::all_equal()` deprecated | Replaced with base `all.equal()` throughout |


<span class="suggestion">Add `update_versioned_archive` to `save_outputs()` so the panel, crosswalk, and presence data are automatically versioned each time the pipeline runs.</span>

<div><tool-call id="toolu_01Q82U9s1WzS1jKgDADiHpiH"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"edit","input":{"file_path":"/Users/bjorkjcr/Dropbox (Personal)/R/ice-detention/R/update-versioned-archive.R","old_string":"  stamp     <- format(Sys.time(), \"%Y-%m-%d_%H%M%S\")\n  candidate <- paste0(path, \"-\", stam...[truncated, total 237 chars]","new_string":"  stamp     <- format(Sys.time(), \"%Y-%m-%d-%Hh\")\n  candidate <- paste0(path, \"-\", stamp, ...[truncated, total 265 chars]"},"caption":"R/update-versioned-archive.R","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"@@ -24,11 +24,12 @@\n append_current_datetime <- function(filename = \"filename.ext\") {\n   path      <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(filename)\n   extension <- tools::file_ext(filename)\n-  stamp     <- format(Sys.time(), \"%Y-%m-%d_%H%M%S\")\n+  stamp     <- format(Sys.time(), \"%Y-%m-%d-%Hh\")\n   candidate <- paste0(path, \"-\", stamp, \".\", extension)\n-  i <- 2\n+  suffix    <- letters\n+  i <- 1\n   while (file.exists(candidate)) {\n-    candidate <- paste0(path, \"-\", stamp, \"-\", i, \".\", extension)\n+    candidate <- paste0(path, \"-\", stamp, \"-\", suffix[i], \".\", extension)\n     i <- i + 1\n   }\n   candidate\n","language":"diff"}}</script></tool-call></div>

Also update the example in the comment above:

<div><tool-call id="toolu_01Bkjb4mexLgYMwkn85eFsRg"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"edit","input":{"file_path":"/Users/bjorkjcr/Dropbox (Personal)/R/ice-detention/R/update-versioned-archive.R","old_string":"# e.g. \"data/panel.rds\" → \"data/panel-2026-03-10_214501.rds\"","new_string":"# e.g. \"data/panel.rds\" → \"data/panel-2026-03-10-21h.rds\"\n#       or   \"data/panel.rds\" → ...[truncated, total 135 chars]"},"caption":"R/update-versioned-archive.R","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"@@ -20,7 +20,8 @@\n # Inserts a datetime stamp into a filepath before the extension, ensuring the\n # result is a filename that does not already exist. If the plain timestamped\n # name is taken (two calls in the same second), appends -2, -3, etc.\n-# e.g. \"data/panel.rds\" → \"data/panel-2026-03-10_214501.rds\"\n+# e.g. \"data/panel.rds\" → \"data/panel-2026-03-10-21h.rds\"\n+#       or   \"data/panel.rds\" → \"data/panel-2026-03-10-21h-b.rds\" (collision)\n append_current_datetime <- function(filename = \"filename.ext\") {\n   path      <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(filename)\n   extension <- tools::file_ext(filename)\n","language":"diff"}}</script></tool-call></div>

igraph::layout_with_sugiyama(g)$layout\ncolnames(layout) <- c(\"x\", \"y\")\n\nvdf <- as.data.frame(layout)\nvdf$name   <- igraph::V(g)$name\nvdf$status <- igraph::V(g)$status\n\nedf <- igraph::as_data_frame(g, what = \"edges\")\nedf <- merge(edf, vdf[, c(\"name\",\"x\",\"y\")], by.x = \"from\", by.y = \"name\")\nedf <- merge(edf, vdf[, c(\"name\",\"x\",\"y\")], by.x = \"to\",   by.y = \"name\",\n             suffixes = c(\"_from\",\"_to\"))\n\nstatus_colors <- c(\n  \"uptodate\" = \"#2196a5\",\n  \"outdated\"  = \"#d95f02\",\n  \"skipped\"   = \"#aaaaaa\",\n  \"built\"     = \"#1b9e77\"\n)\n\nggplot() +\n  geom_segment(data = edf,\n               aes(x = x_from, y = y_from, xend = x_to, yend = y_to),\n               arrow = arrow(length = unit(6, \"pt\"), type = \"closed\"),\n               color = \"grey60\", linewidth = 0.5) +\n  geom_point(data = vdf,\n             aes(x, y, fill = status),\n             shape = 21, size = 9, color = \"white\", stroke = 0.5) +\n  geom_text(data = vdf,\n            aes(x, y, label = name),\n            size = 2.6, hjust = -0.15, vjust = 0.4) +\n  scale_fill_manual(values = status_colors, na.value = \"grey80\") +\n  coord_cartesian(clip = \"off\") +\n  theme_void() +\n  theme(legend.position  = \"bottom\",\n        plot.margin      = margin(10, 120, 10, 10)) +\n  labs(fill = \"Status\", title = \"ICE Detention <div><tool-call id="toolu_016DekzWieXFjQFAggRH3oNn"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"runCode","input":{"code":"\nlibrary(ggplot2)\nlibrary(igraph)\nlibrary(dplyr)\n\nedges <- tibble::tribble(\n  ~from,      ...[truncated, total 3259 chars]","intent":"Plot the dependency graph using igraph layout and ggplot2 without ggraph"},"intent":"Plot the dependency graph using igraph layout and ggplot2 without ggraph","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"\nlibrary(ggplot2)\nlibrary(igraph)\nlibrary(dplyr)\n\nedges <- tibble::tribble(\n  ~from,                    ~to,\n  \"data_file_info\",         \"clean_names_list\",\n  \"data_file_info\",         \"raw_xlsx_files\",\n  \"data_file_info\",         \"facilities_raw\",\n  \"clean_names_list\",       \"facilities_raw\",\n  \"raw_xlsx_files\",         \"facilities_raw\",\n  \"facilities_raw\",         \"facilities_clean\",\n  \"facilities_clean\",       \"facilities_aggregated\",\n  \"facilities_aggregated\",  \"facility_crosswalk\",\n  \"facilities_aggregated\",  \"facilities_keyed\",\n  \"facility_crosswalk\",     \"facilities_keyed\",\n  \"facilities_keyed\",       \"facility_presence\",\n  \"facilities_keyed\",       \"facilities_panel\",\n  \"facilities_panel\",       \"canonical_facilities\",\n  \"facilities_panel\",       \"saved_files\",\n  \"canonical_facilities\",   \"facilities_geocoded\",\n  \"facility_crosswalk\",     \"saved_files\",\n  \"facility_presence\",      \"saved_files\"\n)\n\nnode_meta <- tibble::tribble(\n  ~name,                    ~stage,\n  \"data_file_info\",         \"Source\",\n  \"raw_xlsx_files\",         \"File\",\n  \"clean_names_list\",       \"Import\",\n  \"facilities_raw\",         \"Import\",\n  \"facilities_clean\",       \"Clean\",\n  \"facilities_aggregated\",  \"Clean\",\n  \"facility_crosswalk\",     \"Crosswalk\",\n  \"facilities_keyed\",       \"Crosswalk\",\n  \"canonical_facilities\",   \"Crosswalk\",\n  \"facility_presence\",      \"Crosswalk\",\n  \"facilities_panel\",       \"Crosswalk\",\n  \"facilities_geocoded\",    \"Geocode\",\n  \"saved_files\",            \"Output\"\n)\n\ng   <- igraph::graph_from_data_frame(edges, directed = TRUE, vertices = node_meta)\nlay <- igraph::layout_with_sugiyama(g)$layout\n\nvdf <- as.data.frame(lay) |>\n  setNames(c(\"x\", \"y\")) |>\n  mutate(name  = igraph::V(g)$name,\n         stage = igraph::V(g)$stage)\n\nedf <- igraph::as_data_frame(g, what = \"edges\") |>\n  left_join(vdf |> select(name, x, y), by = c(\"from\" = \"name\")) |>\n  left_join(vdf |> select(name, x, y), by = c(\"to\"   = \"name\"),\n            suffix = c(\"_from\", \"_to\"))\n\nstage_colors <- c(\n  \"Source\"    = \"#78909C\",\n  \"File\"      = \"#66BB6A\",\n  \"Import\"    = \"#42A5F5\",\n  \"Clean\"     = \"#FFA726\",\n  \"Crosswalk\" = \"#AB47BC\",\n  \"Geocode\"   = \"#EF5350\",\n  \"Output\"    = \"#26A69A\"\n)\n\n# nudge labels right; flip for nodes near the right edge\nvdf <- vdf |> mutate(hjust = if_else(x > max(x) - 0.5, 1.5, -0.15))\n\nggplot() +\n  geom_segment(\n    data = edf,\n    aes(x = x_from, y = y_from, xend = x_to, yend = y_to),\n    arrow     = arrow(length = unit(6, \"pt\"), type = \"closed\"),\n    color     = \"grey60\",\n    linewidth = 0.5\n  ) +\n  geom_point(\n    data  = vdf,\n    aes(x, y, fill = stage),\n    shape = 21, size = 11, color = \"white\", stroke = 0.8\n  ) +\n  geom_text(\n    data = vdf,\n    aes(x, y, label = stringr::str_replace_all(name, \"_\", \"\\n\"),\n        hjust = hjust),\n    size       = 2.3,\n    lineheight = 0.85,\n    nudge_x    = 0.07\n  ) +\n  scale_fill_manual(values = stage_colors) +\n  coord_cartesian(clip = \"off\") +\n  theme_void() +\n  theme(\n    legend.position = \"bottom\",\n    legend.title    = element_text(size = 9, face = \"bold\"),\n    plot.title      = element_text(size = 12, face = \"bold\", margin = margin(b = 6)),\n    plot.margin     = margin(10, 100, 10, 10)\n  ) +\n  labs(fill = \"Stage\", title = \"ICE Detention Pipeline — Target Dependencies\")\n","language":"r"}}</script></tool-call></div>

The graph renders correctly. The pipeline flows clearly from `data_file_info` (source) down through import → clean → crosswalk stages, with the geocode branch and `saved_files` output visibly fed from multiple upstream targets.

The label collisions on the right side (`facilities_panel` / `canonical_facilities`) are a minor layout artifact from the sugiyama algorithm placing them close together. If you'd like a cleaner layout those two could be forced apart, but the structure is readable as-is.


<span class="suggestion">Install `ggraph` (`renv::install("ggraph")`) for a more polished version — it handles edge routing and label placement automatically.</span>


<span class="suggestion">Add `renv::snapshot()` to capture the packages installed this session (igraph, sf, mapview, etc.) into the lockfile.</span>

Here's the full picture across the template and the two example pages.

**What existing detention center articles actually use** is quite sparse. Stewart has only 6 fields: `location`, `status`, `classification`, `capacity`, `opened`, `managed_by`. T. Don Hutto has no infobox at all — just a coordinate header. So the bar for "new" fields is low.

---

## Template fields mapped to our data

| Template field | In our data? | Source | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `name` | ✓ | `canonical_name` | Already implemented |
| `location` | ✓ | city + state | Already implemented |
| `coordinates` | ✓ | `facilities_geocoded` | Already implemented |
| `pushpin_map` | ✓ | state lookup | Already implemented |
| `status` | ✓ derived | `facility_presence` | Already implemented |
| `classification` | ✓ | `facility_type_wiki` | Repurposing; see note below |
| `capacity` | ✓ | `inspections_guaranteed_minimum` | Already implemented |
| `population` | ✓ | `sum_criminality_levels` | Already implemented |
| `population_as_of` | ✓ | fiscal year | Already implemented; **not used on existing pages** |
| `opened` | ✓ partial | `first_seen` | Already implemented; **not used on Stewart** |
| `closed` | ✓ partial | `last_seen` | Already implemented |
| `former_name` | ✓ derived | crosswalk | Already implemented; **not used on existing pages** |
| `managed_by` | ✓ partial | contractors patch | Already implemented (join needs fix) |
| `street-address` | ✓ | `facility_address` | Already implemented |
| `city` | ✓ | `facility_city` | Already implemented |
| **`county`** | **derivable** | **reverse geocode** | **Not on any existing page; standard field** |
| `state` | ✓ | `facility_state` | Already implemented |
| `zip` | ✓ | `facility_zip` | Already implemented |
| `country` | ✓ hardcoded | "USA" | Already implemented |
| `director`/`warden` | ✗ | — | Not in our data |
| `website` | ✗ | — | Not in our data |
| `prisoners` | ✗ | — | Not applicable |

---

## Fields we have with no template parameter

These are the more interesting findings — data we have that existing infoboxes don't capture at all:

| Our variable | Description | How to include |
|---|---|---|
| `facility_average_length_of_stay_alos` | Average length of stay (days) | Free text in article, or custom row via `embedded` |
| `inspections_last_inspection_type` | Inspection type (ODO, ERO, PREA…) | Same — distinctive & sourceable |
| `inspections_last_inspection_standard` | Inspection standard (PBNDS 2011, etc.) | Same |
| `inspections_last_final_rating` | Final rating (Acceptable, Deficient…) | Same — high editorial value |
| `inspections_last_inspection_end_date` | Date of last inspection | Same |
| `facility_male_female` | Population gender composition | Could map loosely to a note or `embedded` |
| `facility_aor` | ICE Area of Responsibility | Useful for categorization; no template field |
| `facility_type_detailed` | Raw ICE type code (IGSA, DIGSA, etc.) | Could supplement `classification` |
| `share_non_crim` / `share_no_threat` | Derived proportions | Narrative use only |

---

## The `county` field

This is the most actionable gap. It's a standard template field, not used on any existing detention center page despite often being mentioned in article text (Stewart's article says "Stewart County, Georgia" but the infobox omits it). We already have coordinates in `facilities_geocoded` — we could reverse-geocode them via the Google Maps API to retrieve county names, giving us a clean `county` field for all 348 facilities.

## The `classification` repurposing

Stewart uses `classification = "medium"` in the security-level sense (the intended use). We're using it for facility type ("Jail", "Private Migrant Detention Center"). This is a reasonable repurposing for the immigration detention context — a "medium security" label is less meaningful here than the contractual/legal type — but it's worth noting the tension with the template's intent.

## Inspection data

The inspection fields are arguably the most distinctive contribution we could make to these articles. No existing detention center infobox includes inspection ratings. The most practical path is an `{{embedded}}` sub-infobox or a standardized footnote block. Since the template doesn't have native fields for this, a small custom approach would be needed.


<span class="suggestion">Add reverse geocoding for county names using the Google Maps reverse geocode API, then wire `county` into `generate_infobox()`.</span>


<span class="suggestion">Design an `embedded` inspection sub-infobox using `{{infobox}}` directly, formatted to sit inside the prison infobox's `embedded` parameter, covering inspection type, standard, rating, and date.</span>


<span class="suggestion">Revisit the `classification` field — consider using it for the ICE type code (`facility_type_detailed`) and moving the readable type label to the article text, to stay closer to the template's intent.</span>

<div><tool-call id="toolu_01WrYSbcmUzt9p6aTbixKrKG"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"runCode","input":{"code":"\nlibrary(dplyr)\n\n# Aggregate by fiscal year\nadp_table <- facilities_panel |>\n  group_by(fi...[truncated, total 827 chars]","intent":"Compute nationwide ADP aggregates by fiscal year for all five rows"},"intent":"Compute nationwide ADP aggregates by fiscal year for all five rows","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"\nlibrary(dplyr)\n\n# Aggregate by fiscal year\nadp_table <- facilities_panel |>\n  group_by(fiscal_year) |>\n  summarise(\n    total        = sum(sum_criminality_levels,             na.rm = TRUE),\n    with_crim    = sum(adp_criminality_male_crim +\n                       adp_criminality_female_crim,        na.rm = TRUE),\n    no_crim      = sum(adp_criminality_male_non_crim +\n                       adp_criminality_female_non_crim,    na.rm = TRUE),\n    male_total   = sum(adp_criminality_male_crim +\n                       adp_criminality_male_non_crim,      na.rm = TRUE),\n    female_total = sum(adp_criminality_female_crim +\n                       adp_criminality_female_non_crim,    na.rm = TRUE),\n    .groups = \"drop\"\n  ) |>\n  mutate(fiscal_year = factor(fiscal_year, levels = year_order)) |>\n  arrange(fiscal_year)\n\nadp_table\n","language":"r"}}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01WrYSbcmUzt9p6aTbixKrKG"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"content","value":[{"type":"text","text":"Output:\n# A tibble: 8 × 6\n  fiscal_year  total with_crim no_crim male_total female_total\n ...[truncated, total 679 chars]"}]},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"# A tibble: 8 × 6\n  fiscal_year  total with_crim no_crim male_total female_total\n  <fct>        <dbl>     <dbl>   <dbl>      <dbl>        <dbl>\n1 FY19        49141.    19309.  29832.     41667.        7474.\n2 FY20        32979.    14197.  18781.     28129.        4850 \n3 FY21        18291.     5761.  12530.     15062.        3228.\n4 FY22        21343.     4918.  16425.     18068.        3275.\n5 FY23        28148.     7934.  20214.     24071.        4077.\n6 FY24        36277.     9899.  26378.     31565.        4712.\n7 FY25        47550.    14160.  33390.     41482.        6068.\n8 FY26        66812.    14965.  51847.     58548.        8264."},"elapsedMs":370}</script></tool-result></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01UajX7Q8QaYa2VdcJF1MXC4"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"content","value":[{"type":"text","text":"Output:\nRows: 8\nColumns: 10\n$ year_name         <chr> \"FY19\", \"FY20\", \"FY21\", \"FY22\", \"FY2...[truncated, total 792 chars]"}]},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"Rows: 8\nColumns: 10\n$ year_name         <chr> \"FY19\", \"FY20\", \"FY21\", \"FY22\", \"FY23\", \"FY24\", \"FY25\", \"FY26\"\n$ year              <int> 2019, 2020, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025, 2026\n$ url               <chr> \"https://www.ice.gov/doclib/detention/FY19-detentionstats.xlsx\", \"https:…\n$ local_file        <chr> \"data/FY19-detentionstats.xlsx\", \"data/FY20-detentionstats.xlsx\", \"data/…\n$ sheet_name        <chr> \"Facilities FY19\", \"Facilities EOYFY20 \", \"Facilities FY21 YTD\", \"Facili…\n$ right_column      <chr> \"AE\", \"AE\", \"AE\", \"AD\", \"AG\", \"AB\", \"AB\", \"AA\"\n$ first_header_row  <dbl> 5, 5, 5, 5, 4, 5, 5, 9\n$ second_header_row <dbl> 7, 7, 7, 7, 6, 7, 7, 10\n$ first_data_row    <dbl> 8, 8, 8, 8, 7, 8, 8, 11\n$ right_column_num  <int> 31, 31, 31, 30, 33, 28, 28, 27"},"elapsedMs":197}</script></tool-result></div>

Good — all 8 fiscal years have both male and female detainees, so those rows show for every column. Now I'll build the wikitext and the RIS file.

<div><tool-call id="toolu_01R1HzY6HVzbs4rwFcWrfM3h"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"runCode","input":{"code":"\nlibrary(dplyr)\nlibrary(glue)\n\n# ── 1. Format numbers with commas ────────────────────────...[truncated, total 1001 chars]","intent":"Reshape and format aggregate data for wikitext table construction"},"intent":"Reshape and format aggregate data for wikitext table construction","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"\nlibrary(dplyr)\nlibrary(glue)\n\n# ── 1. Format numbers with commas ──────────────────────────────────────────\nfmt <- function(x) formatC(round(x), format = \"d\", big.mark = \",\")\n\n# Partial-year markers (FY21 sheet says YTD; FY25/FY26 have mid-year dates)\nytd_years <- c(\"FY21\", \"FY25\", \"FY26\")\n\n# ── 2. Build column header labels (FY 20XX format + ref placeholder) ────────\ncol_meta <- data_file_info |>\n  mutate(\n    fy_label = paste(\"FY\", year),\n    ref_name = paste0(\"ice-detention-\", tolower(year_name)),\n    header   = paste0(\"FY\\u00a0\", year,\n                      if_else(year_name %in% ytd_years, \"{{efn|YTD snapshot}}\", \"\"),\n                      '<ref name=\"', ref_name, '\"/>')\n  )\n\n# ── 3. Pivot table to wide format (one column per FY) ──────────────────────\nwide <- adp_table |>\n  mutate(across(total:female_total, fmt)) |>\n  mutate(fiscal_year = as.character(fiscal_year)) |>\n  tidyr::pivot_longer(-fiscal_year) |>\n  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = fiscal_year, values_from = value)\n\nwide\n","language":"r"}}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01R1HzY6HVzbs4rwFcWrfM3h"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"content","value":[{"type":"text","text":"Output:\n# A tibble: 5 × 9\n  name         FY19   FY20   FY21   FY22   FY23   FY24   FY25   ...[truncated, total 546 chars]"}]},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"# A tibble: 5 × 9\n  name         FY19   FY20   FY21   FY22   FY23   FY24   FY25   FY26  \n  <chr>        <chr>  <chr>  <chr>  <chr>  <chr>  <chr>  <chr>  <chr> \n1 total        49,141 32,979 18,291 21,343 28,148 36,277 47,550 66,812\n2 with_crim    19,309 14,197 5,761  4,918  7,934  9,899  14,160 14,965\n3 no_crim      29,832 18,781 12,530 16,425 20,214 26,378 33,390 51,847\n4 male_total   41,667 28,129 15,062 18,068 24,071 31,565 41,482 58,548\n5 female_total 7,474  4,850  3,228  3,275  4,077  4,712  6,068  8,264"},"elapsedMs":772}</script></tool-result></div>



<div><tool-call id="toolu_012eWdQpNGZ6aMeAi5U6ehns"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"runCode","input":{"code":"\n# ── 4. Build the wikitext ────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nheader_c...[truncated, total 1579 chars]","intent":"Generate the horizontal MediaWiki wikitable with placeholde ref names"},"intent":"Generate the horizontal MediaWiki wikitable with placeholde ref names","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"\n# ── 4. Build the wikitext ────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nheader_cells <- paste(col_meta$header, collapse = \" !! \")\n\ndata_row <- function(label, key, df, col_meta, show_if_both = FALSE) {\n  fys <- col_meta$year_name\n  cells <- if (show_if_both) {\n    # Suppress cell if female or male is zero (future-proof)\n    male_row   <- adp_table |> pull(male_total)\n    female_row <- adp_table |> pull(female_total)\n    purrr::map2_chr(df[[key]], seq_along(fys), \\(val, i) {\n      if (male_row[i] == 0 || female_row[i] == 0) \"—\" else val\n    })\n  } else {\n    df[[key]]\n  }\n  paste0(\"| style=\\\"text-align:left\\\" | \", label, \"\\n\",\n         paste0(\"| \", cells, collapse = \"\\n\"))\n}\n\nrows <- list(\n  data_row(\"Total detainee population\",      \"total\",        wide, col_meta),\n  data_row(\"…\\u00a0with a criminal record\",  \"with_crim\",    wide, col_meta),\n  data_row(\"…\\u00a0with no criminal record\", \"no_crim\",      wide, col_meta),\n  data_row(\"Male detainees\",                 \"male_total\",   wide, col_meta, show_if_both = TRUE),\n  data_row(\"Female detainees\",               \"female_total\", wide, col_meta, show_if_both = TRUE)\n)\n\nwikitext <- paste0(\n  '{| class=\"wikitable\" style=\"text-align:right\"\\n',\n  '|+ Average daily detainee population in ICE custody, FY\\u00a02019\\u2013FY\\u00a02026\\n',\n  \"|-\\n\",\n  '! style=\"text-align:left\" | Fiscal year',\n  \" !! \", header_cells, \"\\n\",\n  paste(purrr::map_chr(rows, \\(r) paste0(\"|-\\n\", r)), collapse = \"\\n\"),\n  \"\\n|-\\n\",\n  '| colspan=\"9\" style=\"text-align:left;font-size:90%\" | ',\n  '{{notelist}}\\n',\n  \"|}\"\n)\n\ncat(wikitext)\n","language":"r"}}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_012eWdQpNGZ6aMeAi5U6ehns"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"content","value":[{"type":"text","text":"Output:\nError in `purrr::map2_chr()`:\n! Can't recycle `.x` (size 0) to match `.y` (size 8)...[truncated, total 254 chars]"}]},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"Error in `purrr::map2_chr()`:\n! Can't recycle `.x` (size 0) to match `.y` (size 8).\nRun `rlang::last_trace()` to see where the error occurred.\nError in expression 3 of 5: Can't recycle `.x` (size 0) to match `.y` (size 8)."},"elapsedMs":344}</script></tool-result></div>



<div><tool-call id="toolu_01D3dfR5EFVifACKwDep6rfL"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"runCode","input":{"code":"\n# Fix: wide is keyed on `name`, need to look up values by row\nvals <- function(key) wide ...[truncated, total 1351 chars]","intent":"Rebuild wikitext with corrected row lookup logic"},"intent":"Rebuild wikitext with corrected row lookup logic","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"\n# Fix: wide is keyed on `name`, need to look up values by row\nvals <- function(key) wide |> dplyr::filter(name == key) |>\n  dplyr::select(all_of(year_order)) |> unlist() |> unname()\n\nmale_vals   <- vals(\"male_total\")\nfemale_vals <- vals(\"female_total\")\n\ndata_row <- function(label, key, show_if_both = FALSE) {\n  cells <- vals(key)\n  if (show_if_both) {\n    cells <- ifelse(male_vals == \"0\" | female_vals == \"0\", \"\\u2014\", cells)\n  }\n  paste0('| style=\"text-align:left\" | ', label, \"\\n\",\n         paste0(\"| \", cells, collapse = \"\\n\"))\n}\n\nheader_cells <- paste(col_meta$header, collapse = \" !! \")\n\nrows <- list(\n  data_row(\"Total detainee population\"),\n  data_row(\"\\u2026\\u00a0with a criminal record\",  \"with_crim\"),\n  data_row(\"\\u2026\\u00a0with no criminal record\",  \"no_crim\"),\n  data_row(\"Male detainees\",   \"male_total\",   show_if_both = TRUE),\n  data_row(\"Female detainees\", \"female_total\", show_if_both = TRUE)\n)\n\nwikitext <- paste0(\n  '{| class=\"wikitable\" style=\"text-align:right\"\\n',\n  \"|+ Average daily detainee population in ICE custody, FY\\u00a02019\\u2013FY\\u00a02026\\n\",\n  \"|-\\n\",\n  '! style=\"text-align:left\" | Fiscal year',\n  \" !! \", header_cells, \"\\n\",\n  paste(purrr::map_chr(rows, \\(r) paste0(\"|-\\n\", r)), collapse = \"\\n\"),\n  \"\\n|-\\n\",\n  '| colspan=\"9\" style=\"font-size:90%;text-align:left\" | {{notelist}}\\n',\n  \"|}\"\n)\n\ncat(wikitext)\n","language":"r"}}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01D3dfR5EFVifACKwDep6rfL"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"content","value":[{"type":"text","text":"Output:\nError in `dplyr::filter()`:\nℹ In argument: `name == key`.\nCaused by error in `data...[truncated, total 362 chars]"}]},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"Error in `dplyr::filter()`:\nℹ In argument: `name == key`.\nCaused by error in `data_row()`:\n! argument \"key\" is missing, with no default\nRun `rlang::last_trace()` to see where the error occurred.\nError in expression 6 of 8: ℹ In argument: `name == key`.\nCaused by error in `data_row()`:\n! argument \"key\" is missing, with no default"},"elapsedMs":706}</script></tool-result></div>



<div><tool-call id="toolu_019SrNxVi9xWu8aFNX8y2P8C"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"runCode","input":{"code":"\nvals <- function(key) {\n  wide |> dplyr::filter(name == key) |>\n    dplyr::select(dplyr::...[truncated, total 1468 chars]","intent":"Rebuild wikitext cleanly with all five data rows"},"intent":"Rebuild wikitext cleanly with all five data rows","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"\nvals <- function(key) {\n  wide |> dplyr::filter(name == key) |>\n    dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(year_order)) |>\n    unlist() |> unname()\n}\n\nmale_vals   <- vals(\"male_total\")\nfemale_vals <- vals(\"female_total\")\n\ndata_row <- function(label, key, show_if_both = FALSE) {\n  cells <- vals(key)\n  if (show_if_both)\n    cells <- ifelse(male_vals == \"0\" | female_vals == \"0\", \"\\u2014\", cells)\n  paste0('| style=\"text-align:left\" | ', label, \"\\n\",\n         paste0(\"| \", cells, collapse = \"\\n\"))\n}\n\nheader_cells <- paste(col_meta$header, collapse = \" !! \")\n\nrow_total   <- data_row(\"Total detainee population\",          \"total\")\nrow_crim    <- data_row(\"\\u2026\\u00a0with a criminal record\", \"with_crim\")\nrow_nocrim  <- data_row(\"\\u2026\\u00a0with no criminal record\",\"no_crim\")\nrow_male    <- data_row(\"Male detainees\",                     \"male_total\",   show_if_both = TRUE)\nrow_female  <- data_row(\"Female detainees\",                   \"female_total\", show_if_both = TRUE)\n\nwikitext <- paste(\n  '{| class=\"wikitable\" style=\"text-align:right\"',\n  \"|+ Average daily detainee population in ICE custody, FY\\u00a02019\\u2013FY\\u00a02026\",\n  \"|-\",\n  paste0('! style=\"text-align:left\" | Fiscal year !! ', header_cells),\n  paste0(\"|-\\n\", row_total),\n  paste0(\"|-\\n\", row_crim),\n  paste0(\"|-\\n\", row_nocrim),\n  paste0(\"|-\\n\", row_male),\n  paste0(\"|-\\n\", row_female),\n  \"|-\",\n  '| colspan=\"9\" style=\"font-size:90%;text-align:left\" | {{notelist}}',\n  \"|}\",\n  sep = \"\\n\"\n)\n\ncat(wikitext)\n","language":"r"}}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_019SrNxVi9xWu8aFNX8y2P8C"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"content","value":[{"type":"text","text":"Output:\n{| class=\"wikitable\" style=\"text-align:right\"\n|+ Average daily detainee population...[truncated, total 1277 chars]"}]},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"{| class=\"wikitable\" style=\"text-align:right\"\n|+ Average daily detainee population in ICE custody, FY 2019–FY 2026\n|-\n! style=\"text-align:left\" | Fiscal year !! The wikitext and RIS file are both ready. The wikitext is saved in your session and the RIS file is at `data/citations/ice-detention-stats.ris`.

**To complete the citations, please:**

1. In Zotero, use **File → Import…** and select `data/citations/ice-detention-stats.ris`
2. Zotero will create 8 items. For each, verify the URL resolves (some older ICE links may have changed)
3. Select all 8 items, right-click → **Create Bibliography** → output style: **Wikipedia Citation Templates** (or use the Better BibTeX plugin's QuickCopy in Wikipedia mode)
4. Paste the 8 resulting `{{cite web}}` or `{{cite report}}` templates back here and I'll wire them into the wikitext as `<ref name="ice-detention-fyXX">…</ref>` blocks

A few things to be aware of when Zotero processes these:
- The `TY = DATA` type may map to a generic "document" in Zotero — you may want to manually set each item's type to **Report** or **Web Page** before exporting, as Wikipedia's `{{cite report}}` or `{{cite web}}` templates are more appropriate than `{{cite dataset}}`
- The `DA` field carries the snapshot date for FY25 and FY26, which should appear as the access date
- FY21, FY25, and FY26 are YTD snapshots — the table marks them with `{{efn|YTD snapshot}}` which will render as a lettered footnote via `{{notelist}}`

## Databot

This conversation is being continued from a previous conversation that was compacted to reduce token use. The conversation is summarized below:

## Project Overview

The user is working on two related R projects:

1. **ice-detention** (new): A `targets`-based pipeline for importing, cleaning, and harmonizing 8 years (FY 2019–FY 2026) of U.S. ICE detention facility statistics. This is a refactored extraction from the original `import-ice-detention.qmd` in the wiki-graph project.

2. **wiki-graph** (reference): The original source project containing `import-ice-detention.qmd` and related work on Wikipedia citation scraping and Zotero integration. The ice-detention pipeline was extracted from and is built on work from this project.

Both projects use **renv** for package management and are managed with `targets` for reproducible data pipelines.

## Data Pipeline Structure

The ice-detention pipeline has 13 targets organized as follows:

**Source & Import Stage:**
- `data_file_info` — metadata for 8 ICE spreadsheets (FY19–FY26), including URLs, sheet names, header positions
- `raw_xlsx_files` — downloads missing Excel files from ICE; uses `cue = "never"` to avoid re-downloading
- `clean_names_list` — parses 2-row Excel headers to extract variable names
- `facilities_raw` — raw tibbles, one per fiscal year

**Cleaning & Standardization:**
- `facilities_clean` — name/city standardization, type coercion, Guantanamo patch; also saves **footnote rows** (spreadsheet metadata) to `data/footnotes/{year}-footnotes.txt`
- `facilities_aggregated` — row-level ADP sums, criminality/threat level aggregations, facility type classification

**Crosswalk & Deduplication:**
- `facility_crosswalk` — exact + fuzzy address matching (Pass 1: exact name+city+state; Pass 2: within ZIP, OSA similarity ≥ 0.80) to create canonical facility IDs; uses union-find to merge duplicates
- `facilities_keyed` — joins `canonical_id` and `canonical_name` back to aggregated data
- `facility_presence` — one row per canonical facility, showing year presence (FY19–FY26 booleans) and trajectory labels (continuous, persistent_gaps, closed, new, transient)

**Derived Products:**
- `canonical_facilities` — one row per facility with most recent address columns; authoritative input for downstream steps (like geocoding)
- `facilities_panel` — long format panel: one row per facility per year, with ~30 measurement variables
- `facilities_geocoded` — canonical facilities with lat/lon from Google Maps API; uses `cue = "never"` to avoid re-geocoding
- `saved_files` — writes RDS + CSV to `data/` for external use

**Key Functions by File:**

| File | Functions |
|------|-----------|
| `metadata.R` | `build_data_file_info()` |
| `download.R` | `download_ice_spreadsheets()` |
| `import.R` | `header_rows()`, `clean_variable_names_from_header()`, `build_clean_names()`, `read_facilities_data()`, `import_all_years()` |
| `clean.R` | `clean_facility_names()`, `fix_city_names()`, `clean_facilities_data()`, `clean_all_years()`, `save_year_footnotes()` |
| `aggregate.R` | `aggregate_facilities_data()`, `aggregate_all_years()` |
| `crosswalk.R` | `build_facility_crosswalk()`, `attach_canonical_ids()`, `build_facility_presence()`, `build_canonical_facilities()`, `build_panel()`, `save_outputs()` |
| `geocode.R` | `geocode_facilities(canonical_facilities)` — uses `ggmap::mutate_geocode()` with Google Maps API |
| `wikipedia.R` | `generate_infobox()` — generates `{{Infobox prison}}` wikitext; includes state lookup tables and helper functions |
| `update-versioned-archive.R` | `update_versioned_archive(df, filename)` — archives derived datasets with version control via datetime stamps and metadata sidecars |

## Data Cleaning & Preparation

**Footnote Row Handling:** The original Excel spreadsheets contain non-facility metadata rows (footnotes, notes about facility transitions, inspection methodology notes). These are identified by the absence of `facility_city` and `facility_state`. The `save_year_footnotes()` function (called in `clean_all_years()`) extracts these rows **before** name cleaning garbles them, and writes them to `data/footnotes/{year}-footnotes.txt` (FY22 and FY23 only have these). The rows are then filtered out from the clean dataset.

**Canonical Facility Deduplication:** The crosswalk uses a two-pass strategy:
1. **Exact match** on (facility_name, facility_city, facility_state) → provisional IDs
2. **Fuzzy match** within the same ZIP code on facility_address using Optimal String Alignment (OSA) similarity ≥ 0.80 → merges via union-find, except for manually excluded pairs in `do_not_merge_pairs()`

Result: **348 canonical facilities** (from ~398 distinct name+city+state variants and 59 address-based merges). Canonical name is the most recent year's name for each cluster.

## Geocoding & Coordinates

**Process:**
- `geocode_facilities(canonical_facilities)` uses `ggmap::mutate_geocode()` with the Google Maps Geocoding API
- Requires `google_maps_api_key` environment variable; registration via `ggmap::register_google(key = Sys.getenv("google_maps_api_key"), write = FALSE)`
- Input: 348 canonical facilities with address strings (full address or city+state fallback)
- Output: lat/lon coordinates for all 348 facilities; **0 NAs**
- Initially found 10 junk rows (footnotes from Excel) with spurious coordinates; fixed by improving `clean_facilities_data()` filter

**Known Issue:** Some geocoded coordinates are inaccurate (e.g., La Palma in Eloy, AZ resolved to San Antonio, TX). These need validation via reverse geocoding or manual inspection.

## Wikipedia Infobox Generation

**Template Coverage:** The `{{Infobox prison}}` Wikipedia template has ~20 parameters. Our data covers most standard fields:
- Identity: `name`, `location` (city+state), `street-address`, `city`, `state`, `zip`, `country`
- Operations: `status` (operational/closed), `classification` (facility type)
- Population: `population` (total ADP), `population_as_of` (fiscal year)
- Capacity: `capacity` (guaranteed minimum beds)
- Dates: `opened`, `closed`
- Management: `managed_by` (contractor name — needs join fix)
- Geography: `coordinates`, `pushpin_map` (state-level map), `pushpin_map_caption`
- Other: `former_name` (alternative names from crosswalk)

**Not in our data:** director/warden names, website, prisoner lists

**Derivable but not yet implemented:**
- `county` — could use reverse geocoding on lat/lon
- Inspection data (type, standard, rating, date) — distinctive but requires custom `embedded` sub-infobox

**`generate_infobox()` Function** (in `R/wikipedia.R`):
- Signature: `generate_infobox(cid, canonical_facilities, presence, geocoded, panel = NULL, crosswalk = NULL, wiki_matches = NULL)`
- Takes canonical_id, looks up identity and address from `canonical_facilities`, status from `facility_presence`, coordinates from `facilities_geocoded`, measurements from `facilities_panel` (optional), and management company from wiki_matches (optional, needs join fix)
- Returns complete `{{Infobox prison}}` wikitext

**State Lookup:** `state_pushpin_map` is a named vector mapping 2-letter state codes to Wikipedia location map names (e.g., `"FL" → "USA Florida"`)

## Versioned Archiving

**Fixed Issues in `update-versioned-archive.R`:**

1. **Timestamp granularity:** Changed from daily (`%Y-%m-%d`) to hourly (`%Y-%m-%d-%Hh`), with collision guard using letter suffixes (`-a`, `-b`, etc.) if multiple updates occur in the same hour
2. **Stale undated assumption:** The "no change" branch now verifies the undated file matches its newest dated archive; if they've drifted (e.g., manual edit), the undated file is re-archived
3. **No metadata:** Now writes a `.txt` sidecar alongside each new dated archive with timestamp, rows, columns, column names, and MD5 hash
4. **Deprecated function:** Replaced `dplyr::all_equal()` with base `all.equal()`

**Usage:** Called by pipeline to version `facility_crosswalk`, `facility_presence`, and `facilities_panel` on each `tar_make()` run, ensuring reproducibility and change tracking.

## Key Variables in Datasets

| Variable | Description | Source |
|----------|-------------|--------|
| `canonical_id`, `canonical_name` | Post-crosswalk facility identifiers | crosswalk output |
| `facility_name`, `facility_city`, `facility_state`, `facility_zip`, `facility_address` | Address fields | raw import |
| `facility_type_detailed` | Raw ICE code (IGSA, DIGSA, SPC, etc.) | raw import |
| `facility_type_wiki` | Readable type for Wikipedia (Jail, Private Migrant Detention Center, etc.) | aggregate step |
| `sum_criminality_levels` | Total ADP (average daily population) | summed from gender+criminality columns |
| `adp_criminality_male_crim/non_crim`, `adp_criminality_female_crim/non_crim` | ADP by gender and criminal record | raw import |
| `adp_ice_threat_level_{1-3}`, `adp_no_ice_threat_level` | ADP by ICE threat level classification | raw import |
| `inspections_guaranteed_minimum` | Contracted bed capacity | raw import |
| `inspections_last_inspection_type`, `..._standard`, `..._final_rating`, `..._end_date` | Last inspection details | raw import |
| `share_non_crim`, `share_no_threat` | Derived proportions | aggregate step |
| `facility_average_length_of_stay_alos` | ALOS in days | raw import |
| `fiscal_year` | FY19, FY20, ..., FY26 | added during panel construction |

## Wikipedia Table & Citations (Latest Work)

**Horizontal ADP Table:** Created a Wikipedia-style horizontal population reference table modeled on "Black Population in Tennessee, 1790–1860":

| Metric | FY 2019 | FY 2020 | FY 2021 | FY 2022 | FY 2023 | FY 2024 | FY 2025 | FY 2026 |
|--------|---------|---------|---------|---------|---------|---------|---------|---------|
| Total detainee population | 49,141 | 32,979 | 18,291 | 21,343 | 28,148 | 36,277 | 47,550 | 66,812 |
| … with a criminal record | 19,309 | 14,197 | 5,761 | 4,918 | 7,934 | 9,899 | 14,160 | 14,965 |
| … with no criminal record | 29,832 | 18,781 | 12,530 | 16,425 | 20,214 | 26,378 | 33,390 | 51,847 |
| Male detainees | 41,667 | 28,129 | 15,062 | 18,068 | 24,071 | 31,565 | 41,482 | 58,548 |
| Female detainees | 7,474 | 4,850 | 3,228 | 3,275 | 4,077 | 4,712 | 6,068 | 8,264 |

- FY21, FY25, FY26 are marked as YTD snapshots via `{{efn|YTD snapshot}}`
- Each column has a `<ref name="ice-detention-fyXX"/>` placeholder for per-year citations

**RIS Citation File:** Generated `data/citations/ice-detention-stats.ris` with 8 records (one per fiscal year), each containing:
- TY = DATA
- AU = U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement
- TI = ICE Detention Statistics FY {year}
- PY = {year}
- DA = fiscal year end date or snapshot date (FY25: 2025-09-24, FY26: 2026-02-02)
- PB = U.S. Department of Homeland Security
- UR = direct link to Excel file on ice.gov
- ID = ice-detention-fy{nn} (for ref name matching)
- KW = immigration detention, average daily population

**Next Steps:** User needs to import the RIS file into Zotero, verify sources, and export as Wikipedia citation templates (`{{cite report}}` or `{{cite web}}`) to be inserted as `<ref>` blocks in the wikitext.

## Other Key Infrastructure

- **renv:** Active for package isolation; lockfile created after installing: `readxl`, `dplyr`, `stringr`, `tidyr`, `purrr`, `tibble`, `httr`, `stringdist`, `glue`, `targets`, `ggmap`, `sf`, `mapview`, `igraph`, `htmltools`
- **git branch:** Agents have access to wiki-graph project for reference; permission boundaries respected
- **Footnote files:** `data/footnotes/FY22-footnotes.txt`, `data/footnotes/FY23-footnotes.txt` contain non-facility metadata from source spreadsheets
- **Memory file:** `AGENTS.md` documents project structure, data sources, pipeline targets, and functions for future sessions

## Pipeline Dependency Diagram

All 13 targets are in the store with valid timestamps; the dependency chain flows from `data_file_info` and `raw_xlsx_files` through import/clean/aggregate/crosswalk stages, splitting into three final output branches: `facility_presence`, `facilities_panel` (which feeds `canonical_facilities` → `facilities_geocoded`), and `saved_files`.

3. Filter `facilities_panel` to those years
4. Build the wikitext table with citation refs from `ris_records`

The year order is: FY19, FY20, FY21, FY22, FY23, FY24, FY25, FY26

The table rows should be:


- Total detainee population
- Those with a criminal record
- Those without a criminal record
- Male detainees
- Female detainees

For the column headers, I need to use the fiscal year labels.

Looking at the RIS records, the ID field contains values like `ice-detention-fy19`, which matches the reference format in the existing wikitext. I can derive the reference name from the fiscal year column—if it's "FY19", the ref name becomes "ice-detention-fy19"—so I'll go with that approach.

For the YTD snapshot notation, I'll only include it when the fiscal year is marked as YTD and falls within the active range for that facility. The column headers need to convert from "FY19" format to "FY 2019", and for the table caption, I should use the specific facility name rather than the generic "ICE custody" label.

The `{{notelist}}` should only appear if there are actually YTD years included in the table. For the colspan calculation, I need to account for the row label column plus the number of data columns. When handling missing values, I'll display a dash for NA entries. Looking at how the existing wikitext handles citations, it uses named references like `<ref name="ice-detention-fy19"/>` that point to citations defined elsewhere in the article's references section rather than inline citations.

For the year range in the title, I'm pulling from `first_seen` and `last_seen` in the facility presence data, then formatting it as "FY YYYY–FY YYYY" based on the ordered year list.

Now I'm thinking through how to handle years where the facility has no data — should I show all years in the range with blanks for gaps, or only include years when the facility was actually present? Looking at the existing aggregate table, it shows continuous years, so I'll probably want to display the full range between first and last seen, filling in dashes for any absent years in between. Writing the R function... Adding derived columns to aggregate the criminality data by gender and offense type, then creating a helper function to format fiscal year labels.

Now I'm setting up utility functions to handle the data retrieval and formatting—generating reference names for each fiscal year, formatting numeric values with thousand separators or dashes for missing data, and pulling specific metrics from the filtered dataset for display.

Building the table header... Generating the markdown table... I need to make sure the ellipsis character and spacing match the existing wikitext format, and I'm realizing the ref names should point to citations that are already compiled in the `ris_records` data. The user wants me to use those citations to source the table columns, so I should probably include a references section or at least clarify how the named refs connect to the full citations. I'm realizing that since each fiscal year appears only once in the header row, every reference will be a first use, so I should include the full citation text inline. I'll derive the reference ID directly from the fiscal year using a simple pattern like `ice-detention-fy19`, then write the function to generate these properly formatted citations in the table headers.
