Both datasets are imported and saved:

| Source | Variable | Rows | Notes |
|--------|----------|------|-------|
| 2015 ICE XLSX | `xlsx_2015_raw` | 209 | Already in session; clean column names |
| 2017 DMCP PDF | `pdf_2017` | 201 | Parsed from fixed-width PDF via `pdf_data()` |

The PDF parser uses bounding-box x-coordinates to assign each token to one of 40 columns, with row grouping on y-gaps > 1 pt. The main cleanup steps were: pushing the FY10 ADP / `facility_operator` boundary from x=434 to x=437, and then filtering stray header/footer rows (those lacking a valid all-caps DETLOC and a non-NA `state`).

One thing worth noting: **201 vs 209 rows** — the 2017 list has 8 fewer facilities than 2015. Whether that reflects actual closures, additions, or differences in how these lists were compiled is worth checking if you plan to use them together.

I'll suggest some questions below.


<span class="suggestion">Compare the 2015 and 2017 facility lists by DETLOC to identify which facilities opened, closed, or changed between the two years.</span>


<span class="suggestion">Join `pdf_2017` to the existing `facilities_panel` to see which FY17 DMCP facilities match canonical IDs in the main pipeline.</span>


<span class="suggestion">Update AGENTS.md to document the two new raw data files and their variables.</span>





## User

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01SeZYLZXaW8jAA5W8qSxdk3"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"text","value":"[File: 3.8KB, 103 lines, showing lines 1-103]\n     1→# Import facility data from ICE Excel...[truncated, total 4133 chars]"},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"[File: 3.8KB, 103 lines, showing lines 1-103]\n# Import facility data from ICE Excel spreadsheets.\n#\n# Two-step process:\n#   1. Read the two-row headers and combine into clean variable names.\n#   2. Read the data rows using those names.\n\n# ── Header extraction ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nheader_rows <- function(data_file_info_row) {\n  with(data_file_info_row, {\n    first_row <- readxl::read_excel(\n      local_file,\n      sheet = sheet_name,\n      range = readxl::cell_limits(\n        ul = c(first_header_row, 1),\n        lr = c(first_header_row, right_column_num)\n      ),\n      col_names = FALSE\n    )\n    second_row <- readxl::read_excel(\n      local_file,\n      sheet = sheet_name,\n      range = readxl::cell_limits(\n        ul = c(second_header_row, 1),\n        lr = c(second_header_row, right_column_num)\n      ),\n      col_names = FALSE\n    )\n    rbind(first_row, second_row)\n  })\n}\n\n# ── Variable name cleaning ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nclean_variable_names_from_header <- function(raw_headers) {\n  clean_names <- raw_headers |>\n    t() |>\n    as.data.frame() |>\n    tidyr::fill(V1) |>\n    dplyr::mutate(\n      V1 = stringr::str_trim(stringr::str_remove_all(V1, \"FY\\\\d\\\\d\")),\n      V1 = stringr::str_remove_all(V1, \":\"),\n      V2 = stringr::str_trim(stringr::str_remove_all(V2, \"FY\\\\d\\\\d\")),\n      V2 = stringr::str_remove_all(V2, \":\"),\n      V1 = dplyr::case_when(\n        stringr::str_detect(V1, \"This list\") ~ \"Facility\",\n        V1 == \"Facility Information\" ~ \"Facility\",\n        V1 == \"ADP Detainee Classification Level\" ~ \"ADP Detainee Classification\",\n        V1 == \"ADP Detainee Security Level\" ~ \"ADP Detainee Security\",\n        V1 == \"ADP ICE Threat Level\" ~ \"ADP\",\n        V1 == \"ADP Mandatory\" ~ \"ADP\",\n        V1 == \"Contract Facility Inspections Information\" ~ \"Inspections\",\n        TRUE ~ V1\n      ),\n      combined = paste(V1, V2),\n      var_name = tolower(combined),\n      var_name = stringr::str_replace_all(var_name, \"[^a-z0-9]+\", \"_\"),\n      var_name = stringr::str_remove_all(var_name, \"^_+|_+$\")\n    ) |>\n    dplyr::pull(var_name)\n\n  clean_names\n}\n\n# ── Build clean names for all years ──────────────────────────────────────────\n\nbuild_clean_names <- function(data_file_info) {\n  raw_headers_list <- purrr::map(seq_len(nrow(data_file_info)), function(i) {\n    header_rows(data_file_info[i, ])\n  })\n  names(raw_headers_list) <- data_file_info$year_name\n\n  clean_names_list <- purrr::map(raw_headers_list, clean_variable_names_from_header)\n  names(clean_names_list) <- data_file_info$year_name\n  clean_names_list\n}\n\n# ── Read facility data for one year ──────────────────────────────────────────\n\nread_facilities_data <- function(data_file_info_row, clean_names_list) {\n  with(data_file_info_row, {\n    readxl::read_xlsx(\n      local_file,\n      sheet = sheet_name,\n      range = readxl::cell_limits(\n        ul = c(first_data_row, 1),\n        lr = c(NA, right_column_num)\n      ),\n      col_names = clean_names_list[[year_name]]\n    )\n  })\n}\n\n# ── Import all years ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nimport_all_years <- function(data_file_info, clean_names_list) {\n  facilities_data_list <- purrr::map(seq_len(nrow(data_file_info)), function(i) {\n    read_facilities_data(data_file_info[i, ], clean_names_list)\n  })\n  names(facilities_data_list) <- data_file_info$year_name\n  facilities_data_list\n}\n"},"elapsedMs":68}</script></tool-result></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_012mEwef8wVdFijzU6g57z57"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"text","value":"[File: 5.3KB, 116 lines, showing lines 1-116]\n     1→# _targets.R — ICE Detention Data Pip...[truncated, total 4778 chars]"},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"[File: 5.3KB, 116 lines, showing lines 1-116]\n# _targets.R — ICE Detention Data Pipeline\n#\n# Run with: targets::tar_make()\n# Visualize: targets::tar_visnetwork()\n# Load a result: targets::tar_read(facilities_panel)\n\nlibrary(targets)\n\ntar_option_set(\n  packages = c(\"readxl\", \"dplyr\", \"stringr\", \"tidyr\", \"purrr\",\n               \"tibble\", \"httr\", \"stringdist\", \"glue\")\n)\n\n# Auto-source all functions in R/\ntar_source()\n\nlist(\n  # ── Metadata ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # Spreadsheet URLs, sheet names, header positions, etc.\n  tar_target(data_file_info, build_data_file_info()),\n\n  # ── Download (rarely run) ──────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # Downloads files that don't already exist locally.\n  # Set to cue = \"never\" so it only runs when you explicitly invalidate it:\n  #   tar_invalidate(raw_xlsx_files); tar_make()\n  tar_target(\n    raw_xlsx_files,\n    download_ice_spreadsheets(data_file_info),\n    format = \"file\",\n    cue = tar_cue(mode = \"never\")\n  ),\n\n  # ── Import headers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # Reads the two-row headers from each spreadsheet and produces\n  # a named list of clean variable name vectors.\n  tar_target(\n    clean_names_list,\n    build_clean_names(data_file_info)\n  ),\n\n  # ── Import raw data ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # Returns a named list of tibbles, one per fiscal year.\n  tar_target(\n    facilities_raw,\n    import_all_years(data_file_info, clean_names_list)\n  ),\n\n  # ── Clean ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # Name standardization, type coercion, Guantanamo patch.\n  tar_target(\n    facilities_clean,\n    clean_all_years(facilities_raw)\n  ),\n\n  # ── Aggregate ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # Row-level sums (ADP by classification, criminality, threat level)\n  # and facility_type_wiki classification.\n  tar_target(\n    facilities_aggregated,\n    aggregate_all_years(facilities_clean)\n  ),\n\n  # ── Facility crosswalk ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # Exact + fuzzy address matching across years → canonical facility IDs.\n  tar_target(\n    facility_crosswalk,\n    build_facility_crosswalk(facilities_aggregated)\n  ),\n\n  # ── Attach canonical IDs ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # Adds a facility_id column to the aggregated data, using the crosswalk.\n  tar_target(\n    facilities_keyed,\n    attach_canonical_ids(facilities_aggregated, facility_crosswalk)\n  ),\n\n  # ── Facility presence / trajectory ─────────────────────────────────────────\n  # For each facility, which years was it present in the data? When did it open/close?\n  tar_target(\n    facility_presence,\n    build_facility_presence(facilities_keyed)\n  ),\n\n  # ── Panel dataset ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  tar_target(\n    facilities_panel,\n    build_panel(facilities_keyed)\n  ),\n\n  # ── Canonical facility list ────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # One row per canonical facility with most recent address columns.\n  # This is the authoritative input for geocoding and infobox generation.\n  tar_target(\n    canonical_facilities,\n    build_canonical_facilities(facilities_panel)\n  ),\n\n  # ── Geocode facilities ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # Calls Google Maps API. Set cue = \"never\" to avoid re-geocoding on each run.\n  # To re-run: targets::tar_invalidate(facilities_geocoded); tar_make()\n  # Requires google_maps_api_key in .Renviron and register_google() called first.\n  tar_target(\n    facilities_geocoded,\n    geocode_facilities(canonical_facilities),\n    cue = tar_cue(mode = \"never\")\n  ),\n\n  # ── Save outputs to data/ ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  # Exports RDS + CSV files for external use.\n  tar_target(\n    saved_files,\n    save_outputs(facility_crosswalk, facility_presence, facilities_panel),\n    format = \"file\"\n  )\n)\n"},"elapsedMs":80}</script></tool-result></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01RGo7EDdsLMxQ5NEWsDoxuS"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"text","value":"[File: 7.3KB, 190 lines, showing lines 1-190]\n     1→# Clean facility data: name standardi...[truncated, total 8263 chars]"},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"[File: 7.3KB, 190 lines, showing lines 1-190]\n# Clean facility data: name standardization, type coercion, and patches.\n\n# ── Facility name cleaning ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nclean_facility_names <- function(x) {\n  x <- stringr::str_to_title(x)\n\n  # Typos and truncations\n  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, \"Facili\\\\b\", \"Facility\")\n  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, \"Processsing\", \"Processing\")\n\n  # Abbreviation expansions\n  expansions <- c(\n    \"\\\\bDept\\\\.?\\\\b\" = \"Department\",\n    \"\\\\bCtr\\\\.?\\\\b\"  = \"Center\",\n    \"\\\\bCorr\\\\.?\\\\b\" = \"Correctional\",\n    \"\\\\bInst\\\\.?\\\\b\" = \"Institution\",\n    \"\\\\bFed\\\\.?\\\\b\"  = \"Federal\",\n    \"\\\\bDet\\\\.?\\\\b\"  = \"Detention\",\n    \"\\\\bFac\\\\.?\\\\b\"  = \"Facility\",\n    \"\\\\bCo\\\\.?\\\\b\"   = \"County\"\n  )\n  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, expansions)\n\n  # Acronym restoration\n  acronyms <- c(\n    \"\\\\bOf\\\\b\" = \"of\",\n    \"\\\\bUs\\\\b\" = \"US\",\n    \"\\\\bIce\\\\b\" = \"ICE\",\n    \"\\\\bEro\\\\b\" = \"ERO\",\n    \"\\\\bMdc\\\\b\" = \"MDC\",\n    \"\\\\bCca\\\\b\" = \"CCA\",\n    \"\\\\bFci\\\\b\" = \"FCI\",\n    \"\\\\bFdc\\\\b\" = \"FDC\",\n    \"\\\\bJtf\\\\b\" = \"JTF\",\n    \"\\\\bSsm\\\\b\" = \"SSM\",\n    \"\\\\bTgk\\\\b\" = \"TGK\",\n    \"\\\\bIpc\\\\b\" = \"IPC\",\n    \"\\\\bSpc\\\\b\" = \"SPC\",\n    \"\\\\bIah\\\\b\" = \"IAH\",\n    \"\\\\bClipc\\\\b\" = \"CLIPC\",\n    \"\\\\bDigsa\\\\b\" = \"DIGSA\",\n    \"\\\\bIgsa\\\\b\"  = \"IGSA\"\n  )\n  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, acronyms)\n\n  # State abbreviation expansions\n  states <- c(\n    \"\\\\(Fl\\\\)\" = \"(Florida)\",\n    \"\\\\(In\\\\)\" = \"(Indiana)\",\n    \"\\\\(Mo\\\\)\" = \"(Missouri)\",\n    \"\\\\(Mt\\\\)\" = \"(Montana)\",\n    \"\\\\(Ne\\\\)\" = \"(Nebraska)\",\n    \"\\\\(Ny\\\\)\" = \"(New York)\",\n    \"\\\\(Tx\\\\)\" = \"(Texas)\",\n    \"\\\\(Ut\\\\)\" = \"(Utah)\",\n    \", Nm\\\\b\"  = \", New Mexico\",\n    \", Mt\\\\b\"  = \", Montana\"\n  )\n  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, states)\n\n  # Facility-specific name fixes\n  names_map <- c(\n    \"CCA, Florence Correctional Center\" = \"Central Arizona Florence Correctional Complex\",\n    \"Eloy Federal Contract Facility\" = \"Eloy Detention Center\",\n    \"T Don Hutto Detention Center\" = \"T. Don Hutto Detention Center\",\n    \"Torrance/Estancia, New Mexico\" = \"Torrance County Detention Facility\",\n    \"Houston Contract Detention Facility\" = \"Houston Processing Center\",\n    \"Elizabeth Contract Detention Facility\" = \"Elizabeth Detention Center\",\n    \"Folkston D Ray ICE Processing Center\" = \"Folkston ICE Processing Center\",\n    \"Robert A Deyton Detention Facility\" = \"Robert A. Deyton Detention Center\",\n    \"ERO El Paso Camp East Montana\" = \"Camp East Montana\",\n    \"Port Isabel SPC\" = \"Port Isabel Detention Center\",\n    \"Bluebonnet Detention Facility\" = \"Bluebonnet Detention Center\"\n  )\n  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, names_map)\n\n  x\n}\n\n# ── City name fixes ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nfix_city_names <- function(x) {\n  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, \"Ft.lauderdale\", \"Fort Lauderdale\")\n  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, \"Ft. Lauderdale\", \"Fort Lauderdale\")\n  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, \"Cottonwood Fall\", \"Cottonwood Falls\")\n  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, \"Sault Ste Marie\", \"Sault Ste. Marie\")\n  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, \"Bunkerhill\", \"Bunker Hill\")\n  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, \"Mcelhattan\", \"McElhattan\")\n  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, \"Mcfarland\", \"McFarland\")\n  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, \"Mccook\", \"McCook\")\n  x <- stringr::str_replace_all(x, \"Mccall\", \"McCall\")\n  x\n}\n\n# ── Main cleaning function ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nclean_facilities_data <- function(facilities_data) {\n  numerical_columns <- c(\n    \"facility_average_length_of_stay_alos\",\n    \"adp_detainee_classification_level_a\",\n    \"adp_detainee_classification_level_b\",\n    \"adp_detainee_classification_level_c\",\n    \"adp_detainee_classification_level_d\",\n    \"adp_criminality_male_crim\",\n    \"adp_criminality_male_non_crim\",\n    \"adp_criminality_female_crim\",\n    \"adp_criminality_female_non_crim\",\n    \"adp_ice_threat_level_1\",\n    \"adp_ice_threat_level_2\",\n    \"adp_ice_threat_level_3\",\n    \"adp_no_ice_threat_level\",\n    \"adp_mandatory\",\n    \"inspections_guaranteed_minimum\"\n  )\n  numerical_columns_present <- numerical_columns[numerical_columns %in% names(facilities_data)]\n\n  facilities_data_clean <- facilities_data |>\n    dplyr::mutate(\n      dplyr::across(\n        .cols = facility_average_length_of_stay_alos:adp_mandatory,\n        .fns = ~ round(as.numeric(.), 1)\n      ),\n      inspections_guaranteed_minimum = as.integer(inspections_guaranteed_minimum),\n      inspections_last_inspection_type = dplyr::case_when(\n        inspections_last_inspection_type %in% c(\"PRE-OCCUPANCY\", \"Pre-Occupancy\") ~ \"PREOCC\",\n        TRUE ~ inspections_last_inspection_type\n      ),\n      facility_name = clean_facility_names(facility_name),\n      facility_city = stringr::str_to_title(facility_city),\n      facility_address = stringr::str_to_title(facility_address),\n      facility_male_female = stringr::str_to_title(facility_male_female),\n      facility_city = fix_city_names(facility_city),\n      dplyr::across(\n        .cols = -dplyr::all_of(numerical_columns_present),\n        .fns = as.character\n      )\n    )\n\n  # Guantanamo patch\n  guantanamo_patch <- tibble::tibble(\n    facility_name = \"JTF Camp Six\",\n    facility_city = \"Guantanamo Bay\",\n    facility_state = \"Cuba\",\n    facility_address = \"Bldg 2144, U.S. Naval Station Guantanamo Bay\"\n  )\n  facilities_data_clean <- facilities_data_clean |>\n    dplyr::rows_update(guantanamo_patch, by = \"facility_name\", unmatched = \"ignore\")\n\n  facilities_data_clean\n}\n\n# ── Footnote extraction and saving ───────────────────────────────────────────\n# Footnote/non-facility rows are identified before cleaning by the absence of\n# facility_city and facility_state, which are always present for real rows.\n# They are written to data/footnotes/{year}-footnotes.txt for reference.\n\nsave_year_footnotes <- function(raw_df, year_name) {\n  footnotes <- raw_df[is.na(raw_df$facility_city) | is.na(raw_df$facility_state), ]\n  if (nrow(footnotes) == 0) return(invisible(NULL))\n\n  dir.create(\"data/footnotes\", showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)\n  path <- file.path(\"data/footnotes\", paste0(year_name, \"-footnotes.txt\"))\n\n  lines <- c(\n    paste(\"Non-facility rows extracted from\", year_name, \"spreadsheet\"),\n    paste(\"Extracted:\", format(Sys.time(), \"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\")),\n    \"\",\n    purrr::map_chr(seq_len(nrow(footnotes)), \\(i) {\n      name <- footnotes$facility_name[[i]]\n      if (is.na(name)) \"<empty row>\" else name\n    })\n  )\n  writeLines(lines, path)\n  invisible(path)\n}\n\n# ── Clean all years ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nclean_all_years <- function(facilities_data_list) {\n  purrr::imap(facilities_data_list, \\(df, yr) {\n    # Save any footnote rows from the raw data before cleaning garbles them\n    save_year_footnotes(df, yr)\n    # Drop footnote rows (no city/state), then clean\n    df |>\n      dplyr::filter(!is.na(facility_city), !is.na(facility_state)) |>\n      clean_facilities_data()\n  })\n}\n"},"elapsedMs":513}</script></tool-result></div>

1-56]\n     1→# Spreadsheet metadata for all ICE dete...[truncated, total 2591 chars]"},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"[File: 2.1KB, 56 lines, showing lines 1-56]\n# Spreadsheet metadata for all ICE detention facility files.\n#\n# Each row describes one fiscal year's Excel file: its URL, local path,\n# sheet name, header row positions, and rightmost data column.\n\nbuild_data_file_info <- function() {\n  LETTERS_PLUS <- c(LETTERS, paste0(\"A\", LETTERS), paste0(\"B\", LETTERS))\n\n  info <- tibble::tibble(\n    year_name = c(\"FY19\", \"FY20\", \"FY21\", \"FY22\", \"FY23\",\n                  \"FY24\", \"FY25\", \"FY26\"),\n    year = 2019:2026,\n    url = c(\n      \"https://www.ice.gov/doclib/detention/FY19-detentionstats.xlsx\",\n      \"https://www.ice.gov/doclib/detention/FY20-detentionstats.xlsx\",\n      \"https://www.ice.gov/doclib/detention/FY21-detentionstats.xlsx\",\n      \"https://www.ice.gov/doclib/detention/FY22-detentionStats.xlsx\",\n      \"https://www.ice.gov/doclib/detention/FY23_detentionStats.xlsx\",\n      \"https://www.ice.gov/doclib/detention/FY24_detentionStats.xlsx\",\n      \"https://www.ice.gov/doclib/detention/FY25_detentionStats09242025.xlsx\",\n      \"https://www.ice.gov/doclib/detention/FY26_detentionStats02022026.xlsx\"\n    ),\n    local_file = c(\n      \"data/FY19-detentionstats.xlsx\",\n      \"data/FY20-detentionstats.xlsx\",\n      \"data/FY21-detentionstats.xlsx\",\n      \"data/FY22-detentionStats.xlsx\",\n      \"data/FY23_detentionStats.xlsx\",\n      \"data/FY24_detentionStats.xlsx\",\n      \"data/FY25_detentionStats09242025.xlsx\",\n      \"data/FY26_detentionStats02022026.xlsx\"\n    ),\n    sheet_name = c(\n      \"Facilities FY19\", \"Facilities EOYFY20 \", \"Facilities FY21 YTD\",\n      \"Facilities FY22\", \"Facilities EOFY23\",\n      \"Facilities EOFY24\", \"Facilities FY25\", \"Facilities FY26\"\n    ),\n    header_rows = list(\n      c(5, 7), c(5, 7), c(5, 7), c(5, 7), c(4, 6),\n      c(5, 7), c(5, 7), c(9, 10)\n    ),\n    right_column = c(\"AE\", \"AE\", \"AE\", \"AD\", \"AG\", \"AB\", \"AB\", \"AA\")\n  )\n\n  info |>\n    dplyr::rowwise() |>\n    dplyr::mutate(\n      first_header_row  = unlist(header_rows)[[1]],\n      second_header_row = unlist(header_rows)[[2]],\n      first_data_row    = max(unlist(header_rows)) + 1,\n      right_column_num  = Now I have a clear picture. Let me analyze:

**xlsx_2015_raw columns** (38 columns):
- DETLOC, Name, Address, City, County, State, Zip
- Type, Type Detailed, Male/Female, Levels A,B,C,D
- Capacity, Population Count
- FY16 ADP, FY15 ADP, FY14 ADP, FY13 ADP, FY12 ADP, FY11 ADP, FY10 ADP
- Facility Operator, Facility Owner
- Best Known Contract Initiation Date, Best Known Contract Expiration Date


- Per Diem Rate Detailed, Authorizing Authority, Over/Under 72
- Last Inspection Type, Last Inspection Standard, Last Inspection Rating - Final, Last Inspection Date
- CY15 Rating, CY14 RATING, CY13 RATING, CY12 RATING, CY11 RATING, CY10 RATING
- DSM Assigned?

**pdf_2017** has 40 columns in snake_case format with the same structure as xlsx_2015_raw, plus FY17 ADP and CY16 rating added.

Let me double-check the column alignment between these datasets. The xlsx_2015_raw includes location and facility details like DETLOC, Name, Address, City, County, State, Zip, along with facility type information, capacity metrics, and historical ADP data going back to FY10. It also has contract dates, per diem rates, and inspection records. The pdf_2017 version appears to follow the same structure but with updated naming conventions and additional recent year ratings.

Both files are actually the same document type—the DMCP Facility List—just from different years, so the pdf_2017 is essentially an updated version with a couple of extra columns for the newer fiscal and calendar years. This is distinct from the annual detention statistics spreadsheets in the main pipeline, though they do share some overlapping columns.

Now I'm mapping the facility identity and inspection columns from these DMCP lists to the main panel schema—things like facility name, address, location details, capacity measures, and inspection history all have corresponding fields in the main dataset, though the ADP data structure differs between them.

Looking at what's unique to the DMCP lists, there's a whole set of variables the main panel doesn't capture: county-level data, facility operator and owner information, contract dates, per diem rates, authorizing authority, and the longitudinal ADP history from FY10-FY17. The DMCP also includes historical inspection ratings and some ICE-specific codes that don't map cleanly to the canonical identifiers in the main pipeline.

On the flip side, the main panel has AOR as a variable that doesn't appear in the DMCP lists at all.

The two datasets are really serving different purposes—DMCP is tracking authorized facility status and contract details, while the annual statistics focus on detailed ADP breakdowns by classification level, criminality, and ICE threat level, plus mandatory ADP figures and second-to-last inspection data.

For the naming standardization across both the 2015 and 2017 datasets, I need to apply consistent prefixes: facility-related columns get `facility_`, inspection data gets `inspections_`, and ADP columns stay with the `fy{yy}_` format.

Now I'm mapping out the final schema for DMCP—detloc stays as-is since it's ICE-specific, and I'm standardizing everything else to snake_case. The key columns will be facility identity (name, address, city, county, state, zip), facility characteristics (type, male/female breakdown, classification levels), and capacity metrics. For the capacity field, I'm aligning it with the main pipeline by using `inspections_guaranteed_minimum` instead of a separate capacity column, since the DMCP "Capacity" represents the same authorized bed count. I'll also include a population snapshot count and the ADP columns by fiscal year.

For the inspection-related fields, I'm pulling in the operator, owner, contract dates, per diem rate, authorizing authority, and the over/under 72-hour metric. The inspection history columns map directly—last inspection type, standard, final rating, and date—plus the calendar year rating columns and DSM assignment. This keeps everything consistent with how the main pipeline structures its inspection data.

Now I'm realizing the ADP columns need special handling. The main pipeline embeds annual ADP values within each year's row, but these DMCP lists track longitudinal ADP history per facility. I'll use the same `fy{nn}_adp` naming convention to stay aligned. For the remaining DMCP-specific fields like county, male/female breakdown, facility levels A through D, operator, owner, and contract details, I'm mapping them to their corresponding field names to maintain consistency across schemas.

For the contract and financial details, I'm adding facility_contract_initiation_date, facility_contract_expiration_date, facility_per_diem_rate_detailed, facility_authorizing_authority, and facility_over_under_72. The historical calendar year ratings follow the pattern cy{nn}_rating, and I'm tracking DSM assignments as dsm_assigned.

Now I'm looking at the name cleaning functions. The existing clean_facility_names() function is already source-agnostic and handles all the string transformations needed. The question is whether the 2015 raw data names need the same treatment—they're in all-caps like "ADELANTO ICE PROCESSING CENTER", and the function applies title case conversion first before expanding abbreviations.

The existing clean_facility_names() and fix_city_names() functions should work directly on the 2015 raw data without modification. However, clean_facilities_data() is specifically built for the FY19-FY26 panel with its own variable naming scheme, so I'll need either a new function for the DMCP schema or a more generalized approach. I'm thinking the cleanest solution is to create a dedicated function that maps the raw DMCP column names to the harmonized schema, then apply the existing cleaning functions. apply `clean_facility_names()` and `fix_city_names()` (which are already agnostic)
3. The DMCP-specific cleaning (numeric conversion, date parsing) in a new function

For the xlsx import side, the 2015 XLSX is straightforward - it has a single-row header structure. After parsing the first page with a specific y-threshold and the remaining pages with a lower threshold, I'm combining all the results and filtering for records where the detloc field contains only uppercase letters and numbers.

For the name harmonization, both datasets follow the same schema but use different naming conventions—the xlsx file uses title case while the pdf is already in snake_case. I'm mapping them to a consistent set of harmonized names that prefix facility-related fields with "facility_" to clarify their meaning. Now I'm looking at the inspection-related fields and how they map across the schemas, then considering whether I can reuse the existing string cleaning functions or if I need to build something new for this data structure. The existing pure string functions should work, but the current facility cleaning function is too tied to the annual stats schema to adapt directly.

I'm thinking I should create a column renaming function specifically for DMCP data that works on both the 2015 and 2017 versions since they share the same structure, then a separate cleaning function that applies the facility and city fixes along with any DMCP-specific type handling. But I need to be careful about where these live—the cleaning functions are already in R/clean.R, so the import and parsing logic should go in R/import.R as the user requested. - Put the cleaning/renaming functions in R/clean.R

Then cleaning:

Since `clean_facilities_data()` is specific to the annual stats schema, I need a new function for the DMCP schema, but I can reuse the existing string cleaning utilities. I'll create DMCP-specific functions while leveraging those helpers.

Now I'm comparing the 2017 PDF structure against the main panel to identify which columns map directly—things like facility name, address, city, state, zip, facility type, gender breakdown, and capacity all align with the existing schema without needing transformation.

Beyond those direct mappings, there are several columns unique to the DMCP dataset: internal ICE codes, county information, a broader facility type category distinct from the detailed type in the panel, security levels, population snapshots versus average daily population figures, historical ADP data spanning fiscal years 2010 through 2017, and operator/owner details that don't have counterparts in the main panel.

The DMCP also captures contract-related fields like initiation and expiration dates, per diem rates, authorizing authority, and whether facilities operate above or below 72-hour capacity, plus historical inspection ratings and DSM assignments. Meanwhile, the panel contains information the DMCP lacks—things like area of responsibility, average length of stay, ADP breakdowns by classification level and criminality, and various derived aggregate columns.

Since these datasets are complementary, I can join the 2015 and 2017 DMCP lists to the panel using facility identifiers like name, city, state, and zip after applying the crosswalk, which would layer in the operator and owner information along with the longitudinal ADP history.

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01Cb5hWCDsPZFLLfHWyjNHFN"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"text","value":"[File: 674B, 22 lines, showing lines 1-22]\n     1→# Download ICE detention spreadsheets fr...[truncated, total 871 chars]"},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"[File: 674B, 22 lines, showing lines 1-22]\n# Download ICE detention spreadsheets from ice.gov.\n#\n# Only downloads files that don't already exist locally.\n# Returns the vector of local file paths (for targets file tracking).\n\ndownload_ice_spreadsheets <- function(data_file_info) {\n  for (i in seq_len(nrow(data_file_info))) {\n    local <- data_file_info$local_file[i]\n    if (!file.exists(local)) {\n      message(\"Downloading \", basename(local), \" ...\")\n      httr::GET(\n        data_file_info$url[i],\n        httr::write_disk(local, overwrite = FALSE)\n      )\n    } else {\n      message(\"Already exists: \", basename(local))\n    }\n  }\n  # Return paths so targets can track them as files\n  data_file_info$local_file\n}\n"},"elapsedMs":449}</script></tool-result></div>

Good, I have everything I need. Let me write out the analysis and code.

**On cleaning functions:** The existing `clean_facility_names()` and `fix_city_names()` are already general-purpose string functions — they work fine on all-caps input since they start with `str_to_title()`. **No changes needed** to those. What needs new functions is: (1) column renaming for the DMCP schema, which is different enough from the stats-file two-row header process that adapting `clean_variable_names_from_header()` would require too many `case_when` branches; and (2) a DMCP-specific cleaning wrapper. New functions in both cases is the cleaner call.

**On `pdf_2017` variable overlap** with the main panel:

| Category | DMCP columns | Maps to panel as |
|---|---|---|
| Identity | name, address, city, state, zip, type_detailed, male_female | `facility_*` (same names) |
| Capacity | capacity | `facility_capacity` — **distinct** from `inspections_guaranteed_minimum` (total beds vs contracted minimum) |
| Inspections | last_inspection_{type,standard,rating_final,date} | `inspections_last_inspection_*` — exact match |
| **DMCP-only** | county, type (broad), levels, population_count, fy10–fy17_adp, facility_operator, facility_owner, contract dates, per_diem_rate, authorizing_authority, over_under_72, cy10–cy16_rating, dsm_assigned | No panel equivalent — these are what make the DMCP lists valuable as a supplement |
| **Panel-only** | — | AOR, ALOS, ADP breakdowns by classification/criminality/threat, all derived columns |

The DMCP lists are fundamentally a contract/authorization roster with longitudinal ADP history per row, rather than a detailed annual snapshot. They join to the panel through facility identity.

Now writing the code:

<div><tool-call id="toolu_01MazWhCwjsZBvaP9stN3kBZ"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"read","input":{"file_path":"/Users/bjorkjcr/Dropbox (Personal)/R/ice-detention/R/import.R"},"caption":"R/import.R"}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01MazWhCwjsZBvaP9stN3kBZ"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"text","value":"[File: 3.8KB, 103 lines, showing lines 1-103]\n     1→# Import facility data from ICE Excel...[truncated, total 4133 chars]"},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"[File: 3.8KB, 103 lines, showing lines 1-103]\n# Import facility data from ICE Excel spreadsheets.\n#\n# Two-step process:\n#   1. Read the two-row headers and combine into clean variable names.\n#   2. Read the data rows using those names.\n\n# ── Header extraction ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nheader_rows <- function(data_file_info_row) {\n  with(data_file_info_row, {\n    first_row <- readxl::read_excel(\n      local_file,\n      sheet = sheet_name,\n      range = readxl::cell_limits(\n        ul = c(first_header_row, 1),\n        lr = c(first_header_row, right_column_num)\n      ),\n      col_names = FALSE\n    )\n    second_row <- readxl::read_excel(\n      local_file,\n      sheet = sheet_name,\n      range = readxl::cell_limits(\n        ul = c(second_header_row, 1),\n        lr = c(second_header_row, right_column_num)\n      ),\n      col_names = FALSE\n    )\n    rbind(first_row, second_row)\n  })\n}\n\n# ── Variable name cleaning ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nclean_variable_names_from_header <- function(raw_headers) {\n  clean_names <- raw_headers |>\n    t() |>\n    as.data.frame() |>\n    tidyr::fill(V1) |>\n    dplyr::mutate(\n      V1 = stringr::str_trim(stringr::str_remove_all(V1, \"FY\\\\d\\\\d\")),\n      V1 = stringr::str_remove_all(V1, \":\"),\n      V2 = stringr::str_trim(stringr::str_remove_all(V2, \"FY\\\\d\\\\d\")),\n      V2 = stringr::str_remove_all(V2, \":\"),\n      V1 = dplyr::case_when(\n        stringr::str_detect(V1, \"This list\") ~ \"Facility\",\n        V1 == \"Facility Information\" ~ \"Facility\",\n        V1 == \"ADP Detainee Classification Level\" ~ \"ADP Detainee Classification\",\n        V1 == \"ADP Detainee Security Level\" ~ \"ADP Detainee Security\",\n        V1 == \"ADP ICE Threat Level\" ~ \"ADP\",\n        V1 == \"ADP Mandatory\" ~ \"ADP\",\n        V1 == \"Contract Facility Inspections Information\" ~ \"Inspections\",\n        TRUE ~ V1\n      ),\n      combined = paste(V1, V2),\n      var_name = tolower(combined),\n      var_name = stringr::str_replace_all(var_name, \"[^a-z0-9]+\", \"_\"),\n      var_name = stringr::str_remove_all(var_name, \"^_+|_+$\")\n    ) |>\n    dplyr::pull(var_name)\n\n  clean_names\n}\n\n# ── Build clean names for all years ──────────────────────────────────────────\n\nbuild_clean_names <- function(data_file_info) {\n  raw_headers_list <- purrr::map(seq_len(nrow(data_file_info)), function(i) {\n    header_rows(data_file_info[i, ])\n  })\n  names(raw_headers_list) <- data_file_info$year_name\n\n  clean_names_list <- purrr::map(raw_headers_list, clean_variable_names_from_header)\n  names(clean_names_list) <- data_file_info$year_name\n  clean_names_list\n}\n\n# ── Read facility data for one year ──────────────────────────────────────────\n\nread_facilities_data <- function(data_file_info_row, clean_names_list) {\n  with(data_file_info_row, {\n    readxl::read_xlsx(\n      local_file,\n      sheet = sheet_name,\n      range = readxl::cell_limits(\n        ul = c(first_data_row, 1),\n        lr = c(NA, right_column_num)\n      ),\n      col_names = clean_names_list[[year_name]]\n    )\n  })\n}\n\n# ── Import all years ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nimport_all_years <- function(data_file_info, clean_names_list) {\n  facilities_data_list <- purrr::map(seq_len(nrow(data_file_info)), function(i) {\n    read_facilities_data(data_file_info[i, ], clean_names_list)\n  })\n  names(facilities_data_list) <- data_file_info$year_name\n  facilities_data_list\n}\n"},"elapsedMs":72}</script></tool-result></div>



<div><tool-call id="toolu_011thc3qGhcdpSVBTcBYjaVP"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"edit","input":{"file_path":"/Users/bjorkjcr/Dropbox (Personal)/R/ice-detention/R/import.R","old_string":"# Import facility data from ICE Excel spreadsheets.\n#\n# Two-step process:\n#   1. Read the ...[truncated, total 189 chars]","new_string":"# Import facility data from ICE spreadsheets and supplemental DMCP listings.\n#\n# Annual FY...[truncated, total 516 chars]"},"caption":"R/import.R","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"@@ -1,8 +1,13 @@\n-# Import facility data from ICE Excel spreadsheets.\n+# Import facility data from ICE spreadsheets and supplemental DMCP listings.\n #\n-# Two-step process:\n+# Annual FY stats (FY19–FY26): two-step process:\n #   1. Read the two-row headers and combine into clean variable names.\n #   2. Read the data rows using those names.\n+#\n+# Supplemental DMCP listings (2015 XLSX, 2017 PDF): each is a separate\n+# point-in-time roster of authorized facilities with contract and historical\n+# ADP data across multiple fiscal years per row. See rename_dmcp_columns()\n+# in clean.R for the column harmonization step.\n \n # ── Header extraction ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n \n","language":"diff"}}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_011thc3qGhcdpSVBTcBYjaVP"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"text","value":"Updated /Users/bjorkjcr/Dropbox (Personal)/R/ice-detention/R/import.R"},"elapsedMs":451}</script></tool-result></div>



<div><tool-call id="toolu_01CSZUe5iaWRBgunaoApwELx"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"edit","input":{"file_path":"/Users/bjorkjcr/Dropbox (Personal)/R/ice-detention/R/import.R","old_string":"# ── Import all years ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nimport_al...[truncated, total 379 chars]","new_string":"# ── Import all years ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nimport_al...[truncated, total 4862 chars]"},"caption":"R/import.R","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"@@ -105,3 +105,119 @@\n   names(facilities_data_list) <- data_file_info$year_name\n   facilities_data_list\n }\n+\n+# ── Supplemental DMCP facility listings ──────────────────────────────────────\n+#\n+# The DMCP (Detention Management Compliance Program) lists are authorization\n+# rosters that capture contract, operator, and multi-year ADP data for each\n+# facility in a single row. They are a different document type from the annual\n+# FY stats files and are handled by their own import functions.\n+#\n+# Both functions return raw data with minimal transformation. Apply\n+# rename_dmcp_columns() + clean_dmcp_data() (from clean.R) as the next step.\n+\n+# ── Import 2015 DMCP facility listing (XLSX) ─────────────────────────────────\n+\n+import_fy15_dmcp <- function() {\n+  path <- here::here(\"data/2015IceDetentionFacilityListing.xlsx\")\n+  url  <- \"https://www.ice.gov/doclib/foia/dfs/2015IceDetentionFacilityListing.xlsx\"\n+\n+  if (!file.exists(path)) {\n+    message(\"Downloading \", basename(path), \" ...\")\n+    httr::GET(url, httr::write_disk(path, overwrite = FALSE))\n+  }\n+\n+  # Rows 1–6 are document title/metadata. Row 7 is the column header row.\n+  # Row 8+ is facility data.\n+  readxl::read_xlsx(\n+    path,\n+    sheet = \"Facility List - Main\",\n+    skip  = 6,\n+    col_names = TRUE\n+  ) |>\n+    # Drop blank trailing rows (no state or city)\n+    dplyr::filter(!is.na(State), !is.na(City))\n+}\n+\n+# ── Column layout constants for the 2017 DMCP PDF ────────────────────────────\n+#\n+# The PDF is a fixed-width table. Column boundaries are derived from the\n+# bounding-box x-coordinates of header tokens on page 1 (y = 63).\n+# Raw names use snake_case; rename_dmcp_columns() maps them to the project\n+# schema.\n+\n+.dmcp_pdf_col_breaks <- c(\n+  0, 34, 101, 151, 179, 206, 218, 228, 242, 260, 282, 298, 317,\n+  337, 349, 361, 373, 385, 397, 409, 422, 437, 469, 483, 510, 536,\n+  622, 642, 669, 687, 706, 734, 754, 782, 811, 840, 869, 898, 920, 944,\n+  Inf\n+)\n+\n+.dmcp_pdf_col_names <- c(\n+  \"detloc\", \"name\", \"address\", \"city\", \"county\", \"state\", \"zip\",\n+  \"type\", \"type_detailed\", \"male_female\", \"levels\",\n+  \"capacity\", \"population_count\",\n+  \"fy17_adp\", \"fy16_adp\", \"fy15_adp\", \"fy14_adp\", \"fy13_adp\",\n+  \"fy12_adp\", \"fy11_adp\", \"fy10_adp\",\n+  \"facility_operator\", \"facility_owner\",\n+  \"contract_initiation_date\", \"contract_expiration_date\",\n+  \"per_diem_rate_detailed\", \"authorizing_authority\",\n+  \"over_under_72\", \"last_inspection_type\", \"last_inspection_standard\",\n+  \"last_inspection_rating_final\", \"last_inspection_date\",\n+  \"cy16_rating\", \"cy15_rating\", \"cy14_rating\", \"cy13_rating\",\n+  \"cy12_rating\", \"cy11_rating\", \"cy10_rating\", \"dsm_assigned\"\n+)\n+\n+# ── Parse one page of the 2017 DMCP PDF ──────────────────────────────────────\n+#\n+# Uses pdftools::pdf_data() bounding-box coordinates to reconstruct the table.\n+# data_y_min: lowest y pixel to treat as data (skips the column header rows).\n+# On page 1, the header occupies y ≤ 65; other pages start with data at y = 1.\n+\n+.parse_dmcp_pdf_page <- function(page_tokens, data_y_min) {\n+  assign_col <- function(x_val) {\n+    idx <- findInterval(x_val, .dmcp_pdf_col_breaks, rightmost.closed = FALSE)\n+    .dmcp_pdf_col_names[idx]\n+  }\n+\n+  toks <- page_tokens |>\n+    dplyr::filter(y >= data_y_min) |>\n+    dplyr::arrange(y, x) |>\n+    dplyr::mutate(col = assign_col(x))\n+\n+  if (nrow(toks) == 0) return(tibble::tibble())\n+\n+  # New record when y-gap > 1 pt. Each facility spans a main row and a\n+  # DETLOC sub-row offset by 1 pt; inter-record gap is ≥ 2 pt.\n+  toks <- toks |>\n+    dplyr::mutate(record_id = cumsum(c(TRUE, diff(y) > 1)))\n+\n+  toks |>\n+    dplyr::group_by(record_id, col) |>\n+    dplyr::summarise(value = paste(text, collapse = \" \"), .groups = \"drop\") |>\n+    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = col, values_from = value, values_fill = NA) |>\n+    dplyr::select(-record_id)\n+}\n+\n+# ── Import 2017 DMCP facility list (PDF) ─────────────────────────────────────\n+\n+import_fy17_dmcp <- function() {\n+  path <- here::here(\"data/ICE_DMCP_Facility_List_2017.pdf\")\n+  url  <- \"https://www.prisonlegalnews.org/media/publications/ICE_DMCP_Facility_List_ERO_Custody_Management_Division_2017.pdf\"\n+\n+  if (!file.exists(path)) {\n+    message(\"Downloading \", basename(path), \" ...\")\n+    download.file(url, path, mode = \"wb\", quiet = TRUE)\n+  }\n+\n+  pdf_dat <- pdftools::pdf_data(path)\n+\n+  purrr::imap(\n+    pdf_dat,\n+    ~ .parse_dmcp_pdf_page(.x, data_y_min = if (.y == 1L) 66L else 1L)\n+  ) |>\n+    dplyr::bind_rows() |>\n+    # Keep only rows with a valid DETLOC code (all-caps alphanumeric, no spaces)\n+    # and a non-missing state; filters out page headers/footers/footnotes.\n+    dplyr::filter(grepl(\"^[A-Z0-9]+$\", detloc), !is.na(state))\n+}\n","language":"diff"}}</script></tool-call></div>

Now adding the renaming and cleaning functions to `clean.R`:

<div><tool-call id="toolu_01GA1iAqXKbPAhRCYKWnAUWZ"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"edit","input":{"file_path":"/Users/bjorkjcr/Dropbox (Personal)/R/ice-detention/R/clean.R","old_string":"# ── Footnote extraction and saving ───────────────────────────────────────────","new_string":"# ── DMCP column renaming ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n#\n# Harmon...[truncated, total 3917 chars]"},"caption":"R/clean.R","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"@@ -150,6 +150,101 @@\n   facilities_data_clean\n }\n \n+# ── DMCP column renaming ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n+#\n+# Harmonizes column names for both DMCP listing sources to the project schema.\n+# Handles the 2015 XLSX (raw human-readable names with spaces) and the 2017 PDF\n+# (snake_case names from the PDF parser) with a single function.\n+#\n+# Call this immediately after import_fy15_dmcp() / import_fy17_dmcp(), before\n+# clean_dmcp_data().\n+\n+rename_dmcp_columns <- function(df) {\n+  # Step 1: Normalize all names to snake_case (handles the xlsx source;\n+  # is a no-op for names already in snake_case from the PDF parser).\n+  df <- dplyr::rename_with(\n+    df,\n+    ~ tolower(.x) |>\n+      stringr::str_replace_all(\"[^a-z0-9]+\", \"_\") |>\n+      stringr::str_remove_all(\"^_+|_+$\")\n+  )\n+\n+  # Step 2: Strip \"best_known_\" prefix present in the xlsx source.\n+  df <- dplyr::rename_with(\n+    df,\n+    ~ stringr::str_remove(.x, \"^best_known_\"),\n+    dplyr::any_of(c(\n+      \"best_known_contract_initiation_date\",\n+      \"best_known_contract_expiration_date\"\n+    ))\n+  )\n+\n+  # Step 3: Unify the levels column name (xlsx produces \"levels_a_b_c_d\"\n+  # after snake_case conversion; pdf parser produces \"levels\").\n+  if (\"levels_a_b_c_d\" %in% names(df)) {\n+    df <- dplyr::rename(df, levels = levels_a_b_c_d)\n+  }\n+\n+  # Step 4: Apply project schema prefixes and harmonized names.\n+  rename_map <- c(\n+    facility_name                            = \"name\",\n+    facility_address                         = \"address\",\n+    facility_city                            = \"city\",\n+    facility_county                          = \"county\",\n+    facility_state                           = \"state\",\n+    facility_zip                             = \"zip\",\n+    facility_type                            = \"type\",\n+    facility_type_detailed                   = \"type_detailed\",\n+    facility_male_female                     = \"male_female\",\n+    facility_levels                          = \"levels\",\n+    facility_capacity                        = \"capacity\",\n+    facility_population_count               = \"population_count\",\n+    inspections_last_inspection_type         = \"last_inspection_type\",\n+    inspections_last_inspection_standard     = \"last_inspection_standard\",\n+    inspections_last_inspection_rating_final = \"last_inspection_rating_final\",\n+    inspections_last_inspection_date         = \"last_inspection_date\"\n+  )\n+\n+  dplyr::rename(df, dplyr::any_of(rename_map))\n+}\n+\n+# ── DMCP data cleaning ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n+#\n+# Applies facility/city name standardization and numeric type coercion to a\n+# DMCP listing that has already been through rename_dmcp_columns().\n+# Reuses clean_facility_names() and fix_city_names() from this file.\n+\n+clean_dmcp_data <- function(df) {\n+  # ADP columns arrive as numeric (xlsx, already parsed by readxl) or as\n+  # character strings with commas (pdf). This helper handles both.\n+  parse_adp <- function(x) {\n+    if (is.numeric(x)) return(round(x, 1))\n+    as.numeric(stringr::str_remove_all(as.character(x), \",\"))\n+  }\n+\n+  adp_cols <- dplyr::intersect(\n+    names(df),\n+    c(\"fy17_adp\", \"fy16_adp\", \"fy15_adp\", \"fy14_adp\",\n+      \"fy13_adp\", \"fy12_adp\", \"fy11_adp\", \"fy10_adp\",\n+      \"facility_population_count\")\n+  )\n+\n+  df |>\n+    dplyr::mutate(\n+      dplyr::across(dplyr::all_of(adp_cols), parse_adp),\n+      facility_capacity = as.integer(\n+        stringr::str_remove_all(as.character(facility_capacity), \",\")\n+      ),\n+      facility_name    = clean_facility_names(facility_name),\n+      facility_city    = stringr::str_to_title(facility_city),\n+      facility_address = stringr::str_to_title(facility_address),\n+      facility_city    = fix_city_names(facility_city),\n+      # Normalize whitespace in all remaining character columns\n+      dplyr::across(where(is.character), stringr::str_squish)\n+    ) |>\n+    dplyr::filter(!is.na(facility_city), !is.na(facility_state))\n+}\n+\n # ── Footnote extraction and saving ───────────────────────────────────────────\n # Footnote/non-facility rows are identified before cleaning by the absence of\n # facility_city and facility_state, which are always present for real rows.\n","language":"diff"}}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01GA1iAqXKbPAhRCYKWnAUWZ"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"text","value":"Updated /Users/bjorkjcr/Dropbox (Personal)/R/ice-detention/R/clean.R"},"elapsedMs":434}</script></tool-result></div>

Now let's verify both functions work end-to-end against the live data:

<div><tool-call id="toolu_0131x19NeDTA211MrKZRsxNJ"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"runCode","input":{"code":"library(targets)\ntar_source()  # Re-source all R/ files\n\n# Test the full DMCP pipeline for...[truncated, total 518 chars]","intent":"Verify the full import + rename + clean pipeline for both DMCP sources"},"intent":"Verify the full import + rename + clean pipeline for both DMCP sources","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"library(targets)\ntar_source()  # Re-source all R/ files\n\n# Test the full DMCP pipeline for both sources\nfy15 <- import_fy15_dmcp() |> rename_dmcp_columns() |> clean_dmcp_data()\nfy17 <- import_fy17_dmcp() |> rename_dmcp_columns() |> clean_dmcp_data()\n\ncat(\"FY15 DMCP:\", nrow(fy15), \"rows,\", ncol(fy15), \"cols\\n\")\ncat(\"FY17 DMCP:\", nrow(fy17), \"rows,\", ncol(fy17), \"cols\\n\\n\")\n\n# Confirm column schemas\ncat(\"FY15 columns:\\n\"); cat(names(fy15), sep = \"\\n\"); cat(\"\\n\")\ncat(\"FY17 columns:\\n\"); cat(names(fy17), sep = \"\\n\")\n","language":"r"}}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_0131x19NeDTA211MrKZRsxNJ"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"content","value":[{"type":"text","text":"Output:\n\r-\r/\r                                                                             ...[truncated, total 1760 chars]"}]},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"-\r/\r                                                                                                       \r\r/\r                                                                                                       \r\r-\r                                                                                                       \rFY15 DMCP: 209 rows, 38 cols\nFY17 DMCP: 201 rows, 40 cols\n\nFY15 columns:\ndetloc\nfacility_name\nfacility_address\nfacility_city\nfacility_county\nfacility_state\nfacility_zip\nfacility_type\nfacility_type_detailed\nfacility_male_female\nfacility_levels\nfacility_capacity\nfacility_population_count\nfy16_adp\nfy15_adp\nfy14_adp\nfy13_adp\nfy12_adp\nfy11_adp\nfy10_adp\nfacility_operator\nfacility_owner\ncontract_initiation_date\ncontract_expiration_date\nper_diem_rate_detailed\nauthorizing_authority\nover_under_72\ninspections_last_inspection_type\ninspections_last_inspection_standard\ninspections_last_inspection_rating_final\ninspections_last_inspection_date\ncy15_rating\ncy14_rating\ncy13_rating\ncy12_rating\ncy11_rating\ncy10_rating\ndsm_assigned\n\nFY17 columns:\nfacility_address\nauthorizing_authority\nfacility_capacity\nfacility_city\ncontract_expiration_date\ncontract_initiation_date\nfacility_county\ncy10_rating\ncy11_rating\ncy12_rating\ncy13_rating\ncy14_rating\ncy15_rating\ncy16_rating\ndetloc\ndsm_assigned\nfacility_operator\nfacility_owner\nfy10_adp\nfy11_adp\nfy12_adp\nfy13_adp\nfy14_adp\nfy15_adp\nfy16_adp\nfy17_adp\ninspections_last_inspection_date\ninspections_last_inspection_rating_final\ninspections_last_inspection_standard\ninspections_last_inspection_type\nfacility_levels\nfacility_male_female\nfacility_name\nover_under_72\nper_diem_rate_detailed\nfacility_population_count\nfacility_state\nfacility_type\nfacility_type_detailed\nfacility_zip"},"elapsedMs":3020}</script></tool-result></div>



<div><thinking id="thinking-1774111342139-r7goz">
The functions work correctly. The FY17 columns are just in a different order (because the PDF parser uses `pivot_wider()` which creates columns in order of first appearance, and the DETLOC and other identity columns appear at different y positions). Let me spot-check a few rows to verify the name cleaning is working.
</thinking></div>

Both pipelines run cleanly. The FY17 columns are alphabetically scrambled from `pivot_wider` — worth fixing the column order so both datasets have the same layout. Let me do that and do a spot-check.

<div><tool-call id="toolu_01HaX2KnkvEHTPgdgBXKUZH9"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"runCode","input":{"code":"# FY15 defines the canonical column order (it's the older/simpler source).\n# FY17 adds fy1...[truncated, total 1520 chars]","intent":"Reorder FY17 columns and spot-check shared facilities between the two sources"},"intent":"Reorder FY17 columns and spot-check shared facilities between the two sources","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"# FY15 defines the canonical column order (it's the older/simpler source).\n# FY17 adds fy17_adp and cy16_rating; insert them at the right positions.\ndmcp_col_order <- c(\n  \"detloc\", \"facility_name\", \"facility_address\", \"facility_city\",\n  \"facility_county\", \"facility_state\", \"facility_zip\",\n  \"facility_type\", \"facility_type_detailed\", \"facility_male_female\",\n  \"facility_levels\", \"facility_capacity\", \"facility_population_count\",\n  \"fy17_adp\", \"fy16_adp\", \"fy15_adp\", \"fy14_adp\", \"fy13_adp\",\n  \"fy12_adp\", \"fy11_adp\", \"fy10_adp\",\n  \"facility_operator\", \"facility_owner\",\n  \"contract_initiation_date\", \"contract_expiration_date\",\n  \"per_diem_rate_detailed\", \"authorizing_authority\", \"over_under_72\",\n  \"inspections_last_inspection_type\", \"inspections_last_inspection_standard\",\n  \"inspections_last_inspection_rating_final\", \"inspections_last_inspection_date\",\n  \"cy16_rating\", \"cy15_rating\", \"cy14_rating\", \"cy13_rating\",\n  \"cy12_rating\", \"cy11_rating\", \"cy10_rating\", \"dsm_assigned\"\n)\n\nreorder_dmcp <- function(df) dplyr::select(df, dplyr::any_of(dmcp_col_order))\n\nfy15 <- fy15 |> reorder_dmcp()\nfy17 <- fy17 |> reorder_dmcp()\n\n# Spot check: compare a few shared facilities\ndplyr::inner_join(\n  dplyr::select(fy15, detloc, facility_name, facility_city, facility_state,\n                facility_type_detailed, facility_capacity, facility_operator),\n  dplyr::select(fy17, detloc, facility_name, facility_capacity,\n                facility_operator, fy17_adp),\n  by = \"detloc\",\n  suffix = c(\"_fy15\", \"_fy17\")\n) |> head(8)\n","language":"r"}}</script></tool-call></div>

FY15 has 38 of these; FY17 adds fy17_adp\n+# and cy16_rating (40 total).\n+.dmcp_col_order <- c(\n+  \"detloc\", \"facility_name\", \"facility_address\", \"facility_city\",\n+  \"facility_county\", \"facility_state\", \"facility_zip\",\n+  \"facility_type\", \"facility_type_detailed\", \"facility_male_female\",\n+  \"facility_levels\", \"facility_capacity\", \"facility_population_count\",\n+  \"fy17_adp\", \"fy16_adp\", \"fy15_adp\", \"fy14_adp\", \"fy13_adp\",\n+  \"fy12_adp\", \"fy11_adp\", \"fy10_adp\",\n+  \"facility_operator\", \"facility_owner\",\n+  \"contract_initiation_date\", \"contract_expiration_date\",\n+  \"per_diem_rate_detailed\", \"authorizing_authority\", \"over_under_72\",\n+  \"inspections_last_inspection_type\", \"inspections_last_inspection_standard\",\n+  \"inspections_last_inspection_rating_final\", \"inspections_last_inspection_date\",\n+  \"cy16_rating\", \"cy15_rating\", \"cy14_rating\", \"cy13_rating\",\n+  \"cy12_rating\", \"cy11_rating\", \"cy10_rating\", \"dsm_assigned\"\n+)\n+\n # ── Footnote extraction and saving ───────────────────────────────────────────\n # Footnote/non-facility rows are identified before cleaning by the absence of\n # facility_city and facility_state, which are always present for real rows.\n","language":"diff"}}</script></tool-call></div>

Everything is clean. Here's a summary of what was done and the key decisions made:

**`R/import.R` additions:**
- `import_fy15_dmcp()` — downloads and reads the XLSX, skipping 6 metadata rows to land on the real header at row 7
- `import_fy17_dmcp()` — downloads and parses the PDF using `pdftools::pdf_data()` with `imap` + the page parser
- `.parse_dmcp_pdf_page()`, `.dmcp_pdf_col_breaks`, `.dmcp_pdf_col_names` — PDF parsing helpers (dot-prefixed as internal)

**`R/clean.R` additions:**
- `rename_dmcp_columns()` — **new function**; handles both sources in a single pass: (1) normalize to snake_case, (2) strip `best_known_` prefix (xlsx artifact), (3) unify `levels_a_b_c_d`→`levels`, (4) apply `facility_`/`inspections_` prefixes via `any_of()`
- `clean_dmcp_data()` — **new function**; reuses the existing `clean_facility_names()` and `fix_city_names()` unchanged, adds numeric coercion that handles both numeric-from-xlsx and comma-string-from-pdf, enforces column order via `.dmcp_col_order`

**On the overlap question**: the DMCP lists share identity and inspection columns with the main panel (harmonized naming now matches), but their defining value is the columns the panel doesn't have: `facility_operator`, `facility_owner`, contract dates, per diem rates, longitudinal FY10–17 ADP, annual inspection ratings (CY10–16), and `dsm_assigned`. Joining them to the panel will require the crosswalk — that's the natural next step to discuss.

## Databot



<div><thinking id="thinking-1774111500869-jmxl2">
The user wants a summary of the three completed tasks. Let me provide a concise but thorough summary.
</thinking></div>

**Assessing `clean_facility_names()` and `fix_city_names()` for the new sources**

Both functions are pure string transformers that start with `str_to_title()`, so they handle all-caps input (as found in both the 2015 XLSX and 2017 PDF) without any modification. No adaptation was needed — they're already general-purpose utilities. The wrapper `clean_facilities_data()`, however, is specific to the FY19–FY26 stats schema (hardcoded column names like `facility_name`, `facility_city`, etc.), so new functions were written for the DMCP sources rather than trying to extend the existing one.

**Comparing `pdf_2017` variables against the panel schema**

The DMCP lists and the FY panel are complementary, not parallel. Overlapping concepts include facility identity columns (`name`, `address`, `city`, `state`, `zip`, `type_detailed`, `male_female`) and the last-inspection block — all of which map directly to existing `facility_*` and `inspections_last_inspection_*` names. The DMCP "Capacity" column is *not* the same as `inspections_guaranteed_minimum` in the panel (total authorized beds vs. contracted minimum), so it gets its own name: `facility_capacity`. Everything else in the DMCP lists — `facility_operator`, `facility_owner`, contract dates, per diem rates, historical ADP by fiscal year, annual CY inspection ratings, `over_under_72`, `dsm_assigned` — has no equivalent in the panel and represents the unique value these sources add.

**Harmonizing column names**

Two new functions were added. `rename_dmcp_columns()` in `clean.R` handles both sources with a single code path: it first normalizes all names to snake_case (a no-op for the PDF parser output, which already produces snake_case; active for the XLSX raw names), strips the `best_known_` prefix from the contract date columns (an xlsx-only artifact), unifies the `levels_a_b_c_d` vs. `levels` discrepancy, and then applies a project-schema rename map using `dplyr::any_of()` to silently skip columns absent from either source. `clean_dmcp_data()` wraps the numeric coercion (handling both readxl-parsed numerics and comma-formatted strings from the PDF), applies the existing name/city cleaning utilities, and enforces a canonical 40-column order via `.dmcp_col_order`. The FY15 source produces 38 of those 40 columns; FY17 adds `fy17_adp` and `cy16_rating`.

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_016v11d6HSTrE5qjrVwx5S7h"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"text","value":"[File: 8.9KB, 224 lines, showing lines 1-224]\n     1→# Import facility data from ICE sprea...[truncated, total 9790 chars]"},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"[File: 8.9KB, 224 lines, showing lines 1-224]\n# Import facility data from ICE spreadsheets and supplemental DMCP listings.\n#\n# Annual FY stats (FY19–FY26): two-step process:\n#   1. Read the two-row headers and combine into clean variable names.\n#   2. Read the data rows using those names.\n#\n# Supplemental DMCP listings (2015 XLSX, 2017 PDF): each is a separate\n# point-in-time roster of authorized facilities with contract and historical\n# ADP data across multiple fiscal years per row. See rename_dmcp_columns()\n# in clean.R for the column harmonization step.\n\n# ── Header extraction ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nheader_rows <- function(data_file_info_row) {\n  with(data_file_info_row, {\n    first_row <- readxl::read_excel(\n      local_file,\n      sheet = sheet_name,\n      range = readxl::cell_limits(\n        ul = c(first_header_row, 1),\n        lr = c(first_header_row, right_column_num)\n      ),\n      col_names = FALSE\n    )\n    second_row <- readxl::read_excel(\n      local_file,\n      sheet = sheet_name,\n      range = readxl::cell_limits(\n        ul = c(second_header_row, 1),\n        lr = c(second_header_row, right_column_num)\n      ),\n      col_names = FALSE\n    )\n    rbind(first_row, second_row)\n  })\n}\n\n# ── Variable name cleaning ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nclean_variable_names_from_header <- function(raw_headers) {\n  clean_names <- raw_headers |>\n    t() |>\n    as.data.frame() |>\n    tidyr::fill(V1) |>\n    dplyr::mutate(\n      V1 = stringr::str_trim(stringr::str_remove_all(V1, \"FY\\\\d\\\\d\")),\n      V1 = stringr::str_remove_all(V1, \":\"),\n      V2 = stringr::str_trim(stringr::str_remove_all(V2, \"FY\\\\d\\\\d\")),\n      V2 = stringr::str_remove_all(V2, \":\"),\n      V1 = dplyr::case_when(\n        stringr::str_detect(V1, \"This list\") ~ \"Facility\",\n        V1 == \"Facility Information\" ~ \"Facility\",\n        V1 == \"ADP Detainee Classification Level\" ~ \"ADP Detainee Classification\",\n        V1 == \"ADP Detainee Security Level\" ~ \"ADP Detainee Security\",\n        V1 == \"ADP ICE Threat Level\" ~ \"ADP\",\n        V1 == \"ADP Mandatory\" ~ \"ADP\",\n        V1 == \"Contract Facility Inspections Information\" ~ \"Inspections\",\n        TRUE ~ V1\n      ),\n      combined = paste(V1, V2),\n      var_name = tolower(combined),\n      var_name = stringr::str_replace_all(var_name, \"[^a-z0-9]+\", \"_\"),\n      var_name = stringr::str_remove_all(var_name, \"^_+|_+$\")\n    ) |>\n    dplyr::pull(var_name)\n\n  clean_names\n}\n\n# ── Build clean names for all years ──────────────────────────────────────────\n\nbuild_clean_names <- function(data_file_info) {\n  raw_headers_list <- purrr::map(seq_len(nrow(data_file_info)), function(i) {\n    header_rows(data_file_info[i, ])\n  })\n  names(raw_headers_list) <- data_file_info$year_name\n\n  clean_names_list <- purrr::map(raw_headers_list, clean_variable_names_from_header)\n  names(clean_names_list) <- data_file_info$year_name\n  clean_names_list\n}\n\n# ── Read facility data for one year ──────────────────────────────────────────\n\nread_facilities_data <- function(data_file_info_row, clean_names_list) {\n  with(data_file_info_row, {\n    readxl::read_xlsx(\n      local_file,\n      sheet = sheet_name,\n      range = readxl::cell_limits(\n        ul = c(first_data_row, 1),\n        lr = c(NA, right_column_num)\n      ),\n      col_names = clean_names_list[[year_name]]\n    )\n  })\n}\n\n# ── Import all years ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nimport_all_years <- function(data_file_info, clean_names_list) {\n  facilities_data_list <- purrr::map(seq_len(nrow(data_file_info)), function(i) {\n    read_facilities_data(data_file_info[i, ], clean_names_list)\n  })\n  names(facilities_data_list) <- data_file_info$year_name\n  facilities_data_list\n}\n\n# ── Supplemental DMCP facility listings ──────────────────────────────────────\n#\n# The DMCP (Detention Management Compliance Program) lists are authorization\n# rosters that capture contract, operator, and multi-year ADP data for each\n# facility in a single row. They are a different document type from the annual\n# FY stats files and are handled by their own import functions.\n#\n# Both functions return raw data with minimal transformation. Apply\n# rename_dmcp_columns() + clean_dmcp_data() (from clean.R) as the next step.\n\n# ── Import 2015 DMCP facility listing (XLSX) ─────────────────────────────────\n\nimport_fy15_dmcp <- function() {\n  path <- here::here(\"data/2015IceDetentionFacilityListing.xlsx\")\n  url  <- \"https://www.ice.gov/doclib/foia/dfs/2015IceDetentionFacilityListing.xlsx\"\n\n  if (!file.exists(path)) {\n    message(\"Downloading \", basename(path), \" ...\")\n    httr::GET(url, httr::write_disk(path, overwrite = FALSE))\n  }\n\n  # Rows 1–6 are document title/metadata. Row 7 is the column header row.\n  # Row 8+ is facility data.\n  readxl::read_xlsx(\n    path,\n    sheet = \"Facility List - Main\",\n    skip  = 6,\n    col_names = TRUE\n  ) |>\n    # Drop blank trailing rows (no state or city)\n    dplyr::filter(!is.na(State), !is.na(City))\n}\n\n# ── Column layout constants for the 2017 DMCP PDF ────────────────────────────\n#\n# The PDF is a fixed-width table. Column boundaries are derived from the\n# bounding-box x-coordinates of header tokens on page 1 (y = 63).\n# Raw names use snake_case; rename_dmcp_columns() maps them to the project\n# schema.\n\n.dmcp_pdf_col_breaks <- c(\n  0, 34, 101, 151, 179, 206, 218, 228, 242, 260, 282, 298, 317,\n  337, 349, 361, 373, 385, 397, 409, 422, 437, 469, 483, 510, 536,\n  622, 642, 669, 687, 706, 734, 754, 782, 811, 840, 869, 898, 920, 944,\n  Inf\n)\n\n.dmcp_pdf_col_names <- c(\n  \"detloc\", \"name\", \"address\", \"city\", \"county\", \"state\", \"zip\",\n  \"type\", \"type_detailed\", \"male_female\", \"levels\",\n  \"capacity\", \"population_count\",\n  \"fy17_adp\", \"fy16_adp\", \"fy15_adp\", \"fy14_adp\", \"fy13_adp\",\n  \"fy12_adp\", \"fy11_adp\", \"fy10_adp\",\n  \"facility_operator\", \"facility_owner\",\n  \"contract_initiation_date\", \"contract_expiration_date\",\n  \"per_diem_rate_detailed\", \"authorizing_authority\",\n  \"over_under_72\", \"last_inspection_type\", \"last_inspection_standard\",\n  \"last_inspection_rating_final\", \"last_inspection_date\",\n  \"cy16_rating\", \"cy15_rating\", \"cy14_rating\", \"cy13_rating\",\n  \"cy12_rating\", \"cy11_rating\", \"cy10_rating\", \"dsm_assigned\"\n)\n\n# ── Parse one page of the 2017 DMCP PDF ──────────────────────────────────────\n#\n# Uses pdftools::pdf_data() bounding-box coordinates to reconstruct the table.\n# data_y_min: lowest y pixel to treat as data (skips the column header rows).\n# On page 1, the header occupies y ≤ 65; other pages start with data at y = 1.\n\n.parse_dmcp_pdf_page <- function(page_tokens, data_y_min) {\n  assign_col <- function(x_val) {\n    idx <- findInterval(x_val, .dmcp_pdf_col_breaks, rightmost.closed = FALSE)\n    .dmcp_pdf_col_names[idx]\n  }\n\n  toks <- page_tokens |>\n    dplyr::filter(y >= data_y_min) |>\n    dplyr::arrange(y, x) |>\n    dplyr::mutate(col = assign_col(x))\n\n  if (nrow(toks) == 0) return(tibble::tibble())\n\n  # New record when y-gap > 1 pt. Each facility spans a main row and a\n  # DETLOC sub-row offset by 1 pt; inter-record gap is ≥ 2 pt.\n  toks <- toks |>\n    dplyr::mutate(record_id = cumsum(c(TRUE, diff(y) > 1)))\n\n  toks |>\n    dplyr::group_by(record_id, col) |>\n    dplyr::summarise(value = paste(text, collapse = \" \"), .groups = \"drop\") |>\n    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = col, values_from = value, values_fill = NA) |>\n    dplyr::select(-record_id)\n}\n\n# ── Import 2017 DMCP facility list (PDF) ─────────────────────────────────────\n\nimport_fy17_dmcp <- function() {\n  path <- here::here(\"data/ICE_DMCP_Facility_List_2017.pdf\")\n  url  <- \"https://www.prisonlegalnews.org/media/publications/ICE_DMCP_Facility_List_ERO_Custody_Management_Division_2017.pdf\"\n\n  if (!file.exists(path)) {\n    message(\"Downloading \", basename(path), \" ...\")\n    download.file(url, path, mode = \"wb\", quiet = TRUE)\n  }\n\n  pdf_dat <- pdftools::pdf_data(path)\n\n  purrr::imap(\n    pdf_dat,\n    ~ .parse_dmcp_pdf_page(.x, data_y_min = if (.y == 1L) 66L else 1L)\n  ) |>\n    dplyr::bind_rows() |>\n    # Keep only rows with a valid DETLOC code (all-caps alphanumeric, no spaces)\n    # and a non-missing state; filters out page headers/footers/footnotes.\n    dplyr::filter(grepl(\"^[A-Z0-9]+$\", detloc), !is.na(state))\n}\n"},"elapsedMs":62}</script></tool-result></div>

Now I have a clear picture. Let me assess what's needed:

**What's in place:**
1. `import_fy15_dmcp()` - reads XLSX (with embedded download logic)
2. `import_fy17_dmcp()` - parses PDF (with embedded download logic)
3. `rename_dmcp_columns()` - column renaming
4. `clean_dmcp_data()` - cleaning

**What's missing for a proper targets pipeline:**
The download step is embedded inside the import functions. For a proper targets pipeline following the existing pattern:
- The download should be a separate target with `format = "file"` and `cue = "never"` so targets tracks the file


- The import function should receive the file path as an argument

So I need to refactor this by creating separate download functions in `R/download.R` that handle the file retrieval, then modify the import functions to accept file paths as parameters, and finally wire everything together in `_targets.R`. I'm creating download functions that handle the file retrieval logic... Writing the import functions...

Now I'm thinking through the pipeline structure for the targets file—I need to set up download targets for both the FY15 and FY17 DMCP files, then raw import targets, and finally cleaned versions of each dataset.

I should also add `pdftools` to the package dependencies in `tar_option_set()`. Looking at how the FY stats pipeline handles this, I notice they have a separate metadata stage for headers, but DMCP doesn't need that since the column names come directly from the source files—the 2015 XLSX skips to row 7 for headers, the PDF parser has hardcoded names, and the rename function handles any harmonization needed.

So my pipeline is simpler: download → raw import → clean, which is three stages compared to the five-stage FY stats approach. Now I'm moving on to document this in a YAML sidecar file, following the pattern from the reference example that captures source metadata like institution and URL.

My next steps are to update the download and import functions to accept path arguments, add the new targets to the pipeline, write the YAML documentation, and verify everything loads correctly. I already have the row and column counts I need for the YAML—209 rows and 38 columns for the 2015 file, 201 for the 2017 file. I'm noticing the data currency dates embedded in the files—the FY15 XLSX shows December 2015 and the FY17 PDF shows July 2017—so I'll put those in the notes rather than the import date. Now I'm ready to write out the refactored import functions, add the download logic, update the targets file, and structure the YAML with separate sections for each dataset plus an import section. I'm refactoring the import functions to accept path arguments so they can work with different file locations dynamically.
</thinking></div>

One thing is missing for a proper targets pipeline: **the download logic is embedded inside the import functions**. In the existing pattern, download is a separate `format = "file"` target with `cue = "never"`, and the import function receives the path as an argument. I need to:

1. Add download functions to `download.R`
2. Refactor the import functions in `import.R` to accept a `path` argument
3. Add targets to `_targets.R`
4. Write the YAML sidecar

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01C7zzFZaerV8HtSLQBM349D"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"text","value":"[File: 674B, 22 lines, showing lines 1-22]\n     1→# Download ICE detention spreadsheets fr...[truncated, total 871 chars]"},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"[File: 674B, 22 lines, showing lines 1-22]\n# Download ICE detention spreadsheets from ice.gov.\n#\n# Only downloads files that don't already exist locally.\n# Returns the vector of local file paths (for targets file tracking).\n\ndownload_ice_spreadsheets <- function(data_file_info) {\n  for (i in seq_len(nrow(data_file_info))) {\n    local <- data_file_info$local_file[i]\n    if (!file.exists(local)) {\n      message(\"Downloading \", basename(local), \" ...\")\n      httr::GET(\n        data_file_info$url[i],\n        httr::write_disk(local, overwrite = FALSE)\n      )\n    } else {\n      message(\"Already exists: \", basename(local))\n    }\n  }\n  # Return paths so targets can track them as files\n  data_file_info$local_file\n}\n"},"elapsedMs":559}</script></tool-result></div>
