<div><tool-call id="toolu_01DdJZRYQMyCmVG7eJRy2FeU"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"write","input":{"file_path":"/Users/bjorkjcr/Dropbox (Personal)/R/ice-detention/data/dmcp-listings.yml","content":"---\n# Provenance metadata for ICE DMCP Authorized Facility Listings\n# Two point-in-time sn...[truncated, total 6694 chars]"},"caption":"data/dmcp-listings.yml","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"---\n# Provenance metadata for ICE DMCP Authorized Facility Listings\n# Two point-in-time snapshots: 2015 XLSX from ice.gov, 2017 PDF from Prison Legal News.\n\nsource:\n  title: \"ERO Custody Management Division — Authorized DMCP Facility List\"\n  institution: \"U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), Enforcement and Removal Operations\"\n  program: \"Detention Management Compliance Program (DMCP)\"\n  notes: >\n    These documents are authorization rosters, not annual snapshots. Each row\n    represents one authorized facility and contains contract metadata, operator\n    and owner information, and ADP figures across multiple prior fiscal years.\n    They are a different document type from the annual FY stats spreadsheets\n    (FY19–FY26) that form the main pipeline panel.\n\nfy15_listing:\n  title: \"2015 ICE Detention Facility Listing\"\n  url: \"https://www.ice.gov/doclib/foia/dfs/2015IceDetentionFacilityListing.xlsx\"\n  obtained_via: \"ICE FOIA reading room (ice.gov/foia)\"\n  format: \"XLSX\"\n  data_as_of: \"2015-12-08\"\n  data_currency: >\n    FY2016 data: IIDS as of 12/08/2015; EID data through approximately 10/2/2015.\n    FY2015 and prior years reflect end-of-fiscal-year snapshots.\n  fiscal_years_covered: [2010, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016]\n\n  raw_file:\n    filename: \"data/2015IceDetentionFacilityListing.xlsx\"\n    sheet: \"Facility List - Main\"\n    header_rows: \"Rows 1–6 are document title and metadata; row 7 is the column header row; data begins at row 8.\"\n    rows: 209\n    columns: 38\n\n  outputs:\n    - target: fy15_dmcp_raw\n      description: >\n        Raw tibble read directly from the XLSX with original column names\n        (Title Case with spaces, e.g. \"Type Detailed\", \"Male/Female\").\n        209 rows × 38 columns. Blank trailing rows filtered on State/City.\n    - target: fy15_dmcp\n      description: >\n        Cleaned tibble with project-schema column names (snake_case,\n        facility_/inspections_ prefixes). Facility names title-cased and\n        abbreviations expanded. ADP columns as numeric. 209 rows × 38 columns.\n      columns_added_vs_raw:\n        - \"None — renaming and type coercion only.\"\n      columns_not_in_fy_panel: >\n        detloc, facility_county, facility_type, facility_levels,\n        facility_capacity, facility_population_count, fy10_adp–fy16_adp,\n        facility_operator, facility_owner, contract_initiation_date,\n        contract_expiration_date, per_diem_rate_detailed,\n        authorizing_authority, over_under_72, cy10_rating–cy15_rating,\n        dsm_assigned\n\nfy17_listing:\n  title: \"ICE DMCP Facility List — ERO Custody Management Division, FY2017\"\n  url: \"https://www.prisonlegalnews.org/media/publications/ICE_DMCP_Facility_List_ERO_Custody_Management_Division_2017.pdf\"\n  obtained_via: \"Prison Legal News document library\"\n  original_source: \"ICE / ERO Custody Management Division\"\n  format: \"PDF (4 pages, fixed-width table)\"\n  data_as_of: \"2017-07-10\"\n  data_currency: >\n    FY2017 data: IIDS as of 07/10/2017; EID data through 07/08/2017.\n    FY2016 and prior years reflect end-of-fiscal-year snapshots.\n  fiscal_years_covered: [2010, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, 2017]\n\n  raw_file:\n    filename: \"data/ICE_DMCP_Facility_List_2017.pdf\"\n    pages: 4\n    rows_parsed: 271\n    rows_after_filter: 201\n    columns: 40\n\n  parsing_method: >\n    Extracted using pdftools::pdf_data(), which returns per-token bounding-box\n    coordinates. Tokens are assigned to columns by x-coordinate intervals\n    derived from header token positions on page 1 (y = 63). Records are grouped\n    by y-gap: a gap > 1 pt starts a new record (each facility spans a main row\n    plus a DETLOC sub-row offset by 1 pt; inter-record gap is ≥ 2 pt).\n    Page 1 data starts at y = 66 (below the multi-row column header); pages\n    2–4 start at y = 1. Column boundary constants are stored in\n    .dmcp_pdf_col_breaks in R/import.R.\n\n  filtering:\n    valid_detloc: \"Rows retained only where detloc matches ^[A-Z0-9]+$ (all-caps alphanumeric, no spaces).\"\n    non_null_state: \"Rows retained only where state is not NA.\"\n    removed: >\n      70 rows removed: page header/footer text, column header repetitions,\n      and footnote paragraphs that were parsed as records before filtering.\n\n  outputs:\n    - target: fy17_dmcp_raw\n      description: >\n        Raw tibble from PDF parsing, snake_case column names as assigned\n        by the PDF parser. 201 rows × 40 columns.\n    - target: fy17_dmcp\n      description: >\n        Cleaned tibble with project-schema column names. Facility names\n        title-cased and abbreviations expanded. ADP columns parsed from\n        comma-formatted character strings to numeric. Columns in canonical\n        DMCP order (.dmcp_col_order in R/clean.R). 201 rows × 40 columns.\n      columns_vs_fy15: >\n        Adds fy17_adp and cy16_rating relative to the FY15 listing.\n        All other columns are identical in name and meaning.\n\nimport:\n  pipeline: \"_targets.R\"\n  functions:\n    download: \"download_fy15_dmcp(), download_fy17_dmcp() — R/download.R\"\n    import:   \"import_fy15_dmcp(path), import_fy17_dmcp(path) — R/import.R\"\n    rename:   \"rename_dmcp_columns() — R/clean.R\"\n    clean:    \"clean_dmcp_data() — R/clean.R\"\n  targets:\n    - fy15_dmcp_file   # format: file, cue: never\n    - fy17_dmcp_file   # format: file, cue: never\n    - fy15_dmcp_raw\n    - fy17_dmcp_raw\n    - fy15_dmcp\n    - fy17_dmcp\n  date: \"2026-03-21\"\n  handling_notes:\n    - >\n      Both sources share the same DMCP schema. The FY17 PDF adds two columns\n      (fy17_adp, cy16_rating) relative to the FY15 XLSX. rename_dmcp_columns()\n      handles both with a single code path via dplyr::any_of().\n    - >\n      Column \"Capacity\" in the DMCP lists is total authorized beds, not the\n      contracted guaranteed minimum. It is named facility_capacity to\n      distinguish it from inspections_guaranteed_minimum in the FY panel.\n    - >\n      \"Best Known Contract Initiation/Expiration Date\" in the XLSX becomes\n      contract_initiation_date / contract_expiration_date (prefix stripped).\n      The PDF parser already uses the shorter names.\n    - >\n      FY15 XLSX ADP values are floating-point (readxl parses numeric cells\n      directly). FY17 PDF ADP values are character strings with commas\n      (e.g. \"1,876\"). clean_dmcp_data() handles both via parse_adp().\n    - >\n      Inspection columns are prefixed inspections_ to match the main panel:\n      last_inspection_type → inspections_last_inspection_type, etc.\n    - >\n      facility_type (broad ICE category: IGSA, CDF, SPC, etc.) is distinct\n      from facility_type_detailed (DIGSA, USMS IGA, USMS CDF, etc.). Both are\n      retained; facility_type_detailed matches the main panel convention.\n"}}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-call id="toolu_01FxM5kZV9ZYbS73kFTHQWVV"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"todoWrite","input":{"todos":[{"content":"Add download_fy15_dmcp() and download_fy17_dmcp() to download.R","status":"completed"},{"content":"Refactor import functions to accept path argument (remove embedded download logic)","status":"completed"},{"content":"Add DMCP targets to _targets.R","status":"completed"},{"content":"Write YAML sidecar at data/dmcp-listings.yml","status":"completed"}]},"caption":"4 done"}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01FxM5kZV9ZYbS73kFTHQWVV"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"text","value":"Todo List Updated (4 items):\n✓ Add download_fy15_dmcp() and download_fy17_dmcp() to downlo...[truncated, total 307 chars]"},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"Todo List Updated (4 items):\n✓ Add download_fy15_dmcp() and download_fy17_dmcp() to download.R (completed)\n✓ Refactor import functions to accept path argument (remove embedded download logic) (completed)\n✓ Add DMCP targets to _targets.R (completed)\n✓ Write YAML sidecar at data/dmcp-listings.yml (completed)"},"elapsedMs":0}</script></tool-result></div>

Everything is done. Here's a summary of what was missing and what changed:

**The one missing piece** was the download/import separation. The functions previously combined downloading and reading into a single call, which prevents targets from tracking the files independently. The fix was: `download_fy15/17_dmcp()` → return the local path as a `format = "file"` target; `import_fy15/17_dmcp(path)` → accept that path as an argument. This matches the existing pattern used by `download_ice_spreadsheets()`.

**The full DMCP target chain** added to `_targets.R`:
```
fy15_dmcp_file  →  fy15_dmcp_raw  →  fy15_dmcp
fy17_dmcp_file  →  fy17_dmcp_raw  →  fy17_dmcp
```

There's no header-parsing stage equivalent to `clean_names_list` — it isn't needed because the XLSX uses a simple `skip = 6` read and the PDF parser has hardcoded column positions. The rename/clean work happens in the third target.

The YAML sidecar at `data/dmcp-listings.yml` documents both sources: their provenance, data currency dates, raw file structure, the PDF parsing method (including why filtering removes 70 rows), and all the handling decisions made during import.




## User

In the wiki-graph project, this text was in AGENTS.md.

So let's keep #2 and implement #3 for the first time with the two new data sources. Adjust the text below as needed and propose a version to me to add to this project's AGENTS.md.

## Metadata convention for imported data tables

When data is manually transcribed from a published source (e.g. scanning a table from a book or PDF), the project uses a three-file convention:

1. **Import script** (e.g. `molina-albo-2006.R`): Contains only the data and transformation code. Points to the metadata files via comments at the top.
2. **YAML sidecar** (e.g. `data/molina-albo-2006.yml`): Structured provenance metadata including bibliographic citation, import handling notes (how the raw image was transcribed), published notes (footnotes, formatting conventions from the original), and a list of output files.
3. **Frictionless Data package descriptor** (`data/datapackage.json`): Column-level schemas for the CSV outputs, following the [Frictionless Data](https://frictionlessdata.io/) `datapackage.json` spec. Includes source citation and license info.

### Naming conventions

- YAML sidecar: `data/<script-basename>.yml`
- CSV/RDS outputs: `data/<descriptive_name>.csv` / `.rds`
- Import script: `<author>-<year>.R` (project root)

### Current instances

| Source | Script | YAML | Outputs |
|--------|--------|------|---------|
| Molina Barrios & Albó (2006), Cuadro 8.1 | `molina-albo-2006.R` | `data/molina-albo-2006.yml` | `molina_albo_8_1_es.*`, `molina_albo_8_1_en.*` |
| OEP Elecciones Generales 2025 | `import-eg2025.R` | `data/import-eg2025.yml` | `eg2025_presidente_mesa.*` |

Free-text field; typically includes a guaranteed minimum (GM) bed count and tiered per-diem rates in USD (e.g. '1455 (GM); $111.92 (1-1455), $42.36 (1456-1940)').\"\n          },\n          {\n            \"name\": \"authorizing_authority\",\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"ICE authority authorizing the facility. Typically 'DMCP'.\",\n            \"constraints\": { \"enum\": [\"DMCP\", \"ERO Field\", \"ODP\"] }\n          },\n          {\n            \"name\": \"over_under_72\",\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Whether the facility is authorized to hold detainees for more or fewer than 72 hours.\",\n            \"constraints\": { \"enum\": [\"Over 72\", \"Under 72\"] }\n          },\n          {\n            \"name\": \"inspections_last_inspection_type\",\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Most recent inspection type. Values include Regular, ORSA (Office of Residential and Satellite Audit), Pre-Occupancy (PREOCC).\"\n          },\n          {\n            \"name\": \"inspections_last_inspection_standard\",\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Inspection standard applied. Values include PBNDS 2011 (Performance-Based National Detention Standards), PBNDS 2008, NDS (National Detention Standards), IFSA.\"\n          },\n          {\n            \"name\": \"inspections_last_inspection_rating_final\",\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Final rating from the most recent inspection. Values: Meets Standard, Acceptable, Superior, Does Not Meet Standard, Pending, Deficient.\"\n          },\n          {\n            \"name\": \"inspections_last_inspection_date\",\n            \"type\": \"date\",\n            \"format\": \"%Y-%m-%d\",\n            \"description\": \"Date of the most recent inspection.\"\n          },\n          {\n            \"name\": \"cy15_rating\",\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Annual inspection rating for calendar year 2015.\"\n          },\n          {\n            \"name\": \"cy14_rating\",\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Annual inspection rating for calendar year 2014.\"\n          },\n          {\n            \"name\": \"cy13_rating\",\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Annual inspection rating for calendar year 2013.\"\n          },\n          {\n            \"name\": \"cy12_rating\",\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Annual inspection rating for calendar year 2012.\"\n          },\n          {\n            \"name\": \"cy11_rating\",\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Annual inspection rating for calendar year 2011.\"\n          },\n          {\n            \"name\": \"cy10_rating\",\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Annual inspection rating for calendar year 2010.\"\n          },\n          {\n            \"name\": \"dsm_assigned\",\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Whether a Detention Service Manager is assigned to the facility.\",\n            \"constraints\": { \"enum\": [\"Yes\", \"No\"] }\n          }\n        ],\n        \"primaryKey\": \"detloc\"\n      }\n    },\n    {\n      \"name\": \"fy17_dmcp\",\n      \"path\": \"fy17_dmcp.csv\",\n      \"mediatype\": \"text/csv\",\n      \"description\": \"2017 DMCP authorized facility listing. 201 rows × 40 columns. Data as of 2017-07-10 (IIDS). Parsed from a 4-page fixed-width PDF using pdftools::pdf_data(). Generated by the fy17_dmcp target in _targets.R. Schema identical to fy15_dmcp plus fy17_adp and cy16_rating.\",\n      \"schema\": {\n        \"fields\": [\n          { \"name\": \"detloc\",                                   \"$ref\": \"#/resources/0/schema/fields/0\" },\n          { \"name\": \"facility_name\",                            \"$ref\": \"#/resources/0/schema/fields/1\" },\n          { \"name\": \"facility_address\",                         \"$ref\": \"#/resources/0/schema/fields/2\" },\n          { \"name\": \"facility_city\",                            \"$ref\": \"#/resources/0/schema/fields/3\" },\n          { \"name\": \"facility_county\",                          \"$ref\": \"#/resources/0/schema/fields/4\" },\n          { \"name\": \"facility_state\",                           \"$ref\": \"#/resources/0/schema/fields/5\" },\n          { \"name\": \"facility_zip\",                             \"$ref\": \"#/resources/0/schema/fields/6\" },\n          { \"name\": \"facility_type\",                            \"$ref\": \"#/resources/0/schema/fields/7\" },\n          { \"name\": \"facility_type_detailed\",                   \"$ref\": \"#/resources/0/schema/fields/8\" },\n          { Now for the proposed AGENTS.md text. A few things I noticed while writing the JSON that are worth flagging in the text:

1. **`$ref` in the second resource**: Frictionless Data doesn't natively support JSON Schema `$ref` for cross-resource field reuse — this is a documentation convenience, not a validated spec. Worth being explicit about that.
2. **`facility_zip` is numeric in R**: a known data quality issue that should be called out.
3. The "import script" concept from wiki-graph dissolves into the targets pipeline here, so item #1 needs to describe that clearly.

Here's the proposed AGENTS.md section:

---

```markdown
## Metadata convention for supplemental imported data tables

For data sources supplemental to the main FY annual stats pipeline (e.g. DMCP
authorization lists, external PDFs, manually transcribed tables), the project
uses a three-part convention:

1. **Pipeline targets**: No standalone import script. Each supplemental source
   has dedicated functions spread across three files, plus targets in
   `_targets.R`:
   - `R/download.R` — `download_{source}()`: downloads the file if absent,
     returns the local path. Used as a `format = "file"`, `cue = "never"`
     target.
   - `R/import.R` — `import_{source}(path)`: accepts the file path from the
     download target and returns a raw tibble with minimal transformation.
   - `R/clean.R` — `rename_{source}_columns()` and `clean_{source}_data()`:
     harmonize column names to the project schema and apply type coercion and
     string cleaning. The rename step reuses the general-purpose
     `clean_facility_names()` and `fix_city_names()` utilities.
   - `_targets.R` targets follow the naming convention `{source}_file`
     (download), `{source}_raw` (import), `{source}` (cleaned).

2. **YAML sidecar** (`data/<source-name>.yml`): Structured provenance metadata
   including bibliographic citation, data currency dates, raw file description,
   parsing method notes, filtering decisions, handling notes, and a list of
   pipeline targets and outputs.

3. **Frictionless Data package descriptor** (`data/datapackage.json`):
   Column-level schemas for CSV outputs, following the
   [Frictionless Data](https://frictionlessdata.io/) `datapackage.json` spec.
   Includes source citation and license info. The second resource in the
   current file uses JSON Schema `$ref` pointers to reuse field definitions
   from the first; this is a documentation convenience and is not validated by
   Frictionless tooling.

### Naming conventions

- YAML sidecar: `data/<source-name>.yml`
- CSV/RDS outputs: `data/<target-name>.csv` / `.rds`
- Download/import targets: `{source}_file`, `{source}_raw`, `{source}`
- Functions: `download_{source}()`, `import_{source}(path)`,
  `rename_{source}_columns()`, `clean_{source}_data()`

### Known data quality issues to watch for

- `facility_zip` is parsed as numeric by readxl, which silently drops leading
  zeros (affects ZIP codes in CT, MA, ME, NH, NJ, NY, RI, VT). Future cleaning
  should coerce to character with zero-padding.
- `facility_county` is not normalized to title case in the current pipeline
  (remains uppercase from the raw source).

### Current instances

| Source | Targets | YAML | datapackage resource |
|--------|---------|------|----------------------|
| ICE DMCP Facility Listings (2015 XLSX, 2017 PDF) | `fy15_dmcp`, `fy17_dmcp` | `data/dmcp-listings.yml` | `fy15_dmcp`, `fy17_dmcp` |
```

---

Two things to decide before adding this:

1. **The "known data quality issues" subsection** — I included it because `facility_zip` is a real bug that will affect any future geocoding or crosswalk work. But it might feel out of place in a metadata convention section. Alternatively, it could go in a separate note in the DMCP-specific part of AGENTS.md.

2. **The `$ref` note** — The current `datapackage.json` uses it for the second resource. If you'd prefer the JSON to be fully self-contained and strictly Frictionless-compliant, I can expand all the `$ref` fields in `fy17_dmcp` to be fully spelled out. The file would be longer but unambiguous.

For data sources that contribute to the main annual stats pipeline (e.g. including summary data from multiple prior fiscal years), the project
uses a three-part convention:

Items 1 -3 as listed

For future data sources supplemental to the main annual stats pipeline, the project uses a three-part convention.

1. Separate supplemental import added: Their import and processing functions will be contained in a single supplemental script named `<source-name>-<year>.R` Actually importing will be added to targets.R in a separate supplemental data section. Any data retrieval or downloading will not be automatically repeated.

Steps 2 and 3 as above.

\"adp_detainee_classification_level_b\",\n    \"adp_detainee_classification_level_c\",\n    \"adp_detainee_classification_level_d\",\n    \"adp_criminality_male_crim\",\n %in% names(facilities_data)]\n\n  facilities_data_clean <- facilities_data |>\n    dplyr::mutate(\n      dplyr::across(\n        .cols = facility_average_length_of_stay_alos:adp_mandatory,\n clean_facility_names(facility_name),\n      facility_city = stringr::str_to_title(facility_city),\n      facility_address = stringr::str_to_title(facility_address),\n      facility_male_female = <div><tool-result call-id="toolu_016EEGMBeDXaqfd7znm4vepU"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"text","value":"[File: 9.0KB, 157 lines, showing lines 1-157]\n     1→# ice-detention project memory\n     2...[truncated, total 10337 chars]"},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"[File: 9.0KB, 157 lines, showing lines 1-157]\n# ice-detention project memory\n\n## Related projects\n\nThe **wiki-graph** project at `~/Dropbox (Personal)/R/wiki-graph/` is the original source for `import-ice-detention.qmd`, from which this pipeline was extracted and refactored. Agents should feel free to read files in that folder for reference (e.g., original Qmd code, `AGENTS.md`, conversation plans).\n\n## Project overview\n\n`targets`-based R pipeline for importing, cleaning, and harmonizing 8 years of U.S. ICE detention facility statistics (FY 2019–FY 2026). Extracted and refactored from `import-ice-detention.qmd` in the `wiki-graph` project.\n\n## File system conventions\n\n- Project root: `~/Dropbox (Personal)/R/ice-detention/`\n- Raw data and derived outputs in `data/`\n- All pipeline functions in `R/` (auto-sourced by `tar_source()`)\n- Use `here::here()` for paths in scripts outside `_targets.R`\n\n## Data sources\n\nAll raw spreadsheets live in `data/`:\n\n| File | Fiscal Year |\n|------|-------------|\n| `FY19-detentionstats.xlsx` | FY 2019 |\n| `FY20-detentionstats.xlsx` | FY 2020 |\n| `FY21-detentionstats.xlsx` | FY 2021 |\n| `FY22-detentionStats.xlsx` | FY 2022 |\n| `FY23_detentionStats.xlsx` | FY 2023 |\n| `FY24_detentionStats.xlsx` | FY 2024 |\n| `FY25_detentionStats09242025.xlsx` | FY 2025 |\n| `FY26_detentionStats02022026.xlsx` | FY 2026 |\n\nSource URL pattern: `https://www.ice.gov/doclib/detention/FY{nn}-detentionstats.xlsx`\n\nEach file has a \"Facilities FY{nn}\" sheet with a 2-row merged header. Header row positions and rightmost data column vary by year — all encoded in `build_data_file_info()`.\n\n## Derived data outputs (in `data/`)\n\n| File | Description |\n|------|-------------|\n| `facility_crosswalk.rds/.csv` | Maps every (name, city, state) variant to `canonical_id` / `canonical_name` |\n| `facility_presence.rds/.csv` | One row per canonical facility; FY19–FY26 booleans + trajectory classification |\n| `facilities_panel.rds/.csv` | Long panel: one row per facility per year, all ~30 measurement variables |\n| `facilities_fy26_wiki_matches.rds` | FY26 rows with Wikipedia slug and management columns added |\n| `contractors-patch-fy26.csv` | Manual management company corrections for 98 FY26 facilities |\n| `ice-detention-data-dictionary-FY26.md/.csv` | Variable definitions and acronym guide |\n\n## `_targets.R` pipeline targets\n\n| Target | Function | Description |\n|--------|----------|-------------|\n| `data_file_info` | `build_data_file_info()` | Tibble of file paths, sheet names, header row positions |\n| `raw_xlsx_files` | `download_ice_spreadsheets()` | Downloads missing files (cue = \"never\") |\n| `clean_names_list` | `build_clean_names()` | Named list of variable name vectors, one per year |\n| `facilities_raw` | `import_all_years()` | Named list of raw tibbles, one per year |\n| `facilities_clean` | `clean_all_years()` | Name/city standardization, type coercion, Guantanamo patch |\n| `facilities_aggregated` | `aggregate_all_years()` | Row-level ADP sums, `share_non_crim`, `share_no_threat`, `facility_type_wiki` |\n| `facility_crosswalk` | `build_facility_crosswalk()` | Exact + fuzzy address matching → canonical IDs |\n| `facilities_keyed` | `attach_canonical_ids()` | Panel data with `canonical_id` / `canonical_name` joined |\n| `facility_presence` | `build_facility_presence()` | Per-facility year presence + trajectory label |\n| `facilities_panel` | `build_panel()` | Long format panel dataset |\n| `saved_files` | `save_outputs()` | Writes RDS + CSV to `data/`; format = \"file\" |\n\n## R/ function files\n\n| File | Key functions |\n|------|---------------|\n| `metadata.R` | `build_data_file_info()` |\n| `download.R` | `download_ice_spreadsheets()` |\n| `import.R` | `header_rows()`, `clean_variable_names_from_header()`, `build_clean_names()`, `read_facilities_data()`, `import_all_years()` |\n| `clean.R` | `clean_facility_names()`, `fix_city_names()`, `clean_facilities_data()`, `clean_all_years()` |\n| `aggregate.R` | `aggregate_facilities_data()`, `aggregate_all_years()` |\n| `crosswalk.R` | `build_facility_crosswalk()`, `attach_canonical_ids()`, `build_facility_presence()`, `build_panel()`, `save_outputs()` |\n| `add-wikipedia-matches.R` | `add_wikipedia_matches(df, name_col, lang=\"en\")` — uses WikipediR / MediaWiki API, rate-limited |\n| `gh-copy-assets.R` | JS copy-to-clipboard helper for HTML output |\n\n## Key variables\n\nAll column names are snake_case derived from the 2-row Excel headers.\n\n| Variable | Description |\n|----------|-------------|\n| `facility_name`, `facility_city`, `facility_state`, `facility_zip`, `facility_address` | Facility identity |\n| `facility_type_detailed` | Raw ICE type code (IGSA, DIGSA, SPC, BOP, CDF, etc.) |\n| `facility_type_wiki` | Derived readable type for Wikipedia use |\n| `facility_average_length_of_stay_alos` | Average length of stay |\n| `adp_detainee_classification_level_{a-d}` | ADP by security level |\n| `adp_criminality_male_crim/non_crim`, `adp_criminality_female_crim/non_crim` | ADP by criminality × gender |\n| `adp_ice_threat_level_{1-3}`, `adp_no_ice_threat_level` | ADP by ICE threat level |\n| `adp_mandatory` | Mandatory detention ADP |\n| `inspections_guaranteed_minimum` | Contracted bed capacity (integer) |\n| `inspections_last_inspection_type`, `..._end_date`, `..._standard`, `..._final_rating` | Inspection info |\n| `share_non_crim`, `share_no_threat` | Derived: non-criminal / no-threat shares |\n| `canonical_id`, `canonical_name` | Post-crosswalk facility identifiers |\n\n## Facility type → wiki classification\n\n| `facility_type_detailed` contains | `facility_type_wiki` |\n|-----------------------------------|----------------------|\n| IGSA | Jail |\n| USMS IGA | Jail |\n| DIGSA | Dedicated Migrant Detention Center |\n| STATE | State Migrant Detention Center |\n| BOP | Federal Prison |\n| Family | Private Family Detention Center |\n| CDF / USMS CDF | Private Migrant Detention Center |\n| SPC | ICE Migrant Detention Center |\n| STAGING | ICE Short-Term Migrant Detention Center |\n| DOD | Military Detention Center |\n\n## Crosswalk logic\n\n**Pass 1:** Exact match on `(facility_name, facility_city, facility_state)` → provisional `facility_id`.\n\n**Pass 2:** Within the same ZIP code, compute OSA string similarity on `facility_address`. Pairs with similarity ≥ 0.80 are merged via union-find, except for pairs in `do_not_merge_pairs()`.\n\n**Canonical name:** Most recent year's name for each cluster (ties broken by `year_order`).\n\n**Manual overrides:** `canonical_name_overrides_table()` in `crosswalk.R` — 7 entries correcting ambiguous state suffixes, punctuation, etc.\n\n**Trajectory labels** (in `facility_presence`):\n- `continuous` — present every year FY19–FY26\n- `persistent_gaps` — present in FY19 and FY26 but with gaps\n- `closed` — present in FY19, absent from FY26\n- `new` — absent from FY19, present in FY26\n- `transient` — absent from both FY19 and FY26\n\n## What is NOT yet ported from the original Qmd\n\nThe following sections of `import-ice-detention.qmd` (wiki-graph) have not yet been added as targets:\n\n1. **Wikipedia harmonization** — fuzzy matching of ICE facilities to the Wikipedia \"List of immigrant detention sites\" article; adds `wiki_slug` and `management` columns. Uses `add_wikipedia_matches()` already in `R/`.\n2. **Wikitext table generation** — `get_wikitable()` converts the panel to MediaWiki markup.\n3. **Visualizations** — treemaps (by facility type; by state × type) and three interactive Leaflet maps.\n\n## R package installation guidance\n\nThis project uses **renv** for reproducibility. Agents should follow these rules when packages are relevant:\n\n1. **Always suggest the best R package for a job**, even if it isn't currently installed. Do not avoid recommending a package just because it may require installation.\n2. **Before suggesting installation**, check whether the package is already available in the current renv library (`find.package(\"pkg\", quiet = TRUE)`) and also in the system R library, which on this machine is `/Library/Frameworks/R.framework/Versions/4.5-arm64/Resources/library` — this directory is **not** used by this project due to renv, but packages found there may already be compiled and installable quickly via `renv::install()`. Check with: `find.package(\"pkg\", lib.loc = \"/Library/Frameworks/R.framework/Versions/4.5-arm64/Resources/library\", quiet = TRUE)`.\n3. **If the package exists outside renv** (system or user library), tell the user — they may be able to use `renv::install()` quickly without a full CRAN download, or they may want to note it's already compiled.\n4. **Always tell the user to install via renv**, not `install.packages()`, since this project uses renv. The standard command is:\n   ```r\n   renv::install(\"pkg\")\n   ```\n   After installing, the user should run `renv::snapshot()` to record the dependency.\n5. For packages with system-level dependencies (e.g., `pdftools` requires `poppler`, `sf` requires `GDAL/PROJ`), note the system library requirement alongside the install command.\n\n## Dependencies\n\nCore: `readxl`, `dplyr`, `stringr`, `tidyr`, `purrr`, `tibble`, `httr`, `stringdist`, `glue`, `targets`\n\nFor Wikipedia targets (not yet added): `WikipediR`, `rvest`\n\nFor visualization targets (not yet added): `treemapify`, `ggplot2`, `mapview`, `leaflet`\n"},"elapsedMs":86}</script></tool-result></div>

<div><tool-call id="toolu_012v9bzNy4CtdGmtr7L81Yeh"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"edit","input":{"file_path":"/Users/bjorkjcr/Dropbox (Personal)/R/ice-detention/R/clean.R","old_string":"      facility_name    = clean_facility_names(facility_name),\n      facility_city    = str...[truncated, total 372 chars]","new_string":"      facility_name    = clean_facility_names(facility_name),\n      facility_city    = str...[truncated, total 839 chars]"},"caption":"R/clean.R","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"@@ -238,7 +238,15 @@\n       facility_name    = clean_facility_names(facility_name),\n       facility_city    = stringr::str_to_title(facility_city),\n       facility_address = stringr::str_to_title(facility_address),\n+      facility_county  = stringr::str_to_title(facility_county),\n       facility_city    = fix_city_names(facility_city),\n+      # Zero-pad ZIP codes to 5 digits. readxl parses numeric cells as\n+      # doubles, silently dropping leading zeros (e.g. 07001 → 7001).\n+      # The PDF parser produces character strings without this issue, but\n+      # as.integer() + str_pad handles both sources uniformly.\n+      facility_zip = stringr::str_pad(\n+        as.character(as.integer(facility_zip)), 5, side = \"left\", pad = \"0\"\n+      ),\n       # Normalize whitespace in all remaining character columns\n       dplyr::across(where(is.character), stringr::str_squish)\n     ) |>\n","language":"diff"}}</script></tool-call></div>

Supplemental to the main FY19–FY26 annual stats panel.\",\n  \"sources\": [\n    {\n      \"title\": \"2015 ICE Detention Facility Listing\",\n      \"path\": \"https://www.ice.gov/doclib/foia/dfs/2015IceDetentionFacilityListing.xlsx\",\n      \"author\": \"ICE Enforcement and Removal Operations, Custody Management Division\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"title\": \"ICE DMCP Facility List — ERO Custody Management Division, FY2017\",\n      \"path\": \"https://www.prisonlegalnews.org/media/publications/ICE_DMCP_Facility_List_ERO_Custody_Management_Division_2017.pdf\",\n      \"author\": \"ICE Enforcement and Removal Operations, Custody Management Division\",\n      \"note\": \"Published by Prison Legal News; underlying document is U.S. government public domain.\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"licenses\": [\n    {\n      \"name\": \"us-pd\",\n      \"title\": \"U.S. Government Works (Public Domain)\",\n      \"path\": \"https://www.usa.gov/government-copyright\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"resources\": [\n    {\n      \"name\": \"fy15_dmcp\",\n      \"path\": \"fy15_dmcp.csv\",\n      \"mediatype\": \"text/csv\",\n      \"description\": \"2015 DMCP authorized facility listing. 209 rows × 38 columns. Data as of 2015-12-08 (IIDS). Generated by the fy15_dmcp target in _targets.R.\",\n      \"schema\": {\n        \"primaryKey\": \"detloc\",\n        \"fields\": [\n          {\n            \"name\": \"detloc\",\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"ICE internal facility identifier code (DETLOC). All-caps alphanumeric, 3–8 characters.\"\n          },\n          {\n            \"name\": \"facility_name\",\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Facility name. Title case; common abbreviations expanded (e.g. 'Ctr.' → 'Center').\"\n          },\n          {\n            \"name\": \"facility_address\",\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Street address. Title case.\"\n          },\n          {\n            \"name\": \"facility_city\",\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"City. Title case.\"\n          },\n          {\n            \"name\": \"facility_county\",\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"County name. Title case.\"\n          },\n          {\n            \"name\": \"facility_state\",\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Two-letter U.S. state abbreviation.\"\n          },\n          {\n            \"name\": \"facility_zip\",\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"5-digit ZIP code, zero-padded. Coerced from numeric via str_pad to preserve leading zeros (e.g. New England ZIP codes).\",\n            \"constraints\": { \"pattern\": \"^[0-9]{5}$\" }\n          },\n          {\n            \"name\": \"facility_type\",\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Broad ICE facility type category. Values: IGSA, CDF, SPC, USMS IGA, BOP, FAMILY, STATE, STAGING, DOD.\"\n          },\n          {\n            \"name\": \"facility_type_detailed\",\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"description\": \"Detailed ICE facility type. Now append the metadata convention section to AGENTS.md:

<div><tool-call id="toolu_01QkTY9FSmeQzM1mZg7NfWPr"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"edit","input":{"file_path":"/Users/bjorkjcr/Dropbox (Personal)/R/ice-detention/AGENTS.md","old_string":"## Dependencies\n\nCore: `readxl`, `dplyr`, `stringr`, `tidyr`, `purrr`, `tibble`, `httr`, `...[truncated, total 271 chars]","new_string":"## Dependencies\n\nCore: `readxl`, `dplyr`, `stringr`, `tidyr`, `purrr`, `tibble`, `httr`, `...[truncated, total 3124 chars]"},"caption":"AGENTS.md","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"@@ -151,6 +151,42 @@\n \n Core: `readxl`, `dplyr`, `stringr`, `tidyr`, `purrr`, `tibble`, `httr`, `stringdist`, `glue`, `targets`\n \n+For DMCP listings: `pdftools`\n+\n For Wikipedia targets (not yet added): `WikipediR`, `rvest`\n \n For visualization targets (not yet added): `treemapify`, `ggplot2`, `mapview`, `leaflet`\n+\n+## Metadata convention for imported data tables\n+\n+### Sources that contribute to the main annual stats pipeline\n+\n+For data sources that contribute to the main annual stats pipeline (e.g. authorization lists that include summary data from multiple prior fiscal years), the project uses a three-part convention:\n+\n+1. **Pipeline targets**: No standalone import script. Each source has dedicated functions across three files, plus targets in `_targets.R`:\n+   - `R/download.R` — `download_{source}()`: downloads the file if absent, returns the local path. Used as a `format = \"file\"`, `cue = \"never\"` target.\n+   - `R/import.R` — `import_{source}(path)`: accepts the file path and returns a raw tibble with minimal transformation.\n+   - `R/clean.R` — `rename_{source}_columns()` and `clean_{source}_data()`: harmonize column names to project schema and apply type coercion and string cleaning.\n+   - `_targets.R` targets follow the naming convention `{source}_file` (download), `{source}_raw` (import), `{source}` (cleaned), grouped in a clearly commented supplemental section.\n+\n+2. **YAML sidecar** (`data/<source-name>.yml`): Structured provenance metadata including bibliographic citation, data currency dates, raw file description, parsing method, filtering decisions, and a list of pipeline targets and outputs.\n+\n+3. **Frictionless Data package descriptor** (`data/datapackage.json`): Column-level schemas for CSV outputs, following the [Frictionless Data](https://frictionlessdata.io/) `datapackage.json` spec. Includes source citation and license info. All field definitions are written out fully in each resource — do not use `$ref` cross-references, which are not part of the Frictionless spec.\n+\n+#### Current instances\n+\n+| Source | Targets | YAML | datapackage resources |\n+|--------|---------|------|-----------------------|\n+| ICE DMCP Facility Listings (2015 XLSX, 2017 PDF) | `fy15_dmcp`, `fy17_dmcp` | `data/dmcp-listings.yml` | `fy15_dmcp`, `fy17_dmcp` |\n+\n+---\n+\n+### Future supplemental data sources\n+\n+For future data sources that are supplemental to the main annual stats pipeline (e.g. manually transcribed tables, external reference files), the project uses the same three-part convention, but with a different structure for item 1:\n+\n+1. **Supplemental import script** (`<source-name>-<year>.R`): A single self-contained R script in the project root containing all import and processing functions for that source. Any file downloading must be done once manually and should not be wired to run automatically. The script is imported into the pipeline by adding a dedicated supplemental section to `_targets.R`.\n+\n+2. **YAML sidecar** (`data/<source-name>.yml`): Same as above.\n+\n+3. **Frictionless Data package descriptor** (`data/datapackage.json`): Same as above. New resources are appended to the existing `datapackage.json`.\n","language":"diff"}}</script></tool-call></div>

All done. A few notes on what was done:

**`clean_dmcp_data()` fixes:** `facility_county` gets `str_to_title()` alongside city and address. For `facility_zip`, the fix routes both sources through `as.integer()` → `as.character()` → `str_pad(..., 5, "left", "0")`. The XLSX source needed this to recover leading zeros; the PDF source produces clean character strings already, but the same path handles it correctly. New England ZIP codes like `07601`, `02747`, `04102` are now preserved.

**`datapackage.json`:** All 40 fields in `fy17_dmcp` are now fully self-contained. One difference worth noting: the PDF source produces character strings for date columns (`contract_initiation_date`, `inspections_last_inspection_date`) rather than the `POSIXct` objects that readxl produces from the XLSX — so those fields are typed `"string"` in the `fy17_dmcp` resource rather than `"date"`. That's an accurate reflection of the actual data, and something to address if the two sources are ever merged.

**AGENTS.md:** Structured into two subsections — "sources that contribute to the main pipeline" (targets-based, current DMCP case) and "future supplemental sources" (single script + targets entry, no auto-download).

Lets call it facilities_data_dec2015. FY2012 data: IIDS as of 10/8/12; EID data through 10/5/12.  FY2011 data: IIDS as of 10/7/11; EID data through 10/5/11.  FY2004-10 data: IIDS as of 10/05/10; EID data through 09/30/10. 	 	 	 	 	 	 	 	 	 	 	 	 	 	 	 	 	 	 	 	 	 	 	 	 	 	 	 	 	 	 	 	 	 	 	 	 	"

Facility names title-cased and\n        abbreviations expanded. ADP columns as numeric. 209 rows × 38 columns.\n      columns_added_vs_raw:\n        - \"None — renaming and type coercion only.\"\n      columns_not_in_fy_panel: >\n        detloc, facility_county, facility_type, facility_levels,\n        facility_capacity, facility_population_count, fy10_adp–fy16_adp,\n        facility_operator, facility_owner, contract_initiation_date,\n        contract_expiration_date, per_diem_rate_detailed,\n        authorizing_authority, over_under_72, cy10_rating–cy15_rating,\n        dsm_assigned\n\nfy17_listing:\n  title: \"ICE DMCP Facility List — ERO Custody Management Division, FY2017\"\n  url: \"https://www.prisonlegalnews.org/media/publications/ICE_DMCP_Facility_List_ERO_Custody_Management_Division_2017.pdf\"\n  obtained_via: \"Prison Legal News document library\"\n  original_source: \"ICE / ERO Custody Management Division\"\n  format: \"PDF (4 pages, fixed-width table)\""},"elapsedMs":482}</script></tool-result></div>

<div><tool-call id="toolu_01BtaoAwhrtKasiJ9jbjvkR2"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"runCode","input":{"code":"targets::tar_load(c(facilities_data_dec2015_raw, facilities_data_dec2015, fy17_dmcp_raw, f...[truncated, total 624 chars]","intent":"Find rows where facility_capacity coercion introduced NAs"},"intent":"Find rows where facility_capacity coercion introduced NAs","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"targets::tar_load(c(facilities_data_dec2015_raw, facilities_data_dec2015, fy17_dmcp_raw, fy17_dmcp))\n\n# ── facility_capacity coercion ────────────────────────────────────────────────\n# Find rows where the raw value is non-NA but clean result is NA\ncap_issues_dec2015 <- facilities_data_dec2015_raw |>\n  dplyr::select(DETLOC, Name, Capacity) |>\n  dplyr::filter(!is.na(Capacity)) |>\n  dplyr::mutate(\n    coerced = suppressWarnings(as.integer(stringr::str_remove_all(as.character(Capacity), \",\")))\n  ) |>\n  dplyr::filter(is.na(coerced))\n\ncat(\"=== facility_capacity coercion failures (dec2015) ===\\n\")\nprint(cap_issues_dec2015)\n","language":"r"}}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-call id="toolu_01Ke1LHNrUC1Kd6Xj95MpMf1"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"runCode","input":{"code":"# Get the full picture of non-numeric capacity values\ncat(\"All distinct non-integer Capaci...[truncated, total 938 chars]","intent":"Identify all non-numeric values causing coercion NAs in both capacity and ADP columns"},"intent":"Identify all non-numeric values causing coercion NAs in both capacity and ADP columns","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"# Get the full picture of non-numeric capacity values\ncat(\"All distinct non-integer Capacity values in dec2015:\\n\")\nfacilities_data_dec2015_raw |>\n  dplyr::mutate(\n    coerced = suppressWarnings(as.integer(stringr::str_remove_all(as.character(Capacity), \",\")))\n  ) |>\n  dplyr::filter(!is.na(Capacity), is.na(coerced)) |>\n  dplyr::count(Capacity, sort = TRUE) |>\n  print()\n\ncat(\"\\n=== ADP coercion failures (dec2015) ===\\n\")\nadp_cols_raw <- c(\"FY16 ADP\", \"FY15 ADP\", \"FY14 ADP\", \"FY13 ADP\",\n                  \"FY12 ADP\", \"FY11 ADP\", \"FY10 ADP\")\npurrr::map_dfr(adp_cols_raw, \\(col) {\n  facilities_data_dec2015_raw |>\n    dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data[[col]])) |>\n    dplyr::mutate(\n      coerced = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(stringr::str_remove_all(as.character(.data[[col]]), \",\")))\n    ) |>\n    dplyr::filter(is.na(coerced)) |>\n    dplyr::select(DETLOC, Name, col_name = dplyr::all_of(col)) |>\n    dplyr::mutate(column = col)\n}) |> print()\n","language":"r"}}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01Ke1LHNrUC1Kd6Xj95MpMf1"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"content","value":[{"type":"text","text":"Output:\nAll distinct non-integer Capacity values in dec2015:\n# A tibble: 1 × 2\n  Capacity ...[truncated, total 288 chars]"}]},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"All distinct non-integer Capacity values in dec2015:\n# A tibble: 1 × 2\n  Capacity      n\n  <chr>     <int>\n1 AS NEEDED    49\n\n=== ADP coercion failures (dec2015) ===\n# A tibble: 0 × 4\n# ℹ 4 variables: DETLOC <chr>, Name <chr>, col_name <dbl>, column <chr>"},"elapsedMs":1186}</script></tool-result></div>



<div><tool-call id="toolu_013xm1Nk7KDrA3ZiFpiA82SP"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"runCode","input":{"code":"# Check fy17_dmcp for capacity and ADP coercion issues\ncat(\"=== facility_capacity coercion...[truncated, total 907 chars]","intent":"Check fy17_dmcp_raw for coercion failures in capacity and ADP columns"},"intent":"Check fy17_dmcp_raw for coercion failures in capacity and ADP columns","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"# Check fy17_dmcp for capacity and ADP coercion issues\ncat(\"=== facility_capacity coercion failures (fy17) ===\\n\")\nfy17_dmcp_raw |>\n  dplyr::filter(!is.na(capacity)) |>\n  dplyr::mutate(\n    coerced = suppressWarnings(as.integer(stringr::str_remove_all(capacity, \",\")))\n  ) |>\n  dplyr::filter(is.na(coerced)) |>\n  dplyr::count(capacity, sort = TRUE) |>\n  print()\n\ncat(\"\\n=== ADP coercion failures (fy17) ===\\n\")\nadp_cols_17 <- c(\"fy17_adp\", \"fy16_adp\", \"fy15_adp\", \"fy14_adp\",\n                 \"fy13_adp\", \"fy12_adp\", \"fy11_adp\", \"fy10_adp\")\npurrr::map_dfr(adp_cols_17, \\(col) {\n  fy17_dmcp_raw |>\n    dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data[[col]])) |>\n    dplyr::mutate(\n      coerced = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(stringr::str_remove_all(.data[[col]], \",\")))\n    ) |>\n    dplyr::filter(is.na(coerced)) |>\n    dplyr::select(detloc, name, raw_value = dplyr::all_of(col)) |>\n    dplyr::mutate(column = col)\n}) |> print()\n","language":"r"}}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-call id="toolu_01WV8DLXLnTBuLXn8nBDfqtq"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"runCode","input":{"code":"# Find HASKLTX on page 2 and look at its token x-positions in the capacity/ADP range\npurrr...[truncated, total 264 chars]","intent":"Find the y-position of Rolling Plains / HASKLTX in the PDF"},"intent":"Find the y-position of Rolling Plains / HASKLTX in the PDF","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"# Find HASKLTX on page 2 and look at its token x-positions in the capacity/ADP range\npurrr::imap_dfr(pdf_dat, ~ dplyr::mutate(.x, page = .y)) |>\n  dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(text, \"HASKLTX|ROLLING PLAINS\")) |>\n  dplyr::select(page, y, x, text) |>\n  head(5)\n","language":"r"}}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-call id="toolu_014eFJb5DaKgykzzgxTXbCP3"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"runCode","input":{"code":"# What are the two tokens in fy17_adp for the affected rows?\n# Split the value on space to...[truncated, total 535 chars]","intent":"Split the two-token fy17_adp values to understand what each token represents"},"intent":"Split the two-token fy17_adp values to understand what each token represents","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"# What are the two tokens in fy17_adp for the affected rows?\n# Split the value on space to see what both numbers are\naffected_17 <- fy17_dmcp_raw |>\n  dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(fy17_adp, \" \")) |>\n  dplyr::mutate(\n    first_token  = stringr::str_extract(fy17_adp, \"^[^ ]+\"),\n    second_token = stringr::str_extract(fy17_adp, \"[^ ]+$\")\n  ) |>\n  dplyr::select(detloc, name, population_count, fy17_adp, first_token, second_token, fy16_adp)\n\ncat(\"Rows with two tokens in fy17_adp:\", nrow(affected_17), \"\\n\\n\")\nhead(affected_17, 10)\n","language":"r"}}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-call id="toolu_01V6pJ7fwJiPVNKTbawNmux5"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"runCode","input":{"code":"# Only 10 rows have the space issue in fy17_adp — but 172 ADP failures were reported.\n# Ch...[truncated, total 612 chars]","intent":"Find all non-numeric values across every ADP column in fy17_dmcp_raw"},"intent":"Find all non-numeric values across every ADP column in fy17_dmcp_raw","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"# Only 10 rows have the space issue in fy17_adp — but 172 ADP failures were reported.\n# Check which columns actually have the problem across all ADP columns.\nadp_cols_17 <- c(\"fy17_adp\", \"fy16_adp\", \"fy15_adp\", \"fy14_adp\",\n                 \"fy13_adp\", \"fy12_adp\", \"fy11_adp\", \"fy10_adp\")\n\npurrr::map_dfr(adp_cols_17, \\(col) {\n  fy17_dmcp_raw |>\n    dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data[[col]]),\n                  is.na(suppressWarnings(as.numeric(stringr::str_remove_all(.data[[col]], \",\"))))) |>\n    dplyr::count(raw_value = .data[[col]]) |>\n    dplyr::mutate(column = col)\n}) |>\n  dplyr::arrange(column, dplyr::desc(n))\n","language":"r"}}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-call id="toolu_015rNbQ5n9rEQqQxr5g72E2x"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"runCode","input":{"code":"# Get a sample of affected DETLOC codes for fy10 and fy11\nbad_fy10 <- fy17_dmcp_raw |>\n  d...[truncated, total 392 chars]","intent":"Find page/y positions for a few facilities with bad fy10_adp values"},"intent":"Find page/y positions for a few facilities with bad fy10_adp values","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"# Get a sample of affected DETLOC codes for fy10 and fy11\nbad_fy10 <- fy17_dmcp_raw |>\n  dplyr::filter(stringr::str_detect(fy10_adp, \" \")) |>\n  dplyr::pull(detloc) |>\n  head(4)\n\n# Find their y-positions and look at x-positions across the full ADP range\npurrr::imap_dfr(pdf_dat, ~ dplyr::mutate(.x, page = .y)) |>\n  dplyr::filter(text %in% bad_fy10) |>\n  dplyr::select(detloc = text, page, y)\n","language":"r"}}</script></tool-call></div>

- x=394: 1,560 (fy13_adp, bin [385,397))
- x=406: 1,677 (fy12_adp, bin [397,409))
- x=418: 1,655 (fy11_adp, bin [409,422))
- x=430: 1,614 (fy10_adp, bin [422,437))

**Adelanto (y=69)** shows a similar pattern with consistently high values across the historical funding years. I'm verifying the bin ranges for Adelanto's columns—the small values like 587, 19, and 0 all fall within their expected ranges, so that row checks out. The real issue seems to be with rows containing very small or zero values where right-aligned text extends further right than anticipated. Now I'm moving on to check PINEPLA's data. I'm mapping out the column positions in the data by testing specific coordinates against the break points to verify which field each position corresponds to—checking that the x-coordinates fall within the correct intervals for population_count, fy17_adp, fy16_adp, and the subsequent fiscal year fields. Looking at the pattern for PINEPLA, it seems the facility opened later with zero ADP values across those earlier fiscal years, which explains the missing fy15_adp token. Now I'm checking another record, IRADFCA, to see if it has a similar gap or if the data structure is consistent across different facilities. I'm diving into the fy10_adp issue now—those "0 0" values suggest two tokens within the same bin, which shouldn't happen. Looking at the Adelanto row more carefully, x=435 falls inside the fy10_adp range [422, 437) while x=438 crosses into the next column, so the boundary placement seems correct. I think the issue might be that a smaller "0" from fy11_adp is bleeding into the [422, 437) range where fy10_adp should be, causing both values to appear in the same column. Looking at Adelanto's fy11_adp value of "19" at x=421, it's correctly positioned in its range, but smaller numbers might shift differently and overlap into the adjacent column.

The real problem is that right-aligned numbers have their left edges at different positions depending on their width, so single digits can end up crossing column boundaries. Instead of patching individual boundaries, I should use the actual header token positions to define where each column truly starts and ends.

Looking at the header row positions, I can see each column header is precisely placed—population_count at x=320, then the ADP columns stepping through x=341, 353, 365, 377, 389, 401, 413, 426, with Facility Operator starting at x=438. These header positions give me the exact column boundaries I need to properly classify the data values. The right edges align with the next column's start positions, confirming each column is about 12 pixels wide. For the rightmost fy10_adp column at x=426, the right edge extends to around 437 where the operator column begins, making it slightly narrower at about 11 pixels—a single character like "0" would fit near that edge.

Wait, I think I've been approaching this wrong. The real issue might be that each facility actually has two separate y-rows in the data: one for the main fields and another offset row for DETLOC information. When I'm grouping records by y-gap, both rows are getting merged into the same record, which could explain why I'm seeing unexpected values like "0 0" appearing in columns where they shouldn't be.

Let me trace through what's actually happening with the DETLOC row. DETLOC should only appear at x=19, so it should only populate the detloc column. Now I can see the root cause clearly. In the PDF, numbers are right-aligned, and small values (especially `0`) consistently appear 3–4 pixels further right than large values in the same column. The current column breaks are midpoints between headers, which sit right in the gap — splitting small values into the wrong column. The correct fix is to use the actual **header x-positions** as left boundaries, which is the principled approach. Let me verify this repairs all affected cases before changing the constant.

<div><tool-call id="toolu_01NuGNLj7ijTQga5cDmmRv7V"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"runCode","input":{"code":"# Test the corrected col_breaks using header positions as left columns","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"# Test the corrected col_breaks using header positions as left boundaries\nnew_breaks <- c(\n  0, 34, 101, 151, 179, 206, 218, 228, 242, 260, 282,\n  302,  # capacity\n  320,  # population_count\n  341,  # fy17_adp\n  353,  # fy16_adp\n  365,  # fy15_adp\n  377,  # fy14_adp\n  389,  # fy13_adp\n  401,  # fy12_adp\n  413,  # fy11_adp\n  426,  # fy10_adp\n  438,  # facility_operator\n  469, 483, 510, 536, 622, 642, 669, 687, 706, 734,\n  754, 782, 811, 840, 869, 898, 920, 944, Inf\n)\n\ncol_names_vec <- c(\n  \"detloc\", \"name\", \"address\", \"city\", \"county\", \"state\", \"zip\",\n  \"type\", \"type_detailed\", \"male_female\", \"levels\",\n  \"capacity\", \"population_count\",\n  \"fy17_adp\", \"fy16_adp\", \"fy15_adp\", \"fy14_adp\", \"fy13_adp\",\n  \"fy12_adp\", \"fy11_adp\", \"fy10_adp\",\n  \"facility_operator\", \"facility_owner\",\n  \"contract_initiation_date\", \"contract_expiration_date\",\n  \"per_diem_rate_detailed\", \"authorizing_authority\",\n  \"over_under_72\", \"last_inspection_type\", \"last_inspection_standard\",\n  \"last_inspection_rating_final\", \"last_inspection_date\",\n  \"cy16_rating\", \"cy15_rating\", \"cy14_rating\", \"cy13_rating\",\n  \"cy12_rating\", \"cy11_rating\", \"cy10_rating\", \"dsm_assigned\"\n)\n\nassign_col_new <- function(x_val) col_names_vec[findInterval(x_val, new_breaks)]\n\n# Re-parse page 1 with corrected breaks and check bad rows\nparse_page_test <- function(page_tokens, data_y_min) {\n  toks <- page_tokens |>\n    dplyr::filter(y >= data_y_min) |>\n    dplyr::arrange(y, x) |>\n    dplyr::mutate(col = assign_col_new(x))\n  if (nrow(toks) == 0) return(tibble::tibble())\n  toks <- toks |> dplyr::mutate(record_id = cumsum(c(TRUE, diff(y) > 1)))\n  toks |>\n    dplyr::group_by(record_id, col) |>\n    dplyr::summarise(value = paste(text, collapse = \" \"), .groups = \"drop\") |>\n    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = col, values_from = value, values_fill = NA) |>\n    dplyr::select(-record_id)\n}\n\ntest_raw <- purrr::imap(pdf_dat, ~ parse_page_test(.x, if (.y == 1L) 66L else 1L)) |>\n  dplyr::bind_rows() |>\n  dplyr::filter(grepl(\"^[A-Z0-9]+$\", detloc), !is.na(state))\n\n# Count coercion failures with new breaks\nadp_cols <- c(\"fy17_adp\",\"fy16_adp\",\"fy15_adp\",\"fy14_adp\",\n              \"fy13_adp\",\"fy12_adp\",\"fy11_adp\",\"fy10_adp\")\n\npurrr::map_dfr(adp_cols, \\(col) {\n  n <- test_raw |>\n    dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data[[col]]),\n                  is.na(suppressWarnings(as.numeric(stringr::str_remove_all(.data[[col]], \",\"))))) |>\n    nrow()\n  tibble::tibble(column = col, bad_rows = n)\n})\n","language":"r"}}</script></tool-call></div>

- x=438: AGS, AHTNA, ASSET, CCA, CEC, CITY, COUNTY, GEO, GPS-ASSET, etc. — these are also facility_operator tokens

So the facility_operator column starts at both x=437 AND x=438. 

Most operators cluster at x=438, but a small subset of 17 operators appear at x=437 instead. The fy10_adp values are primarily at x=435 with some scattered at x=434, and there's no overlap with x=437. I'm also seeing footnote text bleeding into the data rows at x=435 and x=436. The solution is to place the column boundary at x=437 to properly separate the fy10_adp and facility_operator columns.

Setting the break at x=437 would correctly assign the x=435 numeric values to fy10_adp and push the x=437 operator codes into facility_operator, though I need to account for the footnote text at x=436 that would fall within the fy10_adp range—but those rows should already be filtered out by the existing DETLOC and state validation. The interval logic confirms this is the right approach—with the break at 437, the facility_operator values at x=437 and x=438 will land in the correct bin, while the fy10_adp numbers at x=435 stay where they belong. <div><tool-call id="toolu_01MQ7wemiDGPMqtTX8KWazAt"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"runCode","input":{"code":"final_breaks <- c(\n  0, 34, 101, 151, 179, 206, 218, 228, 242, 260, 282,\n  302,   # capaci...[truncated, total 1524 chars]","intent":"Verify the final corrected breaks eliminate all ADP coercion failures"},"intent":"Verify the final corrected breaks eliminate all ADP coercion failures","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"final_breaks <- c(\n  0, 34, 101, 151, 179, 206, 218, 228, 242, 260, 282,\n  302,   # capacity\n  320,   # population_count\n  341,   # fy17_adp\n  353,   # fy16_adp\n  365,   # fy15_adp\n  377,   # fy14_adp\n  389,   # fy13_adp\n  401,   # fy12_adp\n  413,   # fy11_adp\n  426,   # fy10_adp\n  437,   # facility_operator (some operator tokens land at x=437, not x=438)\n  469, 483, 510, 536, 622, 642, 669, 687, 706, 734,\n  754, 782, 811, 840, 869, 898, 920, 944, Inf\n)\n\nassign_final <- function(x_val) col_names_vec[findInterval(x_val, final_breaks)]\n\nparse_page_final <- function(page_tokens, data_y_min) {\n  toks <- page_tokens |>\n    dplyr::filter(y >= data_y_min) |>\n    dplyr::arrange(y, x) |>\n    dplyr::mutate(col = assign_final(x))\n  if (nrow(toks) == 0) return(tibble::tibble())\n  toks <- toks |> dplyr::mutate(record_id = cumsum(c(TRUE, diff(y) > 1)))\n  toks |>\n    dplyr::group_by(record_id, col) |>\n    dplyr::summarise(value = paste(text, collapse = \" \"), .groups = \"drop\") |>\n    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = col, values_from = value, values_fill = NA) |>\n    dplyr::select(-record_id)\n}\n\nfinal_raw <- purrr::imap(pdf_dat, ~ parse_page_final(.x, if (.y == 1L) 66L else 1L)) |>\n  dplyr::bind_rows() |>\n  dplyr::filter(grepl(\"^[A-Z0-9]+$\", detloc), !is.na(state))\n\npurrr::map_dfr(adp_cols, \\(col) {\n  n <- final_raw |>\n    dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data[[col]]),\n                  is.na(suppressWarnings(as.numeric(stringr::str_remove_all(.data[[col]], \",\"))))) |>\n    nrow()\n  tibble::tibble(column = col, bad_rows = n)\n})\n","language":"r"}}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-call id="toolu_01Tds8r9hyXxaxm6qrYhMmtk"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"edit","input":{"file_path":"/Users/bjorkjcr/Dropbox (Personal)/R/ice-detention/R/import.R","old_string":".dmcp_pdf_col_breaks <- c(\n  0, 34, 101, 151, 179, 206, 218, 228, 242, 260, 282, 298, 317,...[truncated, total 237 chars]","new_string":"# Column left-edge x-positions derived from header token bounding boxes (y=63,\n# page 1). ...[truncated, total 841 chars]"},"caption":"R/import.R","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"@@ -141,11 +141,27 @@\n # Raw names use snake_case; rename_dmcp_columns() maps them to the project\n # schema.\n \n+# Column left-edge x-positions derived from header token bounding boxes (y=63,\n+# page 1). Using header positions (not midpoints) as boundaries is critical:\n+# right-aligned small values like \"0\" appear 3–4 px further right than large\n+# values in the same column, and midpoint boundaries split them incorrectly.\n+# The facility_operator break is at 437 (not 438) because 17 operator tokens\n+# appear at x=437 rather than x=438.\n .dmcp_pdf_col_breaks <- c(\n-  0, 34, 101, 151, 179, 206, 218, 228, 242, 260, 282, 298, 317,\n-  337, 349, 361, 373, 385, 397, 409, 422, 437, 469, 483, 510, 536,\n-  622, 642, 669, 687, 706, 734, 754, 782, 811, 840, 869, 898, 920, 944,\n-  Inf\n+  0, 34, 101, 151, 179, 206, 218, 228, 242, 260, 282,\n+  302,   # capacity\n+  320,   # population_count\n+  341,   # fy17_adp\n+  353,   # fy16_adp\n+  365,   # fy15_adp\n+  377,   # fy14_adp\n+  389,   # fy13_adp\n+  401,   # fy12_adp\n+  413,   # fy11_adp\n+  426,   # fy10_adp\n+  437,   # facility_operator\n+  469, 483, 510, 536, 622, 642, 669, 687, 706, 734,\n+  754, 782, 811, 840, 869, 898, 920, 944, Inf\n )\n \n .dmcp_pdf_col_names <- c(\n","language":"diff"}}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-call id="toolu_01Merk9LdUhcX5HxdC83LnuD"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"runCode","input":{"code":"targets::tar_load(c(fy17_dmcp, facilities_data_dec2015))\n\n# Confirm the AS NEEDED capacity...[truncated, total 664 chars]","intent":"Verify data quality of formerly-problematic rows in final fy17_dmcp target"},"intent":"Verify data quality of formerly-problematic rows in final fy17_dmcp target","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"targets::tar_load(c(fy17_dmcp, facilities_data_dec2015))\n\n# Confirm the AS NEEDED capacity → NA is the only remaining coercion issue\ncat(\"AS NEEDED facilities (capacity = NA):\\n\")\ncat(\"  fy17_dmcp:           \", sum(is.na(fy17_dmcp$facility_capacity)), \"\\n\")\ncat(\"  facilities_data_dec2015:\", sum(is.na(facilities_data_dec2015$facility_capacity)), \"\\n\\n\")\n\n# Spot-check a few formerly-bad rows in fy17_dmcp\nfy17_dmcp |>\n  dplyr::filter(detloc %in% c(\"HASKLTX\", \"PINEPLA\", \"VPREGVA\", \"HASHENE\")) |>\n  dplyr::select(detloc, facility_name, facility_population_count,\n                fy17_adp, fy15_adp, fy14_adp, fy11_adp, fy10_adp,\n                facility_operator)\n","language":"r"}}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01Merk9LdUhcX5HxdC83LnuD"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"content","value":[{"type":"text","text":"Output:\nAS NEEDED facilities (capacity = NA):\n  fy17_dmcp:            98 \n  facilities_dat...[truncated, total 879 chars]"}]},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"AS NEEDED facilities (capacity = NA):\n  fy17_dmcp:            98 \n  facilities_data_dec2015: 49 \n\n# A tibble: 4 × 9\n  detloc  facility_name               facility_population_…¹ fy17_adp fy15_adp fy14_adp fy11_adp fy10_adp\n  <chr>   <chr>                                        <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>\n1 PINEPLA Pine Prairie Correctional …                    539      717        0        0        0        0\n2 HASKLTX Rolling Plains Detention C…                      0      224      440      472      481      509\n3 VPREGVA Virginia Peninsula Regiona…                     47       64       39        1        0        0\n4 HASHENE Hall County Department of …                     87       51       10       26       82       67\n# ℹ abbreviated name: ¹​facility_population_count\n# ℹ 1 more variable: facility_operator <chr>"},"elapsedMs":1860}</script></tool-result></div>

Clean. All formerly-bad rows now parse correctly — `facility_population_count`, `fy17_adp`, and the historic ADP columns are all numeric with no multi-token bleed. The only remaining NAs-by-coercion are the 147 "AS NEEDED" capacity values, which are correct: these are IGSA jails that provide beds on-demand and have no fixed authorized capacity. That's semantically accurate as `NA`.

**Summary of what was found and fixed:**

The PDF uses right-aligned numbers in tight columns (~12px wide). The original column breaks were estimated midpoints between header positions, which placed them right in the danger zone: single-digit and zero values are right-aligned close to the column's right edge and consistently appear 3–4px further right than large values in the same column. The fix was to use the **actual header x-positions** as left-edge boundaries rather than midpoints — this naturally accounts for the full column width. One additional adjustment: 17 `facility_operator` tokens appear at x=437 rather than x=438, so the operator boundary was set at 437.
